The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages a smallpox vaccine emergency stockpile in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat manages the biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the authorized variola virus repositories as mandated by the World Health Assembly, and ensures annual reporting to WHO Governing Bodies. The Smallpox Secretariat supports WHO Archives in preserving the global history of smallpox and documenting the lessons learned from successful eradication of smallpox. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat's current roles and responsibilities include:
translations.reference: Mnamo 1996, katika Mkutano wa 49 wa Afya Ulimwenguni, Nchi Wanachama ziliamua kuharibu hisa mnamo Juni 1999, na kuruhusu tu sampuli ndogo kuhifadhiwa kwa madhumuni ya utafiti katika maabara mbili zilizowekwa salama - moja nchini Merika na moja katika Urusi. Uhifadhi wa muda wa hifadhi zilizopo za virusi vya variola hutumiwa kwa utafiti zaidi muhimu, ambao unasimamiwa na Kamati ya Ushauri ya Utafiti wa Virusi vya Variola. Kikundi hiki hukutana kila mwaka. Sekretarieti ya Ugonjwa wa Ndui ya WHO iliyoko Makao Makuu ya WHO inaratibu shughuli zote zinazohusiana na kutokomeza ndui na baada ya kutokomeza. Hizi ni pamoja na kusimamia utafiti unaoidhinishwa na virusi vya variola hai kwa ajili ya kutengeneza hatua za kupinga kama vile chanjo na dawa za kuzuia virusi. Tangu enzi ya kutokomeza, chanjo salama na matibabu mahususi yametengenezwa kwa ugonjwa wa ndui na magonjwa yanayohusiana kama vile ndui ya nyani. Sekretarieti pia inawajibika kwa maandalizi ya dharura na inasimamia hifadhi ya dharura ya chanjo ya ndui katika tukio lisilowezekana kwamba ugonjwa huo utaibuka tena. Sekretarieti inasimamia ukaguzi wa usalama wa viumbe na usalama wa viumbe hai wa hifadhi zilizoidhinishwa za virusi vya variola kama ilivyoagizwa na Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni, na kuhakikisha ripoti ya kila mwaka kwa Mashirika Yanayoongoza ya WHO. Sekretarieti ya Ndui inaunga mkono Hifadhi za WHO katika kuhifadhi historia ya kimataifa ya ndui na kuweka kumbukumbu za mafunzo tuliyopata kutokana na kutokomeza kabisa ugonjwa wa ndui. Majukumu ya sasa ya Sekretarieti na wajibu wa Ndui kwa WHO ni pamoja na:
translations.hypothesis: Mnamo 1996, katika Mkutano wa 49 wa Jumuiya ya Afya ya Dunia, mataifa ya wanachama waliamua kwamba hazina hii itoakizwa mnamo Juni 1999, na kuwaacha tu sampuli ndogo kuhifadhiwa kwa madhumuni ya utafiti katika лаборатории mbili maalumu na salama – moja katika Marekani na moja katika Jamhuri ya Urusi. Uhifadhi wa muda wa hazina za soko la virusi la variola hutumika kwa utafiti muhimu zaidi, ambapo shirika la ushauri kuhusu utafiti wa virusi la variola linaangazia. Kundi hili linakutana kila mwaka. Serekritariat la WHO la virusi la variola, lililopo katika ofisi ya WHO, linataratibu shughuli zote zinazohusiana na utengenezaji na baada ya utengenezaji. Hii ni pamoja na usimamizi wa utafiti unaoruhusiwa na virusi la variola la kuishi, kwa ajili ya kuendeleza tiba kama vile chanjo na dawa za virusi. Tangu enzi ya utengenezaji, chanjo salama na tiba maalum zimeundwa kwa virusi la variola na magonjwa yanayohusiana kama vile mpox. Serekritariat pia ni jukumu la tathmini za usalama wa biolojia na usalama, na huendesha hazina ya chanjo ya virusi la variola katika tukio la kutokea tena. Serekritariat pia huendesha ukaguzi wa hazina za virusi la variola zilizoidhinishwa, kama ilivyotegelezwa na Mkutano wa Afya ya Dunia, na kuhakikisha taarifa ya kila mwaka kwa vyombo vya serikali vya WHO. Serekritariat la virusi la variola inasaidia arhivu za WHO katika kuhifadhi historia ya kimataifa ya virusi la variola na kuandika masomo yaliyopatikana kutoka kwa utengenezaji wa virusi la variola. Majukumu na majukumu ya sasa ya Serekritariat la virusi la variola ni pamoja na:

translations.source: Variola virus research and development of countermeasures organizing annual meetings of the WHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) coordinating review of research proposals for work with variola virus through the ACVVR managing relationships with the two variola virus repositories (which are also WHO Collaborating Centres) recording, processing and monitoring requests from researchers for variola virus DNA from the 2 repositories under specific rules conducting biennial biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the 2 global variola virus repositories developing normative guidance regarding research on variola virus DNA and related matters Emergency preparedness and response coordinating and preparing normative guidance on development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopoxviruses developing preparedness and response strategies, and tools to implement preparedness measures
translations.reference: Utafiti wa virusi vya Variola na maendeleo ya hatua za kupinga kuandaa mikutano ya kila mwaka ya Kamati ya Ushauri ya WHO kuhusu Utafiti wa Virusi vya Variola (ACVVR) kuratibu mapitio ya mapendekezo ya utafiti wa kufanya kazi na virusi vya variola kupitia ACVVR kusimamia mahusiano hazina na virusi viwili vya variola (ambazo pia ni Vituo vya Kushirikiana vya WHO) kurekodi, kuchakata na kufuatilia maombi kutoka kwa watafiti wa DNA ya virusi vya variola kutoka kwa hazina 2 chini ya sheria maalum. kufanya ukaguzi wa usalama wa viumbe na ulinzi wa viumbe hifadhi 2 za kimataifa katika hazina za virusi vya variola kuendeleza mwongozo wa kawaida kuhusu utafiti juu ya DNA ya virusi vya variola na masuala yanayohusiana Maandalizi ya dharura na majibu kuratibu na kuandaa mwongozo wa kawaida juu ya ukuzaji na utumiaji wa hatua za matibabu kwa virusi vya orthopox. kuandaa mikakati ya kujiandaa na kukabiliana, na zana za kutekeleza hatua za kujitayarisha
translations.hypothesis: Utafiti kuhusu virusi vya variola na maendeleo ya hatua za kupinga, pamoja na ushirikishwaji kwa mashauriano ya WHO kuhusu utafiti wa virusi vya variola (ACVVR), ushirikishwaji katika tathmini za mradi za utafiti kuhusu virusi vya variola kupitia ACVVR, usimamizi wa mahusiano na vituo viwili vya hifadhi za virusi vya variola (ambavyo pia ni vituo vya ushirikiano vya WHO), usajili, utambuzi nawachungisho za maombi kutoka kwa watafiti kuhusu DNA ya virusi vya variola kutoka kwa vituo hivyo, kwa sheria maalum, utekelezaji wa ukaguzi wa usalama wa kijamii na usalama wa biashara kwa vituo viwili vya kimataifa vya hifadhi za virusi vya variola, kuendeleza mwongozo wa kanuni kuhusu utafiti wa DNA ya virusi vya variola na masuala yanayoambatana, tathmini na majibu, ushirikishwaji na kuandaa mwongozo wa kanuni kuhusu maendeleo na matumizi ya hatua za kupinga, kuendeleza mikakati na zana za kutekeleza hatua za tathmini.

translations.source: maintaining and managing the smallpox global vaccine stockpile coordinating development of a laboratory network for orthopoxvirus detection and diagnostics developing normative guidance and response strategies for human monkeypox and other orthopoxviruses supporting capacity-building and developing training materials contributing to global health security initiatives Preserving global smallpox history overseeing the Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with WHO Archives and the WHO Library leading on policy discussions and commemorative events regarding smallpox eradication reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly The Secretariat also mobilizes and manages funding for these activities and for ad hoc events as required such as technical consultations and training.
translations.reference: kudumisha na kusimamia hifadhi ya kimataifa ya chanjo ya ndui kuratibu maendeleo ya mtandao wa maabara kwa utambuzi na uchunguzi wa virusi vya orthopox kuunda mwongozo wa kawaida na mikakati ya kukabiliana na ndui ya nyani kwa binadamu na virusi vingine vya (ndui) orthopox kusaidia kujenga uwezo na kuendeleza nyenzo za mafunzo kuchangia mipango ya usalama wa afya duniani Kuhifadhi historia ya ndui duniani kusimamia kumbukumbu za Mpango wa Kutokomeza Ugonjwa wa Ndui kwa ushirikiano na Shirika la Kumbukumbu la WHO na Maktaba ya WHO kuongoza mijadala ya sera na matukio ya ukumbusho kuhusu kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui kuripoti kila mwaka kwa Bodi ya Utendaji ya WHO na Bunge la Afya Ulimwenguni Sekretarieti pia hukusanya na kusimamia ufadhili wa shughuli hizi na kwa matukio ya dharura kama inavyohitajika kama vile mashauriano ya kiufundi na mafunzo.
translations.hypothesis: **Kuendeleza na kusimamia hazina ya chanjo ya ugonjwa wa smallpox, ushiriki katika uendelezaji wa mtandao wa лаборатория kwa utambuzi na uchunguzi wa virusi vya orthopox, kuandaa miongozo na majibu kwa ugonjwa wa human monkeypox na virusi vingine vya orthopox, kusaidia katika uendelezaji wa uwezo, na kuandaa vifaa vya mafunzo, kushiriki katika mashirika ya afya ya kimataifa, kuhifadhi historia ya ugonjwa wa smallpox, kuendesha arhivu za Mpango wa Kutotisha Ugonjwa wa Smallpox katika ushirikiano na arhivu za WHO na maktaba ya WHO, kuongoza katika majadiliano na hafla za kumbukumbu kuhusu kutotisha ugonjwa wa smallpox, kuripoti kila mwaka kwa Bodi ya Katibuni ya WHO na Majengo ya Afya ya Dunia, Seketarieti pia inakuza na kusimamia fedha kwa shughuli hizi na hafla za haraka kama inavyohitajika, kama vile mashauriano za kiufundi na mafunzo.**

translations.source: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments. However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable.
translations.reference: Kung'atwa na nyoka Muhtasari: Kuumwa na nyoka ni ugonjwa unaoweza kuhatarisha maisha unaosababishwa na sumu wakati wa kuumwa na nyoka mwenye sumu. Uchafuzi unaweza pia kusababishwa na kunyunyiziwa sumu machoni na aina fulani za nyoka ambao wana uwezo wa kutema sumu kama njia ya kujikinga. Juhudi duni za hapo awali za kudhibiti kuumwa kwa nyoka zimetoa data iliyogawanyika, isiyo sahihi ya epidemiolojia. Waathiriwa wengi hawahudhurii vituo vya afya au hospitali na badala yake wanategemea matibabu ya kienyeji. Hata hivyo, takwimu zinazopatikana zinaonyesha watu milioni 4.5–5.4 huumwa na nyoka kila mwaka. Kati ya hizi, milioni 1.8-2.7 hupata ugonjwa wa kliniki na 81,000 hadi 138,000 hufa kutokana na matatizo. Vikundi vilivyo katika hatari kubwa ni pamoja na wafanyikazi wa kilimo vijijini, wafugaji, wavuvi, wawindaji, watoto wanaofanya kazi, watu wanaoishi katika nyumba ambazo hazijajengwa vizuri na wale ambao wana fursa ndogo ya kupata elimu na huduma za afya. Maradhi na vifo hutokea mara nyingi zaidi miongoni mwa vijana na watoto hukumbwa na vifo vingi zaidi. Zaidi ya hayo, wanawake hupata vikwazo vilivyoongezeka vya kupata huduma za matibabu katika baadhi ya tamaduni na wanawake wajawazito wako katika hatari kubwa.
translations.hypothesis: Maelezo kuhusu ugumu: Ugumu ni ugonjwa ambao unaweza kuwa hatari na kusababisha vifo, na unaoasili kutokana na vitisho katika ugumu la mnyama hatari. Ugumu pia unaweza kutokea kutokana na mnyama kuweka vitisho kwenye macho, na aina fulani za nyama wana uwezo wa kuweka vitisho kama njia ya kujilinda. Majaribio ya zamani ya kudhibiti ugumu hayafikii matokeo, na hivyo kusababisha data zisizo sahihi. Watu wengi hawenda kwenye hospitali au zahanati, badala yake, wanategemea tiba za jadi. Hata hivyo, data zinazoexist kunaonyesha kwamba watu milioni 4.5–5.4 hupigwa na nyama kila mwaka. Kati ya hao, milioni 1.8–2.7 hupata magonjwa, na watu 81,000 hadi 138,000 kufa kutokana na matatizo. Kundi la hatari ni pamoja na wafanyikazi wa kilimo katika maeneo ya vijijini, wakulima, wasafishaji, wapiga, watoto wanaofanya kazi, watu wanaishi katika nyumba zisizo vizuri, na wale ambao hawana ufikiaji wa kielimu na huduma za afya. Magonjwa na vifo hutokea mara nyingi zaidi kwa vijana, na watoto wanapata vifo zaidi. Pia, wanawake wanakabiliana na vizuizi zaidi katika kupata huduma za matibabu katika baadhi ya tamaduni, na wanawake wanaogha ni hatarini sana.

translations.source: An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical. People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites.
translations.reference: Mgogoro unaoendelea unaozuia upatikanaji wa matibabu salama na madhubuti ya antivenom katika maeneo mengi, na hasa Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara, ni sababu moja inayochangia uwezekano wa kutafuta msaada kupitia dawa za jadi. Dalili: Kuumwa au kunyunyiziwa sumu kutoka kwa nyoka wenye sumu kunaweza kusababisha aina mbalimbali za dharura za kimatibabu. Ugonjwa wa sumu kutoka kwa aina tofauti za nyoka unaweza kusababisha dalili tofauti, zingine mbaya zaidi kuliko zingine. Hii inafanya utayarishaji wa antivenino sahihi kuwa tatizo linaloendelea. Evenoming inaweza kusababisha kupooza hatari ambayo inaweza kuzuia kupumua, na kufanya matibabu ya haraka muhimu. Watu wanaweza pia kupata matatizo ya kutokwa na damu ambayo yanaweza kusababisha kuvuja damu mbaya au kushindwa kwa figo kusikoweza kurekebishwa. Uharibifu mkubwa wa tishu za ndani pia unaweza kutokea, ambayo inaweza kusababisha ulemavu wa kudumu na hata kukatwa kwa kiungo. Watoto wako katika hatari kubwa ya athari kali kutokana na uzito mdogo wa mwili kuliko watu wazima. Vifo vingi na madhara makubwa kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka vinaweza kuzuilika kabisa kwa kufanya antivenom salama na madhubuti zipatikane na kufikiwa zaidi, hasa katika maeneo yenye hatari kubwa. Dawa za kupambana na nyoka za ubora wa juu ndizo matibabu pekee madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za sumu za kuumwa na nyoka.
translations.hypothesis: Mgogoro unaendelea ambao unauzuia upatikanaji wa matibabu salama na yenye athari za mzio, katika maeneo mengi, na hasa katika Afrika Kusini, ni moja ya sababu ambazo husababisha watu kusita katika kutafuta msaada kupitia dawa za jadi. Dalili: Majeraha au mzio kutoka kwenye nyani hatari yanaweza kusababisha matatizo ya kiafya kali. Mzio kutoka kwenye aina mbalimbali za nyani unaweza kusababisha dalili tofauti, na baadhi ni hatari zaidi. Hii inafanya maandalizi sahihi za mizoo kuwa tatizo linaloendelea. Mzio unaweza kusababisha ulemavu mkubwa ambao unaweza kuzuia kupumua, na hivyo uhusiano wa matibabu wa haraka ni muhimu. Watu pia wanaweza kukumbana na matatizo ya damu ambayo yanaweza kusababisha majeraha kali au kushindwa kwa vidai. Pia, unaweza kutokea uharibifu mkubwa wa tishu, ambao unaweza kusababisha ulemavu wa kudumu na hata upotezaji wa mtego. Watoto wana hatari kubwa ya athari kali kuliko watu wazima. Vifo na matokeo hatari kutoka kwenye majeraha ya nyani, kwa ujumla, yanaweza kuzuwa kwa kufanya mizoo salama na yenye athari kuwa naweza, hasa katika maeneo yenye hatari. Mizoo za nyani za ubora wa juu ndio matibabu pekee yenye athari ya kuzuia au kurekebisha athari nyingi za majeraha ya nyani.

translations.source: Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs. Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe).
translations.reference: Matibabu: Dawa za kuua nyoka ni matibabu madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za kuumwa na nyoka na zimejumuishwa katika orodha ya WHO ya dawa muhimu. Upatikanaji na ufikiwaji wa dawa hizi, pamoja na kuongeza uelewa juu ya mbinu za kimsingi za kuzuia miongoni mwa jamii na wahudumu wa afya, ni njia bora za kupunguza madhara makubwa na vifo kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka. Baada ya kuumwa na nyoka anayeshukiwa kuwa na sumu, fuata hatua hizi: Ondoka mara moja kutoka eneo ambalo kuumwa kulitokea. Ondoa kitu chochote kinachokaza karibu na sehemu ya mwili iliyoumwa ili kuepusha madhara ikiwa uvimbe utatokea. Mhakikishie mhathiriwa, kwani kuumwa na nyoka wengi wenye sumu hakusababishi kifo cha papo hapo. Zuisha mtu huyo kabisa na umpeleke kwenye kituo cha afya haraka iwezekanavyo Kuweka shinikizo kwenye sehemu za kung'atwa na padi ya shinikizo inayoweza kufaa katika baadhi ya matukio. Epuka njia za jadi za misaada ya kwanza au dawa za mitishamba. Paracetamol inaweza kutolewa kwa maumivu ya ndani (ambayo inaweza kuwa kali).
translations.hypothesis: Matibabu: العقارات za anti-venom za nyani ni tiba za ufanisi za kuzuia au kurekebisha madhara ya kawaida ya kuathiriwa na nyani, na zina katika orodha ya dawa muhimu za WHO. Upatikanaji na ufikiaji wa hizi anti-venom, pamoja na kuendeleza uelewa kuhusu njia za kuzuia, ni njia bora za kupunguza matokeo na vifo vya madhara ya nyani. Baada ya kuathiriwa na nyani, fuata hatua hizi: Halika haraka kutoka eneo ambapo uathiriwa. Ondoa kila kitu ambacho kinaweza kusumbua eneo lililotumiwa, ili kuepuka uharibifu ikiwa kuna uzito. Anzaisha mtu, kwani karibu kila uathiriwa na nyani haufurahishi vifo. Fanya mtu kuwa salama na upeleke hospitalini haraka. Kutumia kitovu cha shinikizo katika eneo lililotumiwa kunaweza kuwa na matumizi katika baadhi ya matendo. Epuka njia za matibabu za jadi au dawa za mimea. Paracetamol inaweza kutumika kwa maumivu ya eneo (ambayo inaweza kuwa kubwa).

translations.source: Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary
translations.reference: Kutapika kunaweza kutokea, hivyo kumweka mtu upande wake wa kushoto katika nafasi ya kurejeshwa. Fuatilia kwa karibu njia ya hewa na kupumua na uwe tayari kufufua ikiwa ni lazima
translations.hypothesis: Mdomo kunaweza kuwa na matumbo, kwa hivyo weka mtu huyo kwenye upande wa kushoto katika nafasi ya kupulizana.fuata kwa karibu njia ya hewa na kupumua, na kuwa tayari kutoa msaada ikiwa inahitajika.

translations.source: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness. Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled;
translations.reference: helminthiases ya udongo Muhtasari: Maambukizi ya ugonjwa wa helminth (STH) ni miongoni mwa maambukizi ya kawaida duniani kote na huathiri jamii maskini zaidi na maskini zaidi. Husambazwa na mayai yaliyopo kwenye kinyesi cha binadamu ambayo nayo huchafua udongo katika maeneo ambayo usafi wa mazingira ni duni. Aina kuu zinazoambukiza watu ni minyoo (Ascaris lumbricoides), mjeledi (Trichuris trichiura) na ndoano (Necator americanus na Ancylostoma duodenale). Minyoo ya matumbo hutoa dalili nyingi ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara, maumivu ya tumbo), malaise ya jumla na udhaifu. Hookworms husababisha kupoteza damu kwa muda mrefu kwa utumbo na kusababisha anemia. Helminths ya udongo hupitishwa kwa mayai ambayo hupitia kwenye kinyesi cha watu walioambukizwa. Minyoo ya watu wazima huishi kwenye utumbo ambapo hutoa maelfu ya mayai kila siku. Katika maeneo ambayo hayana usafi wa kutosha, mayai haya huchafua udongo.: mayai ambayo yameshikanishwa na mboga humezwa wakati mboga haijapikwa kwa uangalifu, kuosha au kuchambuliwa;
translations.hypothesis: Maelezo kuhusu magonjwa yanayosababishwa na nyama: Magonjwa yanayosababishwa na nyama (STH) ni moja wao maarufu duniani, na yanaathiri jamii za maskini zaidi. Hufanyika kupitia mayai ambayo yana kwenye dondoo za binadamu, ambapo kwa wimbo, vyanzo vinavifanya vimumbe katika maeneo ambapo usafi ni hafifu. Visi vikuu ambavyo vinasababisha magonjwa ni nyama ya mzungo (Ascaris lumbricoides), nyama ya mshale (Trichuris trichiura) na nyama za mshale (Necator americanus na Ancylostoma duodenale). Nyama za matumbo husababisha dalili nyingi, pamoja na dalili za matumbo (matumbo, maumivu), ugonjwa wa jumla na ukosefu wa nguvu. Nyama za mshale husababisha upotezaji wa damu wa muda mrefu katika matumbo, ambapo husababisha ugonjwa wa upungufu wa damu. Nyama za matumbo hufanyika kupitia mayai ambavyo hutoka kwenye dondoo za watu walioambukizwa. Nyama za watu wazima hujitwa katika matumbo, ambapo husambaza mayai elfu kila siku. Katika maeneo ambapo usafi ni hafifu, mayai haya vinaweza kufanya vimumbe. Hii inaweza kutokea kwa njia mbalimbali: mayai ambayo yameambatishwa kwenye mboga, hutumwa wakati mboga hazijawashwa, au kuondolewa.

translations.source: eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development. Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas.
translations.reference: mayai humezwa kutoka kwa vyanzo vya maji vilivyochafuliwa; mayai humezwa na watoto wanaocheza kwenye udongo uliochafuliwa na kisha kuweka mikono midomoni mwao bila kunawa. Dalili: Ugonjwa unahusiana na idadi ya minyoo waliohifadhiwa. Watu walio na maambukizi ya nguvu ya mwanga (minyoo wachache) kwa kawaida hawateseki kutokana na maambukizi. Maambukizi mazito zaidi yanaweza kusababisha dalili mbalimbali ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara na maumivu ya tumbo), utapiamlo, malaise ya jumla na udhaifu, na kuharibika kwa ukuaji na ukuaji wa mwili. Maambukizi ya nguvu ya juu sana yanaweza kusababisha kizuizi cha matumbo ambacho kinapaswa kutibiwa kwa upasuaji. S. stercoralis inaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa ngozi na utumbo na pia inajulikana kuhusishwa na utapiamlo sugu kwa watoto. Katika kesi ya kupungua kwa kinga ya mwenyeji, vimelea vinaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa hyperinfection/usambazaji ambao ni hatari kila wakati ikiwa haujaponywa mara moja na ipasavyo na mara nyingi husababisha kifo licha ya matibabu. Mnamo mwaka wa 2001, wajumbe katika Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni waliidhinisha kwa kauli moja azimio (WHA54.19) likizitaka nchi zilizo na ugonjwa huo kuanza kukabiliana na minyoo kwa umakini, haswa kichocho na helminths ya udongo. Mkakati wa udhibiti wa maambukizo ya helminth yanayopitishwa kwenye udongo ni kudhibiti maradhi kupitia matibabu ya mara kwa mara ya watu walio hatarini wanaoishi katika maeneo yenye ugonjwa.
translations.hypothesis: ماجي, hupatikana kutoka kwenye maji iliyo na uchafuzi; watoto hupata maji, na kisha wanaweka mikono yao kwenye midongo bila kuosha. Athari: Athari zinahusishwa na idadi ya nyama. Watu wenye magonjwa hafifu (nyama wachache) kwa kawaida hawapati magonjwa. Magonjwa zaidi yanaweza kusababisha matokeo mbalimbali, pamoja na magonjwa ya matumbo (matumbo na maumivu), utoaji, malaise, na ukosefu wa nguvu, na ukuaji na maendeleo ya mwili. Magonjwa yenye nguvu kubwa yanaweza kusababisha blockage ya matumbo, ambayo inahitaji matibabu. S. stercoralis inaweza kusababisha magonjwa ya ngozi na matumbo, na pia inajulikana kuwa inaambatana na utoaji wa nyama katika watoto. Katika kesi ya uondoaji wa kinga, parasite inaweza kusababisha magonjwa ya hyperinfection/dissemination, ambazo kwa kawaida huwa hatari ikiwa hazitibuliwa haraka na kwa usahihi, na mara nyingi huwa hatari licha ya matibabu. Katika mwaka wa 2001, wajumbe katika Mkutano wa Afya wa Dunia walitambua kwa pamoja (WHA54.19) kuwa wajitahidi kupunguza magonjwa, haswa schistosomiasis na nyama iliyoenezeshwa kwenye ardhi. Mbinu ya kudhibiti magonjwa yanayoenezeshwa kwenye ardhi ni kudhibiti athari kupitia matibabu ya mara kwa mara kwa watu wenye hatari wanaojiishi katika maeneo yenye magonjwa.

translations.source: People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners. TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.reference: Watu walio katika hatari ni: watoto wa shule ya mapema/ chekechea watoto wa umri wa shule wanawake wa umri wa uzazi (ikiwa ni pamoja na wanawake wajawazito katika trimester ya pili na ya tatu na wanawake wanaonyonyesha); watu wazima katika kazi fulani hatarishi kama vile wachumaji chai au wachimbaji. Matibabu Dawa zinazopendekezwa na WHO -albendazole (400 mg) na mebendazole (miligramu 500) - ni nzuri, bei nafuu na ni rahisi kusimamiwa na wafanyikazi wasio wa matibabu (k.m. walimu). Zimepitia majaribio ya kina ya usalama na zimetumika kwa mamilioni ya watu wenye athari chache na ndogo. Albendazole na mebendazole zote mbili zimetolewa kwa wizara za afya za kitaifa kupitia WHO katika nchi zote zilizo na ugonjwa huo kwa matibabu ya watoto wote wa umri wa kwenda shule. Ivermectin kwa udhibiti wa S. stercoralis inatarajiwa kupatikana kwa bei nafuu kuanzia 2021.
translations.hypothesis: Watu wana hatari ni: watoto wa shule, watoto wa umri wa shule, wanawake wanaoshikilia, pamoja na wanawake wajawazishi (kama vile wanawake wanaoshikilia katika trimester ya pili na tatu, na wanawake wanaolala), watu katika kazi fulani ambazo zina hatari kubwa, kama vile wakulima wa chai au wachimbaji. Matibabu: Madawa iliyopendekezwa na WHO – albendazole (400 mg) na mebendazole (500 mg) – ni na ufanisi, bei nafuu, na rahisi kutumika na watu ambavyo si madaktari (kama vile walimu). Madawa haya imepitia majaribio kamili ya usalama, na wameitumika kwa mamilioni ya watu, na madhara madogo. Albendazole na mebendazole zimepewa serikali kupitia WHO katika nchi zote ambazo magonjwa haya ni ya kawaida, kwa matibabu ya watoto wote wa umri wa shule. Ivermectin kwa kudhibiti S. stercoralis inatarajiwa kuwa inapatikana kwa bei nafuu kuanzia mwaka wa 2021.

translations.source: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030. In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics.
translations.reference: watoto wanazaliwa wakiwa wamekufa Muhtasari: Mtoto anayefariki dunia baada ya wiki 28 za ujauzito, lakini kabla au wakati wa kuzaliwa, huainishwa kuwa mfu. Kuna karibu watoto milioni 2 wanaozaliwa kila mwaka - mmoja kila baada ya sekunde 16. Zaidi ya 40% ya watoto wote wanao kufa kabla ya kuzaliwa hutokea wakati wa leba - hasara ambayo inaweza kuepukwa kwa kuboreshwa kwa huduma bora na yenye heshima wakati wa kujifungua ikiwa ni pamoja na ufuatiliaji wa kawaida na upatikanaji wa huduma ya dharura ya uzazi inapohitajika. Kupata mtoto aliyekufa wakati wa ujauzito au kuzaa ni janga ambalo halijashughulikiwa vya kutosha katika ajenda za kimataifa, sera na programu zinazofadhiliwa. Kuna gharama za kisaikolojia, hasa kwa wanawake na familia zao, kama vile unyogovu wa uzazi, athari za kifedha na athari za kiuchumi, pamoja na unyanyapaa na mwiko. Mnamo mwaka wa 2014, Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni liliidhinisha Mpango wa Utekelezaji wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (ENAP), ambao unajumuisha lengo la kimataifa la watoto 12 au pungufu wa trimester ya tatu (marehemu) wanaojifungua kwa jumla ya watoto 1,000 wanaozaliwa katika kila nchi ifikapo 2030. Mnamo 2021, nchi 139 hasa zenye kipato cha juu na za juu za kipato cha kati zilifikia lengo hili, lakini nchi 56 hazitafikia lengo la ENAP kufikia 2030 ikiwa juhudi zaidi hazitafanywa. Ikiwa mwelekeo wa sasa utaendelea, watoto milioni 15.9 watazaliwa wamekufa; karibu nusu ya hizi (milioni 7.7, 48%) zitatokea katika Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara. Sababu: Kukiwa na huduma bora za afya wakati wote wa ujauzito na kujifungua, watoto wengi wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa wanaweza kuzuilika. Sababu kuu za uzazi ni pamoja na matatizo ya ujauzito na uzazi, ujauzito wa muda mrefu, maambukizi ya uzazi kama vile malaria, kaswende na VVU, hali ya uzazi hasa shinikizo la damu, kisukari, na kizuizi cha ukuaji wa fetasi (wakati mtoto ambaye hajazaliwa hawezi kufikia uwezo wake wa ukuaji na hivyo kuwa mdogo kuliko inavyopaswa kuwa). Matatizo ya kuzaliwa nayo yanasababisha chini ya 10% ya watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamefariki walioripotiwa kitaifa katika nchi zenye mapato ya juu na idadi kamili katika mazingira ya kipato cha chini haijulikani kwa sababu ya mapungufu katika uchunguzi.
translations.hypothesis: Muhtasari wa vifo vya watoto: Mtoto ambaye kufa baada ya wiki 28 za ujauzito, lakini kabla au wakati wa kuzaliwa, huandikwa kama vifo vya watoto. Kimsingi, kuna vifo milioni 2 kila mwaka – mojawapo kila baada ya sekunde 16. Zaidi ya 40% ya vifo vya watoto hufanyika wakati wa kuzaliwa – ambayo inaweza kuepukwa kwa utunzaji bora na wa heshima wakati wa kuzaliwa, pamoja nawachungisho wa mara kwa mara na upatikanaji wa haraka wa huduma za matibabu za ujauzito wakati inahitajika. Kukumbana na vifo vya watoto wakati wa ujauzito au kuzaliwa ni tukio ambalo haujawahi kujitokeza katika mipango, sera, na programu za kimataifa. Kuna gharama za kisaikolojia, hasa kwa wanawake na familia zao, kama vile uchocheaji wa wanawake, matokeo ya kifedha, na athari za kiuchumi, pamoja na tabu na utata. Katika mwaka wa 2014, Kamati ya Afya ya Dunia ilithibitisha "Mpango wa Kufanya kwa Watoto," ambao una lengo la kimataifa la vifo vya watoto katika trimester ya tatu (siku za mwisho) kuwa 12 au chini zaidi kwa kila 1,000 kuzaliwa katika kila nchi hadi mwaka wa 2030. Katika mwaka wa 2021, mataifa 139, hasa mataifa yenye uchumi wa juu na wastani, yalifanikiwa kufikia lengo hili, lakini mataifa 56 hawatafanikiwa kufikia lengo la ENAP hadi mwaka wa 2030 ikiwa juhudi zaidi hazitolewa. Ikiwa mwenendo wa sasa utaendelea, watoto milioni 15.9 wata kufa; karibu nusu ya hao (milioni 7.7, 48%) watajiitokea katika Afrika Kusini. Sababu: Kwa huduma bora za afya wakati wa ujauzito na kuzaliwa, vifo vya watoto wengi vinaweza kuzuiliwa. Sababu kuu za vifo vya watoto ni matatizo yanayohusiana na ujauzito na kuzaliwa, ujauzito wa muda mrefu, magonjwa ya wanawake kama malaria, syphilis, na ugonjwa wa HIV, hali za wanawake hasa shinikizo la damu, na ugonjwa wa sukari, na usimamizi wa ukuaji wa mtoto (ambapo mtoto ambaye haijaliwa hawezi kufikia uwezo wake wa ukuaji na kwa hiyo ni mdogo zaidi kuliko inavyopaswa). Ugonjwa wa kuzaliwa ni msababisho wa chini ya 10% ya vifo vya watoto ambavyo vimeelezwa kitaifa katika mataifa yenye uchumi wa juu, na uwiano halisi katika mataifa yenye uchumi mdogo haijulikana kutokana na vikwazo katika utambuzi.

translations.source: Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond. This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered.
translations.reference: Mambo yanayohusiana na umri wa mama na kufanya mazoezi ya kuvuta sigara yanaweza pia kuongeza hatari ya ugonjwa wa uzazi na vifo vya kabla ya kuzaliwa. Kujifungua kunaweza kuzuiwa kwa njia ya kupanga uzazi ili kuepuka mimba zisizotarajiwa, afya bora na lishe bora kabla na wakati wa ujauzito, huduma bora na yenye heshima katika ujauzito na uzazi ikijumuisha wafanyakazi wa afya wenye ujuzi wa kutosha wakiwemo wakunga. Matibabu ya kaswende kwa wajauzito inaweza kuzuia watoto wanaokadiriwa kufikia 200,000 wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa, wakati ufuatiliaji wa mapigo ya moyo wa fetasi na ufuatiliaji wa leba na uingiliaji wa haraka inapohitajika ni muhimu kwa kuzuia uzazi 832,000 ndani ya uzazi na kupunguza vifo vya watoto wachanga. Watoto waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa huhusishwa sana na viashirio vya kijamii na kiuchumi vya kiafya. Kinga na utunzaji sikivu unahitaji kuunganishwa katika mwendelezo wa huduma ya afya ya uzazi, na zaidi. Hii ni pamoja na utunzaji wa heshima na msaada katika tukio la kifo. Kusikiliza uzoefu na sauti za wanawake na jumuiya zao ni muhimu ili kusaidia kushughulikia masuala ya unyanyapaa unaohusishwa na kifo cha kabla. Jibu la WHO: Mengi zaidi yanahitajika kufanywa ili kujumuisha uzuiaji wa mimba mfu ndani ya ajenda za kimataifa na kitaifa za huduma ya afya ya hali ya juu kwa wanawake, vijana na watoto. Kupitia Mkakati wa Kimataifa wa Afya ya Wanawake, Watoto na Vijana 2016–2030 na kutekeleza mapendekezo ya Mpango Kazi wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (2014), WHO inajitahidi kukomesha uzazi unaoweza kuzuilika, kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuboresha afya ya uzazi, watoto wachanga, watoto na vijana. Watoto wengi waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa hawapati cheti cha kuzaliwa na hawajasajiliwa.
translations.hypothesis: Faktor zinazohusiana na umri wa mama na tabibu, zinaweza pia kuongeza hatari ya magonjwa ya mama na kuzaliwa. Magonjwa ya kuzaliwa nje ya mwili yanaweza kuzuwa kupitia kupanga familia ili kuepuka ujauzito ambao si unayotaka, afya na lishe bora kabla na wakati wa ujauzito, huduma bora na za heshima za ujauzito na kuzaliwa, pamoja na wataalamu wa afya wenye ujuzi, pamoja na wachungaji. Matibabu ya syphilis wakati wa ujauzito yanaweza pia kuzuwa magonjwa ya kuzaliwa nje ya mwili takribani 200,000, wakati pia,wachungaji na utunzaji wa ujauzito na matibabu za haraka wakati inahitajika ni muhimu kwa kuzuwa magonjwa ya kuzaliwa nje ya mwili takribani 832,000 na kupunguza vifo vya watoto. Magonjwa ya kuzaliwa nje ya mwili yana uhusiano mkubwa na sababu za kiafya ambazo ni mbaya. Kuzuwa na huduma inayofaa lazima ziongozwa katika mfululizo wa huduma za afya za mama, na zaidi. Hii ni pamoja na huduma za heshima na za kusaidia katika tukio la kifo. Kusikiliza matarajio na sauti za wanawake na jamii zao ni muhimu ili kushughulikia masuala yanayohusiana na stigma inayohusiana na magonjwa ya kuzaliwa nje ya mwili. Majibu ya WHO: Ni muhimu zaidi kufanya kazi ili kuunganisha kuzuwa magonjwa ya kuzaliwa nje ya mwili katika agenda za kimataifa na za kitaifa za huduma bora za afya kwa wanawake, watoto, na watoto wachanga. Kwa kutumia Mkakati wa Kimataifa wa Afya ya Wanawake, Watoto, na Watoto wachanga 2016–2030 na kutekeleza mapendekezo ya Mkakati wa Kufanya Kila Jambo kwa Watoto wachanga (2014), WHO ina kazi ya kuondoa magonjwa ya kuzaliwa nje ya mwili ambazo zinaweza kuzuwa, kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuboresha afya ya mama, watoto wachanga, watoto, na watoto wachanga. Magonjwa ya kuzaliwa nje ya mwili, karibu yote, hayafanyi hati za kuzaliwa na hayataji.

translations.source: Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by: increasing awareness and reducing stigma supporting bereaved women and families strengthening health systems to provide high-quality care nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets improving measurement of stillbirths to enhance evidence and knowledge.
translations.reference: Kuboresha mifumo ya kuripoti kuzaliwa na vifo vya watoto wachanga ni suala la haki za binadamu na sharti la kupunguza watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa. Aidha, kuna haja ya kuimarisha mifumo ya kawaida ya taarifa za afya ili kunasa na kuripoti kwa usahihi watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa ajili ya ufuatiliaji na kuboresha ubora wa huduma. WHO hutoa zana kusaidia nchi kuboresha data zao kuhusu watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa na vifo vya watoto wachanga. WHO pia ina mwongozo wa kusaidia nchi kukagua na kuchunguza vifo vya watu binafsi ili ziweze kupendekeza na kutekeleza masuluhisho ya kuzuia vile vile katika siku zijazo na mfumo wa kawaida wa uainishaji wa vifo wakati wa kujifungua (ICD-PM). WHO na UNICEF wanaunga mkono wito wa kuchukua hatua za pamoja kukomesha watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa njia zifuatazo: kuongeza uelewa na kupunguza unyanyapaa kusaidia wanawake na familia zilizofiwa kuimarisha mifumo ya afya ili kutoa huduma bora kutaifisha na kuainisha malengo ya uzazi kuboresha upimaji wa watoto waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa ili kuongeza ushahidi na maarifa.
translations.hypothesis: Kuboresha mifumo ya taarifa kuhusu kuzaliwa na vifo vya watoto mapema ni jambo la haki za binadamu na ni hitaji wa kupunguza vifo vya watoto mapema. Pia, kuna haja ya kuimarisha mifumo ya taarifa za afya ya kawaida ili kukamata na taarifa sahihi kuhusu vifo vya watoto mapema kwa ajili yawachungulia na kuboresha ubora wa huduma. WHO inatoa zana za kusaidia nchi kuboresha data zao kuhusu vifo vya watoto mapema na vifo vya watoto mapema. WHO pia ina mwongozo ili kusaidia nchi kukagua na kuchunguza vifo vyenyewe ili waweze kuanzisha na kutekeleza suluhisho za kuzuia vifo vya aina hiyo katika siku zijazo, na mfumo wa kawaida wa ufuatiliaji wa vifo vya perinatal (ICD-PM). WHO na UNICEF zinaunga mkono wito wa hatua pamoja za kuacha vifo vya watoto mapema ambavyo yanaweza kuzuwa kwa: kuongeza uelewa na kupunguza stigma, kusaidia wanawake na familia waliopitia, kuimarisha mifumo ya afya ili kutoa huduma bora, kuweka malengo ya vifo vya watoto mapema, na kuboresha ukaguzi wa vifo vya watoto mapema ili kuendeleza ushahidi na maarifa.

translations.source: Substandard and falsified medical products Overview: Up to two billion people around the world lack access to necessary medicines, vaccines, medical devices including in vitro diagnostics, and other health products, which creates a vacuum that is too often filled by substandard and falsified products. This problem is growing as global supply chains become more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may be packaged in a second country and distributed across borders to be marketed or sold to consumers in a third. The growth of e-commerce also contributes to this trend by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often from unauthorized sources. WHO has identified this issue one of the urgent health challenges for the next decade given that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or falsified. No country remains untouched from this issue, and WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical medicines, vaccines and in vitro diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both generic and innovator medicines can be falsified, ranging from very expensive products for cancer to very inexpensive products for treatment of pain. WHO Member States have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection and response to move towards achieving increased access to quality, safe, effective and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products create many problems for both the individual taking the medicine and for families, the larger society, health care system and supply chain. When medicines do not work the way they should, they can fail to treat or prevent disease, contribute to antimicrobial resistance, waste precious resources and lead to a loss of confidence in medicines and distrust of healthcare providers.
translations.reference: Bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na duni Muhtasari: Hadi watu bilioni mbili duniani kote hawana uwezo wa kufikia dawa zinazohitajika, chanjo, vifaa vya matibabu ikiwa ni pamoja na uchunguzi wa ndani na bidhaa nyingine za afya, jambo ambalo huzua ombwe ambalo mara nyingi hujazwa na bidhaa duni na ghushi. Tatizo hili linaongezeka huku misururu ya ugavi duniani ikizidi kuwa changamano, kumaanisha kuwa bidhaa zinazotengenezwa katika nchi moja zinaweza kuunganishwa katika nchi ya pili na kusambazwa katika mipaka ili kupelekwa sokoni au kuuzwa kwa watumiaji katika nchi ya tatu. Ukuaji wa biashara ya mtandaoni pia huchangia mwelekeo huu kwa kurahisisha ununuzi wa dawa mtandaoni, mara nyingi kutoka kwa vyanzo visivyoidhinishwa. WHO imebainisha suala hili kuwa moja ya changamoto za dharura za kiafya kwa muongo ujao ikizingatiwa kuwa zaidi ya dawa moja kati ya kumi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati inakadiriwa kuwa duni au kughushi. Hakuna nchi ambayo haijaguswa kutokana na suala hili, na WHO imepokea ripoti za dawa za kimatibabu zisizo na viwango au duni, chanjo na uchunguzi wa kimatibabu kutoka maeneo yote ya dunia. Dawa zote mbili za kawaida na za wabunifu zinaweza kughushiwa, kuanzia bidhaa za bei ghali sana za saratani hadi bidhaa za bei nafuu kwa matibabu ya maumivu. Nchi Wanachama wa WHO zimekubaliana kuhusu mkakati wa kina wa kimataifa unaolenga kuzuia, kugundua na kukabiliana na hali hiyo ili kufikia kiwango cha juu cha upatikanaji wa bidhaa bora za matibabu, salama, makini na bora. Athari: Bidhaa za kimatibabu zisizo na kiwango na zisizo za kweli huleta matatizo mengi kwa mtu binafsi anayetumia dawa na kwa familia, jamii kubwa, mfumo wa huduma za afya na mnyororo wa usambazaji. Wakati dawa hazifanyi kazi inavyopaswa, zinaweza kushindwa kutibu au kuzuia magonjwa, kuchangia upinzani wa antimicrobial, kupoteza rasilimali za thamani na kusababisha kupoteza imani katika madawa na kutoamini watoa huduma za afya.
translations.hypothesis: Bidhaa za matibabu za ubora na bandia: Muhtasari: Hadi bilioni mbili za watu ulimwenguni hazipata ufikiaji wa dawa, chanjo, vifaa za matibabu, pamoja na utambuzi wa nje, na bidhaa zingine za afya, ambazo husababisha fursa ambayo mara nyingi linajaa na bidhaa za ubora na bandia. Masuala haya yanazidi, kwani matawi ya kimataifa yanazidi kuwa ngumu, kumaanisha kuwa bidhaa zozote zozote zinazozalishwa katika nchi moja zinaweza kupakwa katika nchi nyingine na kuuzwa katika mipaka, ili kuuzwa kwa watumiaji katika nchi nyingine. Pia, ukuaji wa biashara mtandaoni unaunga mkono mwenendo huu, kwa kuwa ni rahisi kununua dawa mtandaoni, mara nyingi kutoka kwa vyanzo ambavyo si halali. Shirika la Afya ya Dunia (WHO) amechambua masuala haya kama mojawapo ya changamoto muhimu za afya katika muongo ujao, kwani takriban dawa moja kati ya kumi katika nchi na kiwango cha chini na katikati zinatarajiwa kuwa za ubora au bandia. Hakuna nchi ambayo imepita masuala haya, na WHO imepokea taarifa kuhusu dawa, chanjo na utambuzi wa nje za ubora au bandia kutoka kwa kila eneo ulimwenguni. Dawa za kawaida na za ubunifu zinaweza kuwa bandia, kuanzia bidhaa za gharama kubwa kwa ajili ya tiba ya saratani hadi bidhaa za bei nafuu kwa ajili ya tiba ya maumivu. Matawi ya WHO wamekubaliana na mkakati kamili wa kimataifa unaoangazia kuzuia, kugundua na kujibu ili kufikia ufikiaji wa bidhaa za matibabu bora, salama, na za ubora. Athari: Bidhaa za matibabu za ubora na bandia husababisha masuala mengi kwa mtu anayotumia dawa na pia kwa familia, jamii kubwa, mfumo wa huduma za afya, na matawi ya kimataifa. Wakati dawa hazifanya kama zinapaswa, zinaweza kushindwa kutibu au kuzuia magonjwa, kusababisha upinzani wa bakteria, kutumia rasilimali muhimu, na kusababisha upotezaji wa imani katika dawa na uadui kwa watoaji huduma za afya.

translations.source: This can have a negative socioeconomic impact, contributing to related loss in productivity and adding expenses to the individual and national health system. They are most likely to reach patients in situations where there is constrained access to quality and safe medical products, poor governance and weak technical capacity. Although this issue impacts all countries, countries where there are weak or non-existent regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no one country has sufficient resources and capacity to deal with this issue in isolation. Through global systems and processes such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and WHO Member State Mechanism, Member States are working together to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.reference: Hii inaweza kuwa na athari mbaya ya kijamii na kiuchumi, kuchangia hasara inayohusiana na tija na kuongeza gharama kwa mfumo wa afya wa mtu binafsi na wa kitaifa. Wana uwezekano mkubwa wa kufikia wagonjwa katika hali ambapo kuna vikwazo vya upatikanaji wa bidhaa bora na salama za matibabu, utawala duni na uwezo dhaifu wa kiufundi. Ingawa suala hili linaathiri nchi zote, nchi ambako kuna mifumo dhaifu ya udhibiti au haipo hubeba mzigo mkubwa zaidi wa bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na duni. Katika ulimwengu unaozidi kuwa wa utandawazi, hakuna nchi moja yenye rasilimali na uwezo wa kutosha wa kushughulikia suala hili kwa kujitenga. Kupitia mifumo na michakato ya kimataifa kama vile Mfumo wa Ufuatiliaji na Ufuatiliaji wa Kimataifa wa WHO na Utaratibu wa Nchi Wanachama wa WHO, Nchi Wanachama zinafanya kazi pamoja ili kuzuia, kugundua na kujibu bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na potofu.
translations.hypothesis: Hili linaweza kuwa na athari hasi kiuchumi na kijamii, na kusababisha upungufu wa uzalishaji na kuongeza gharama kwa mfumo wa afya wa mtu binafsi na wa taifa. Wahusika wana uwezekano wa kufikia wagonjwa katika hali ambapo kuna upatikanaji mdogo wa bidhaa za matibabu bora na salama, serikali isiyo na uongo, na uwezo wa kiufundi dhaifu. Ingawa suala hili linaathiri nchi zote, nchi ambazo zina mifumo dhaifu au isiyo, zina athari kubwa za bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na ubora na bandilishi. Katika ulimwengu unaoendelea kuwa wa kimataifa, hakuna nchi ambayo ina rasilimali na uwezo wa kutosha kushughulikia suala hili peke yake. Kazi za kimataifa na taratibu, kama vile mfumo wa WHO wa Ufuatiliaji na Ufuatiliaji na Mfumo wa Mataifa Mshiriki, zinafanywa na mataifa ili kuzuia, kugundua, na kujibu bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na ubora na bandilishi.

translations.source: Suicide prevention Overview: More than 700 000 people die by suicide every year. For each suicide, there are an estimated more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all regions of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among 15–29-year-olds globally, and 77% of all suicides happened in low- and middle-income countries. Almost 20% of global suicides were due to pesticide self-poisoning. While the link between suicide and mental health conditions (in particular, depression and alcohol use disorders) and a previous suicide attempt is well established in high-income countries, many suicides happen impulsively in moments of crisis. Further risk factors for suicide include experience of loss, loneliness, discrimination, a relationship break-up, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and conflict or other humanitarian emergencies. Stigma around mental health conditions and suicide means many people thinking about suicide do not seek help. Suicides and suicide attempts have a ripple effect that impacts on families, friends, colleagues, communities and societies.
translations.reference: Kuzuia kujiua Muhtasari: Zaidi ya watu 700,000 hufa kwa kujiua kila mwaka. Kwa kila kujiua, kunakadiriwa kuwa zaidi ya majaribio 20 ya kujiua. Kujiua kunaweza kutokea katika hatua yoyote ya maisha na katika maeneo yote ya dunia. Mnamo mwaka wa 2019, kujiua ilikuwa sababu ya nne ya vifo kati ya vijana wenye umri wa miaka 15-29 ulimwenguni, na 77% ya watu wote waliojiua walitokea katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Takriban 20% ya watu wanaojiua duniani kote walitokana na kujitia sumu. Ingawa uhusiano kati ya kujiua na hali ya afya ya akili (haswa, huzuni na matatizo ya matumizi ya pombe) na jaribio la awali la kujiua limethibitishwa vyema katika nchi za mapato ya juu, kujiua kwa watu wengi hutokea bila kutarajia wakati wa shida. Sababu zaidi za hatari ya kujiua ni pamoja na uzoefu wa kupoteza, upweke, ubaguzi, kuvunjika kwa uhusiano, matatizo ya kifedha, maumivu ya kudumu na ugonjwa, vurugu, unyanyasaji na migogoro au dharura nyingine za kibinadamu. Unyanyapaa kwenye afya ya akili na kujiua inamaanisha watu wengi wanaofikiria juu ya kujiua hawatafuti msaada. Kujiua na majaribio ya kujiua kuna athari mbaya ambayo huathiri familia, marafiki, wafanyakazi wenza, jamii na jamii.
translations.hypothesis: Ushauri kuhusu kupunguza mauaji: Zaidi ya watu 700,000 kufa kutokana na mauaji kila mwaka. Kwa kila mauaji, kuna takwimu za zaidi ya majaribio 20 ya mauaji. Mauaji yanaweza kutokea katika kila hatua ya maisha na katika kila eneo duniani. Mnamo 2019, mauaji ilikuwa mojawapo ya sababu kuu za vifo miongoni mwa watu wa umri wa 15-29 duniani, na asilimia 77 ya mauaji yaliendelea katika nchi na mataifa yenye uchumi mdogo na wa katikati. Kimsingi asilimia 20 ya mauaji duniani ilikuwa kwa sababu ya pombe. Ingawa uhusiano kati ya mauaji na hali za afya ya akili (hasa, matumaini na ugonjwa wa pombe) na jaribio la mauaji lililo, ni dhahiri katika nchi na mataifa yenye uchumi wa juu, mauaji mengi hufanyika kwa njia ya ghafla katika wakati wa shida. Mambo mengine yanayouza hatari ya mauaji ni pamoja na kupoteza, loneliness, ubaguzi, kutengana, matatizo ya kifedha, magonjwa ya muda, uvunjaji, unyanyasaji, na migogoro au majanga mengine ya kiraia. Tabia ya ubaguzi kuhusu hali za afya ya akili na mauaji, inamaanisha kwamba watu wengi wanafikiria kuhusu mauaji, hawatazungukia msaada. Mauaji na majaribio ya mauaji ina athari inayoelekea kwenye familia, marafiki, wenzas, jamii na jamii.

translations.source: Suicide: facts and figures globally Suicide worldwide in 2019 report LIVE LIFE: an implementation guide for suicide prevention in countries Prevention: Much can be done at individual, community and national levels to prevent suicide and suicide attempts. WHO’s LIVE LIFE approach recommends four effective, evidence-based key interventions which should be included in every national response to suicide: limit access to the means of suicide interact with the media for responsible reporting of suicide foster socio-emotional life skills in adolescents early identify, assess, manage and follow up anyone who is affected by suicidal behaviours. The foundational pillars of the interventions are situation analysis, multisectoral collaboration, awareness raising, capacity building, financing, surveillance, monitoring and evaluation.
translations.reference: Kujiua: ukweli na takwimu duniani kote Kujiua ulimwenguni kote katika ripoti ya 2019 LIVE LIFE: mwongozo wa utekelezaji wa kuzuia kujiua katika nchi Kinga: Mengi yanaweza kufanywa katika ngazi ya mtu binafsi, jamii na kitaifa ili kuzuia majaribio ya kujiua na kujiua. Mtazamo wa LIVE LIFE wa WHO unapendekeza hatua nne muhimu, zenye msingi wa ushahidi ambazo zinapaswa kujumuishwa katika kila mwitikio wa kitaifa wa kujiua: kupunguza upatikanaji wa njia za kujiua kuingiliana na vyombo vya habari kwa kuripoti habari za kujiua kukuza stadi za maisha ya kijamii na kimhemko kwa vijana kutambua mapema, kutathmini, kusimamia na kufuatilia mtu yeyote ambaye ameathiriwa na tabia za kujiua. Nguzo za msingi za afua hizo ni uchambuzi wa hali, ushirikiano wa sekta mbalimbali, kuongeza uelewa, kujenga uwezo, ufadhili, ufuatiliaji, ufuatiliaji na tathmini.
translations.hypothesis:
**Suicide: الحقائق na takwimu kimataifa**

Suicide duniani mwaka 2019: Ripoti YA KWISHA: Mwongozo wa matumizi kwa kupunguza suicide katika nchi
Kupunguza: Jambo nyingi zinaweza kufanyika katika viwango vya mtu binafsi, jamii na taifa ili kupunguza suicide na majaribio ya suicide. Mbinu ya "LIVE LIFE" ya WHO inapendekeza michakato minne yenye ufanisi, inayotegemea ushahidi ambayo yanapaswa kujumuishwa katika majibu yote ya taifa kuhusu suicide: kupunguza ufikiaji wa alama za suicide, kuwasiliana na vyombo vya habari kwa taarifa mahsusi kuhusu suicide, kuendeleza ujuzi wa kijamii na kihembinu kwa vijana, na kutambua, kutathmini, kusimamia na kufuatilia watu walioathiriwa na tabia za suicide. Mipingo muhimu ya michakato ni uchambuzi wa hali, ushirikiano wa sekta mbalimbali, uelewa, uendelezaji wa uwezo, ufadhili,wachungaji,wachungwa na tathmini.

translations.source: Suicide prevention efforts require coordination and collaboration among multiple sectors, including health, education, labour, agriculture, business, justice, law, defence, politics and the media. Suicide prevention is often not adequately addressed because of stigma and lack of awareness that suicide is a major public health problem. Communities also play a crucial role in preventing suicide. The WHO community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying priorities and implementing activities according to local contexts. WHO response: The urgency to act to prevent suicides has been recognized and prioritized at the highest levels. The reduction of suicide rates in countries is an indicator in the UN Sustainable Development Goals (the only indicator for mental health), WHO’s General Programme of Work and WHO’s Comprehensive Mental Health Action Plan 2013–2030. The first WHO world suicide report, Preventing suicide: a global imperative, published in 2014, aimed to increase awareness of the public health significance of suicide and suicide attempts and make suicide prevention a public health priority. Governments are encouraged to implement WHO’s LIVE LIFE package of effective and evidence-based key interventions for suicide prevention, and to develop or strengthen comprehensive national suicide prevention strategies in a multisectoral public health approach. National leadership and coordination on suicide prevention are further supported by the publication of National suicide prevention strategies: progress, examples and indicators. Recognizing that data and statistics are necessary for advocacy and decision-making, WHO published Suicide worldwide in 2019: Global Health Estimates.
translations.reference: Juhudi za kuzuia kujiua zinahitaji uratibu na ushirikiano kati ya sekta nyingi, ikiwa ni pamoja na afya, elimu, kazi, kilimo, biashara, haki, sheria, ulinzi, siasa na vyombo vya habari. Uzuiaji wa kujiua mara nyingi haushughulikiwi ipasavyo kwa sababu ya unyanyapaa na ukosefu wa ufahamu kwamba kujiua ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma. Jamii pia ina jukumu muhimu katika kuzuia kujiua. Zana ya ushiriki wa jumuiya ya WHO huongoza jamii katika kutambua vipaumbele na kutekeleza shughuli kulingana na mazingira ya ndani. Jibu la WHO: Uharaka wa kuchukua hatua ili kuzuia kujiua umetambuliwa na kupewa kipaumbele katika viwango vya juu zaidi. Kupungua kwa viwango vya kujiua katika nchi ni kiashirio katika Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu ya Umoja wa Mataifa (kiashiria pekee cha afya ya akili), Mpango wa Kazi Mkuu wa WHO na Mpango Kamili wa Utekelezaji wa Afya ya Akili wa 2013–2030. Ripoti ya kwanza ya WHO ya kujiua duniani, Kuzuia kujiua: umuhimu wa kimataifa, iliyochapishwa katika 2014, ililenga kuongeza ufahamu wa umuhimu wa afya ya umma wa majaribio ya kujiua na kujiua na kufanya kuzuia kujiua kuwa kipaumbele cha afya ya umma. Serikali zinahimizwa kutekeleza kifurushi cha WHO cha LIVE LIFE cha afua muhimu zinazofaa na zenye msingi wa ushahidi kwa ajili ya kuzuia kujitoa mhanga, na kuendeleza au kuimarisha mikakati ya kitaifa ya kuzuia kujiua katika mkabala wa afya ya umma wa sekta mbalimbali. Uongozi wa kitaifa na uratibu juu ya kuzuia kujiua unasaidiwa zaidi na uchapishaji wa mikakati ya Kitaifa ya kuzuia kujiua: maendeleo, mifano na viashiria. Kwa kutambua kwamba data na takwimu ni muhimu kwa utetezi na kufanya maamuzi, WHO ilichapisha kujiua duniani kote mwaka wa 2019: Makadirio ya Afya Ulimwenguni.
translations.hypothesis: Mfululizo wa kupunguza mauaji ni muhimu na zinahitaji ushirikiano kati ya idara mbalimbali, pamoja na afya, elimu, ajira, kilimo, biashara, haki, sheria, ulinzi, siasa na vyombo vya habari. Kupunguza mauaji mara nyingi hazielewe vizuri kwa sababu ya tabia na ukosefu wa ufahamu kwamba mauaji ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma. Jumuiya pia zina jukumu muhimu katika kupunguza mauaji. Kitovu cha ushirikiano cha WHO kinaongoza jamii katika kutambua kipaumbele na kutekeleza shughuli kulingana na muktadha wa eneo. Jibu la WHO: Uthibitisho wa hatua za kupunguza mauaji umeigawanywa na umechaguliwa katika viwango vya juu. Kupunguza idadi ya mauaji katika nchi ni dalili katika Malengo ya Maendeleo endelevu ya Umoja wa Mataifa (dalili pekee kwa afya ya akili), Mpango Mkuu wa Kazi wa WHO na Mpango Kamili wa Hatua za Afya ya Akili la WHO 2013–2030. Ripoti ya kwanza ya ulimwengu kuhusu mauaji, "Kupunguza mauaji: jukumu muhimu", iliyotolewa mwaka wa 2014, ilikuwa lengo la kuongeza ufahamu kuhusu umuhimu wa mauaji katika afya ya umma na majaribio ya mauaji, na kuweka kupunguza mauaji kama jukumu muhimu la afya ya umma. Serikali zinaombwa kutekeleza paketi ya WHO ya mbinu muhimu na za msingi za kupunguza mauaji, na kuendeleza au kuimarisha mikakati kamili ya kitaifa za kupunguza mauaji katika mbinu za afya ya umma za idara mbalimbali. Uongozi na ushirikiano wa kitaifa katika kupunguza mauaji pia unaunga mkono kupitia utolewa wa "Mikakati ya Kupunguza Mauaji: Maendeleo, mifano na dalili". Rekebishi kwamba data na takwimu ni muhimu kwa utetezi na uamuzi, WHO ilitolea "Mauaji ulimwenguni mwaka wa 2019: Tathmini za Afya ya Umma" mwaka wa 2019.

translations.source: WHO’s vision is a world where death by suicide is no longer a leading cause of death and where people who are attempting or thinking about suicide feel comfortable seeking help which is provided capably and respectfully to all.
translations.reference: Maono ya WHO ni ulimwengu ambapo kifo kwa kujiua si sababu kuu ya vifo tena na ambapo watu wanaojaribu au wanaofikiria kujiua hujisikia vizuri kutafuta msaada ambao hutolewa kwa uwezo na heshima kwa wote.
translations.hypothesis: Maono wa WHO ni dunia ambapo ajali za pombe si sababu kuu ya vifo, na ambapo watu wanaojaribu au wanafikiria pombe, wana hisia ya faraja katika kuomba msaada, ambao una tawiwa kwa ufanisi na kwa heshima kwa wote.

translations.source: Sustainable development Overview: Sustainable development is a broad term to describe policies, projects and investments that provide benefits today without sacrificing environmental, social and personal health in the future. These policies are often described as green because they focus on limiting the impact of development on the environment. However, the benefits of sustainable development are also felt across a wide cross section of human health and well-being, including reductions in pollution- and environment-related disease, improved health outcomes and decreased stress. As the threats of climate change become increasingly important, win-win strategies for mitigation, health improvement and cost savings offer a range of advantages for various stakeholders. For example, greener health care operations can generate patient and worker health benefits while also saving energy, mitigating climate risks and creating long-term cost savings. The World Health Organization is committed to pursuing sustainable development in all its work to help protect the people of tomorrow from the health growing health risks of today. Impact: Many sustainable development strategies can offer significant wins for health, climate and the environment, and the benefits can be seen almost immediately. For example, many health and development goals can be achieved simultaneously by tackling air pollution, which is responsible for an estimated 7 million premature deaths annually. Polluting emissions include powerful short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—particularly black carbon, ozone and methane—which are very harmful to health but have only brief atmospheric lifetimes.
translations.reference: Maendeleo endelevu Muhtasari: Maendeleo endelevu ni neno pana la kuelezea sera, miradi na uwekezaji ambao hutoa manufaa leo bila kutoa dhabihu afya ya mazingira, kijamii na kibinafsi katika siku zijazo. Sera hizi mara nyingi hufafanuliwa kuwa za kijani kwa sababu zinalenga kupunguza athari za maendeleo kwa mazingira. Hata hivyo, manufaa ya maendeleo endelevu yanaonekana pia katika sehemu mbalimbali za afya na ustawi wa binadamu, ikiwa ni pamoja na kupunguzwa kwa uchafuzi- na magonjwa yanayohusiana na mazingira, matokeo bora ya afya na kupungua kwa dhiki. Kadiri matishio ya mabadiliko ya tabianchi yanavyozidi kuwa muhimu, mikakati ya kushinda, kuboresha afya na kuokoa gharama inatoa faida mbalimbali kwa wadau mbalimbali. Kwa mfano, shughuli za afya za kijani zinaweza kuzalisha manufaa ya afya ya mgonjwa na mfanyakazi huku pia kuokoa nishati, kupunguza hatari za hali ya hewa na kuokoa gharama za muda mrefu. Shirika la Afya Ulimwenguni limejitolea kutafuta maendeleo endelevu katika kazi zake zote kusaidia kuwalinda watu wa kesho dhidi ya hatari za kiafya zinazoongezeka leo. Athari: Mikakati mingi ya maendeleo endelevu inaweza kutoa mafanikio makubwa kwa afya, hali ya hewa na mazingira, na manufaa yanaweza kuonekana mara moja. Kwa mfano, malengo mengi ya afya na maendeleo yanaweza kufikiwa kwa wakati mmoja kwa kukabiliana na uchafuzi wa hewa, ambao unawajibika kwa vifo vya mapema milioni 7 kila mwaka. Uzalishaji chafuzi unajumuisha vichafuzi vikali vya hali ya hewa vya muda mfupi (SLCPs)—hasa kaboni nyeusi, ozoni na methane—ambazo ni hatari sana kwa afya lakini zina maisha mafupi tu ya angahewa.
translations.hypothesis: Maelezo ya maendeleo endelevu: Maendeleo endelevu ni mrefu ambao unaweza kutumika kufanya maelezo kuhusu sera, miradi na matumizi ambazo hutoa faida leo bila kuhatarisha mazingira, afya ya jamii na afya ya mtu binafsi katika siku zijazo. Sera hizi mara nyingi hualikwa kama "za kijani" kwa sababu zinaangazia kupunguza athari za maendeleo kwenye mazingira. Hata hivyo, faida za maendeleo endelevu pia zinaweza kufaidisha kote katika afya na ustawi wa binadamu, pamoja na kupunguza magonjwa zinazohusiana na mazingira, uboreshaji wa matokeo ya afya na kupunguza mvutano. Huku hatari za mabadiliko ya ikolojia zinazoongezeka, majaribio endelevu yanatoa faida nyingi kwa washiriki mbalimbali. Kwa mfano, operesheni endelevu za afya zinaweza kuleta faida kwa wagonjwa na wafanyikazi, na pia kuokoa nishati, kupunguza hatari za ikolojia na kuunda malipo endelevu. Shirika la Afya Duniani limejumuisha lengo la kuendeleza maendeleo endelevu katika kazi zake zote ili kusaidia kulinda watu wa kesho kutoka kwa hatari za afya zinazoongezeka leo. Athari: Stratيجيا nyingi za maendeleo endelevu zinaweza kutoa faida kubwa kwa afya, ikolojia na mazingira, na faida hizi zinaweza kuonekana mara moja. Kwa mfano, malengo mengi ya afya na maendeleo yanaweza kufanikishwa kwa wakati mmoja kwa kutuliza mchaka, ambao una athari milioni 7 za vifo mapema kila mwaka. Michakato ya mchaka ni pamoja na mchaka mwenye nguvu na mfupi (SLCPs)—hasa, kaboni nyeusi, ozoni na methane—ambayo ni hatari sana kwa afya lakini zina maisha fupi sana katika anga.

translations.source: Transport policies and investments that favour clean public transport, along with walking and cycling, can have a dramatic impact on air quality. This can reduce multiple health risks at the same time, including stroke, heart attack, lung disease and some cancers. They can also reduce the estimated 1.25 million deaths annually from traffic injury, while also improving access to health services, particularly in densely populated areas. Sustainable development housing policies can further reduce emissions through considerations such as building siting and land use, choices of construction materials, design features and ventilation and energy. WHO response: WHO’s work is guided by the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3: “Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.” The Goals inform projects and policies across the Organization, including guidelines on housing, urban development, transportation systems and health care delivery. For example, the WHO Housing and health guidelines, first published in 2018, highlight the increasing impact of housing conditions on human health in light of urban growth, climate and demographic changes. The guidelines provide Member States, partners and the public with evidence-based recommendations relevant to inadequate living space (crowding), low and high indoor temperatures, injury hazards in the home, and accessibility of housing for people with functional impairments. The Urban Health Initiative (UHI) also focuses on ways for cities to enable good health by catalysing effective action on urban air pollution and short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—saving lives by linking health, environment and sustainable development. WHO also collaborates with the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) on the Breathe Life campaign, which aims to cut air pollution and reduce the 7 million premature deaths annually that it causes.
translations.reference: Sera za usafiri na uwekezaji unaopendelea usafiri safi wa umma, pamoja na kutembea na kuendesha baiskeli, zinaweza kuwa na athari kubwa katika ubora wa hewa. Hii inaweza kupunguza hatari nyingi za kiafya kwa wakati mmoja, pamoja na kiharusi, mshtuko wa moyo, ugonjwa wa mapafu na saratani kadhaa. Wanaweza pia kupunguza makadirio ya vifo milioni 1.25 kila mwaka kutokana na majeraha ya barabarani, huku pia wakiboresha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya, hasa katika maeneo yenye watu wengi. Sera za maendeleo endelevu za makazi zinaweza kupunguza zaidi uzalishaji wa hewa chafu kupitia mambo ya kuzingatia kama vile eneo la majengo na matumizi ya ardhi, uchaguzi wa vifaa vya ujenzi, vipengele vya muundo na uingizaji hewa na nishati. Jibu la WHO: Kazi ya WHO inaongozwa na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu, hasa Lengo la 3: "Hakikisha maisha yenye afya na kukuza ustawi kwa wote katika umri wote." Malengo yanaarifu miradi na sera kote katika Shirika, ikijumuisha miongozo ya makazi, maendeleo ya mijini, mifumo ya usafirishaji na utoaji wa huduma za afya. Kwa mfano, miongozo ya Makazi na afya ya WHO, iliyochapishwa kwa mara ya kwanza mwaka wa 2018, inaangazia ongezeko la athari za hali ya makazi kwa afya ya binadamu kwa kuzingatia ukuaji wa miji, hali ya hewa na mabadiliko ya idadi ya watu. Mwongozo huo unazipa Nchi Wanachama, washirika na umma mapendekezo yenye msingi wa ushahidi yanayohusiana na uhaba wa nafasi ya kuishi (msongamano), halijoto ya chini na ya juu ndani ya nyumba, hatari za majeraha nyumbani, na upatikanaji wa nyumba kwa watu walio na matatizo ya utendaji. Mpango wa Afya Mijini (UHI) pia unaangazia njia za miji kuwezesha afya njema kwa kuchochea hatua madhubuti dhidi ya uchafuzi wa hewa mijini na vichafuzi vya hali ya hewa ya muda mfupi (SLCPs)—kuokoa maisha kwa kuunganisha afya, mazingira na maendeleo endelevu. WHO pia inashirikiana na Muungano wa Hali ya Hewa na Safi (CCAC) kwenye kampeni ya Breathe Life, ambayo inalenga kupunguza uchafuzi wa hewa na kupunguza vifo vya mapema milioni 7 kila mwaka ambavyo husababisha.
translations.hypothesis: Sera na matumizi za rasilimali ambazo unaendeleza usafiri wa umma safi, pamoja na mambo ya kutembea na kukaa, inaweza kuwa na athari kubwa kwenye ubora wa hewa. Hii inaweza kupunguza hatari nyingi za afya wakati huo huo, pamoja na mashambulizi, magonjwa ya moyo, magonjwa ya pneuma, na baadhi ya saratani. Pia inaweza kupunguza idadi takribani ya 1.25 milioni ya vifo kila mwaka kutokana na majeraha ya usafiri, huku ikiimarisha ufikiaji wa huduma za afya, hasa katika maeneo yenye watu wengi. Sera za makazi za maendeleo endelevu zinaweza kupunguza zaidi viwango vya mchomo kupitia vigezo kama vile eneo la ujenzi, matumizi ya ardhi, aina za vifaa vya ujenzi, vipengele vya muundo, na uingizaji hewa na nishati. Majibu ya WHO: Kazi ya WHO inaongozwa na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu, hasa Lengo 3: "Kuandaa maisha salama na kukuza ustawi kwa wote katika umri wote." Malengo haya yanaelekeza miradi na sera katika Kituo, pamoja na miongozo kuhusu makazi, maendeleo ya miji, mifumo ya usafiri, na utoaji wa huduma za afya. Kwa mfano, miongozo ya WHO kuhusu makazi na afya, ambayo ilitolewa kwa mara ya kwanza mwaka 2018, zinaangazia athari inayoongezeka ya hali za makazi kwenye afya ya binadamu, ikiambatana na ukuaji wa miji, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, na mabadiliko ya idadi ya watu. Miongozo hutoa mapendekezo inayotegemeka kwa mataifa, washiriki, na umma, zinazohusiana na nafasi za makazi zisizofaa (ujambawazi), halijoto za ndani, na hatari za majeraha nyumbani, na ufikiaji wa makazi kwa watu wenye ulemavu. Mradi wa Afya ya Miji (UHI) pia unaangazia njia ambazo miji zinaweza kukuza afya nzuri kwa kupambana na uchafuzi wa hewa katika miji na mbadala za hapa, ambayo yanaathiri hali ya hewa—ukiwashughulikia, na hivyo kuokoa maisha kwa kuunganisha afya, mazingira, na maendeleo endelevu. WHO pia inashiriki katika kampeni ya "Breathe Life" na Mshirika wa Hali ya Hewa na Hea (CCAC), ambayo ina lengo la kupunguza uchafuzi wa hewa na kupunguza vifo milioni 7 kila mwaka ambavyo husababishwa.

translations.source: Syphilis Overview: Syphilis is a bacterial sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by Treponema pallidum which results in substantial morbidity and mortality, and it is curable. Syphilis is transmitted through sexual contact with the infectious lesions, via blood transfusion or from a pregnant woman to her fetus. Mother-to-child transmission of syphilis is usually devastating to the fetus in cases that were not detected or treated sufficiently early in pregnancy. Maternal syphilis, when untreated, treated late or not treated with penicillin, results in adverse birth outcomes (ABOs) estimated in 50−80% of cases, depending on the stage of syphilis. These ABOs are often severe and include stillbirth (most commonly), neonatal death, prematurity, low birth weight, and congenitally infected infants. Syphilis can be prevented through safer sex practices including the correct and consistent use of condoms. Many people with syphilis do not have any symptoms or have only minor symptoms that go unnoticed. Identifying infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes including congenital syphilis. Screening can be done using rapid tests that can provide results in less than 20 minutes, allowing for immediate treatment.
translations.reference: Kaswende Muhtasari: Kaswende ni ugonjwa wa zinaa unaosababishwa na bakteria (STI) unaosababishwa na Treponema pallidum ambayo husababisha magonjwa na vifo vingi, na inatibika. Kaswende huambukizwa kwa njia ya kujamiiana na vidonda vya kuambukiza, kwa kuongezewa damu au kutoka kwa mwanamke mjamzito hadi kwa kijusi chake. Uambukizaji wa kaswende kutoka kwa mama kwenda kwa mtoto huwa mbaya kwa kijusi katika hali ambazo hazikugunduliwa au kutibiwa vya kutosha mapema katika ujauzito. Kaswende ya kina mama, isipotibiwa, kutibiwa kuchelewa au kutotibiwa na penicillin, husababisha matokeo mabaya ya uzazi (ABOs) yanayokadiriwa katika 50-80% ya kesi, kutegemeana na hatua ya kaswende. ABO hizi mara nyingi huwa kali na hujumuisha kuzaliwa mfu (mara nyingi), kifo cha watoto wachanga, kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, uzito wa chini, na watoto wachanga waliozaliwa kuzaliwa. Kaswende inaweza kuzuiwa kwa njia salama za ngono ikijumuisha matumizi sahihi na ya mara kwa mara ya kondomu. Watu wengi wenye kaswende hawana dalili zozote au wana dalili ndogo tu ambazo hazitambuliki. Kutambua maambukizi kupitia vipimo vya maabara na matibabu ya kesi chanya kutazuia maambukizi zaidi na matokeo mabaya ya ujauzito ikiwa ni pamoja na kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Uchunguzi unaweza kufanywa kwa kutumia vipimo vya haraka ambavyo vinaweza kutoa matokeo kwa chini ya dakika 20, kuruhusu matibabu ya haraka.
translations.hypothesis: Muhtasari wa Syphilis: Syphilis ni ugonjwa wa kejeli unaosababishwa na bakteria (STI), ambao unaasababishwa na Treponema pallidum, na unaoleta matokeo makubwa ya magonjwa na vifo. Pia, ugonjwa huu una tiba. Syphilis hutumika kupitia mzozo na vidonda, kupitia upasuaji wa damu, au kutoka kwa mama kwa mtoto wake. Uchungaji wa syphilis kutoka mama kwa mtoto, katika kesi ambapo haujulikwa au hutibwa mapema, huwa hatari kwa mtoto.
