Syphilis kwa mama, ikiwa haitibwa, hutibwa baadaye, au haitibwa na penicillin, inaweza kusababisha matokeo mbaya za kuzaliwa, ambayo inaelezwa kuwa katika 50-80% ya kesi, kulingana na hatua ya syphilis. Matokeo haya yanaweza kuwa hatari na ni pamoja na kuzaliwa bila maisha (kwa kawaida), vifo vya watoto, kuzaliwa mapema, uzazi hafifu, na watoto wenye ugonjwa wa kuzaliwa. Syphilis inaweza kuzuwa kupitia utumiaji salama wa mzozo, pamoja na matumizi sahihi na mara kwa mara ya kondomu. Watu wengi wenye syphilis hawana dalili, au wana dalili ndogo ambazo hazigunduliwa. Kutambua ugonjwa kupitia majaribio katika лаборатория na kutibua kesi ambazo zina matokeo, itasaidia kuzuia usambazaji zaidi na matokeo mbaya za uzazi, pamoja na syphilis ya kuzaliwa. Majaribio yanaweza kufanyika kwa kutumia majaribio haraka ambavyo yanaweza kutoa matokeo ndani ya dakika 20, na hivyo kuruhusu matibabu ya haraka.

translations.source: When untreated, syphilis lasts many years and goes through different stages. Treatment for syphilis is done with injectable penicillin benzathine, with 1 to 3 doses depending on the stage of the disease. Only penicillin can prevent congenital syphilis. Treatment with penicillin has been hampered by periodic and prolonged shortages of benzathine penicillin in numerous countries, including high-, low- and middle-income countries. Symptoms: Many people with syphilis do not present symptoms or do not perceive them. Untreated, syphilis lasts many years and is characterized into different stages. Sexual transmission typically occurs during early stages, i.e., up to 2 years after infection. During the initial phase (primary syphilis), which lasts on average 21 days, a solitary, painless, usually hard and round sore (chancre) appears at the site of inoculation (contact), often in the vagina, penis or anus (but it may also be extra-genital) and may go unnoticed. The chancre heals with or without treatment within 3 to 10 weeks. If untreated, the disease progresses to the secondary stage.
translations.reference: Isipotibiwa, kaswende hudumu miaka mingi na hupitia hatua tofauti. Matibabu ya kaswende hufanywa na penicillin ya sindano ya benzathine, na dozi 1 hadi 3 kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. Penicillin pekee inaweza kuzuia kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Matibabu na penicillin yametatizwa na upungufu wa mara kwa mara na wa muda mrefu wa penicillin ya benzathine katika nchi nyingi, zikiwemo nchi zenye kipato cha juu, cha chini na cha kati. Dalili: Watu wengi wenye kaswende hawaonyeshi dalili au hawazioni. Kaswende ambayo haijatibiwa, hudumu miaka mingi na ina sifa katika hatua tofauti. Maambukizi ya ngono kwa kawaida hutokea katika hatua za mwanzo, yaani, hadi miaka 2 baada ya kuambukizwa. Wakati wa awamu ya awali (kaswende ya msingi), ambayo hudumu kwa wastani wa siku 21, kidonda cha upweke, kisicho na uchungu, kawaida kigumu na cha pande zote (chancre) huonekana kwenye sehemu ya chanjo (mawasiliano), mara nyingi kwenye uke, uume au mkundu (lakini pia inaweza kuwa nje ya sehemu ya siri) na inaweza kwenda bila kutambuliwa. Chancre huponya kwa au bila matibabu ndani ya wiki 3 hadi 10. Ikiwa haijatibiwa, ugonjwa unaendelea hadi hatua ya sekondari.
translations.hypothesis: عندما hali si kutibiwa, kiungo kinadumu kwa miaka mengi na ina hatua tofauti. Matibabu ya kiungo hutumika na penicillin inayotumika ndani, na dozi kutoka 1 hadi 3, kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. Tu penicillin inaweza kuzuia kiungo cha kuzaliwa. Matibabu na penicillin imesumbuliwa na ukosefu wa mara kwa mara na muda mrefu wa penicillin inayotumika ndani katika nchi nyingi, pamoja na nchi za kiuchumi za juu, za chini na za katikati. Athari: Watu wengi wenye kiungo hawataonyesha athari, au hawazidai. Bila matibabu, kiungo kinadumu kwa miaka mengi na ina hatua tofauti. Uenezi wa kijinsia kwa kawaida hutokea katika hatua za awali, yaani, hadi miaka 2 baada ya ugonjwa. Katika hatua ya awali (kiungo cha kwanza), ambayo inadumu wastani siku 21, chombo kimoja, bila maumivu, kwa kawaida thabiti na wa umbo, (chancre) huonekana katika eneo la uingizaji (faragha), mara nyingi katika utumbo, penis au anus (lakini pia inaweza kuwa nje ya viungo), na inaweza kupuuzwa. Chombo hifadhi na au bila matibabu ndani ya wiki 3 hadi 10. Ikiwa hali si kutibiwa, ugonjwa huendelea hadi hatua ya pili.

translations.source: Secondary syphilis is characterized by a rash that can vary widely and mimic other infectious or non-infectious conditions, but characteristically affects the palms and soles. The rash is non-itchy and can be minimal enough to be overlooked. In warm and moist areas of the body, such as the anus and labia, large white or grey raised lesions may develop at the location of the primary chancre (condyloma lata). The symptoms and signs of secondary syphilis spontaneously resolve, even without treatment. Without treatment, the latent stage starts. Latent syphilis presents no clinical symptoms or signs and the treatment is different from the early phase. After years or decades without treatment, neurological and cardiovascular diseases among others may occur. Treatment: Syphilis diagnosis is based on the person’s history, physical examination, laboratory testing and sometimes radiology, as symptoms are not common or noticeable. Identifying asymptomatic infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes, including congenital syphilis. Laboratory tests for syphilis include direct detection of T. pallidum or indirect methods such as serology.
translations.reference: Pili kaswende ina sifa ya upele ambao unaweza kutofautiana sana na kuiga hali zingine za kuambukiza au zisizo za kuambukiza, lakini kitabia huathiri viganja na nyayo. Upele hauwashi na unaweza kuwa mdogo vya kutosha kupuuzwa. Katika sehemu zenye joto na unyevunyevu za mwili, kama vile njia ya haja kubwa na midomo, vidonda vikubwa vyeupe au kijivu vilivyoinuliwa vinaweza kutokea katika eneo la chancre ya msingi (condyloma lata). Dalili na ishara za kaswende ya pili hutatuliwa yenyewe, hata bila matibabu. Bila matibabu, hatua ya latent huanza. Kaswende iliyojificha haitoi dalili zozote za kimatibabu au ishara na matibabu ni tofauti na awamu ya awali. Baada ya miaka au miongo bila matibabu, magonjwa ya neva na ya moyo na mishipa kati ya wengine yanaweza kutokea. Matibabu: Utambuzi wa kaswende unatokana na historia ya mtu, uchunguzi wa kimwili, upimaji wa kimaabara na wakati mwingine radiolojia, kwani dalili si za kawaida au hazionekani. Kutambua maambukizi ya dalili kupitia vipimo vya maabara na matibabu ya kesi chanya kutazuia maambukizi zaidi na matokeo mabaya ya ujauzito, ikiwa ni pamoja na kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Vipimo vya kimaabara vya kaswende ni pamoja na kugundua moja kwa moja T. pallidum au mbinu zisizo za moja kwa moja kama vile seroloji.
translations.hypothesis: Syfilis ya pili inaelezwa na mzio ambao unaweza kuwa tofauti sana na kuvingatia matatizo mengine ya kuambukiza au isiyo kuambukiza, lakini kwa kawaida husababisha vidonda kwenye kucha na kwenye kucha. Mzio huu hauvuti na unaweza kuwa hafifu sana kwa kuwa haonekani. Katika eneo la mwili lenye joto na unyevu, kama vile kwenye anus na kwenye sehemu za kiba, vidonda kubwa nyeupe au nyeusi yanaweza kuunda katika eneo la chancre ya msingi (condyloma lata). Athari na ishara za syfilis ya pili husababisha, hata bila matibabu. Bila matibabu, hatua ya siri huanza. Syfilis ya siri haipatikani ishara au athari za kliniki, na matibabu ni tofauti na hatua ya awali. Baada ya miaka au dekada bila matibabu, magonjwa ya neva na moyo, pamoja na mengine, yanaweza kutokea. Matibabu: Uaguzi wa syfilis una msingi kwenye historia ya mtu, ukaguzi wa mwili, upimishaji wa kimatibabu, na wakati mwingine, upimishaji wa picha, kwani athari hazipatikani au hazionekani. Kutambua magonjwa isiyo na ishara kupitia upimishaji wa kimatibabu na matibabu ya kesi za chanya, itasaidia kuzuia maambukizi zaidi na matokeo hasi ya ujauzito, pamoja na syfilis ya kuzaliwa. Upimishaji wa syfilis una jumuisha utambuzi wa moja kwa moja wa T. pallidum au njia za kirefu kama vile serolojia.

translations.source: Rapid screening and diagnostic tests are also available and can provide results in less than 20 minutes, facilitating immediate treatment initiation. WHO recommends syphilis to be treated with injectable penicillin (benzathine penicillin G) with 1 or 3 doses, depending on the stage of the disease. WHO has detailed treatment guidelines for syphilis, and one specifically for the screening and treatment of syphilis in pregnancy. Congenital syphilis There are currently no diagnostic tests for congenital syphilis. All live or stillborn infants of women with syphilis should be examined for evidence of congenital syphilis. Live-born infants should be examined and tested at birth to define treatment requirement. When indicated, also at monthly intervals for 3 months until the infant’s serology are, and remain, negative, as maternal antibodies can be passively transmitted to the baby during pregnancy.
translations.reference: Uchunguzi wa haraka na vipimo vya uchunguzi pia vinapatikana na vinaweza kutoa matokeo kwa chini ya dakika 20, kuwezesha kuanza kwa matibabu mara moja. WHO inapendekeza kaswende kutibiwa kwa penicillin ya sindano (benzathine penicillin G) kwa dozi 1 au 3, kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. WHO ina miongozo ya kina ya matibabu ya kaswende, na moja mahsusi kwa uchunguzi na matibabu ya kaswende katika ujauzito. Kaswende ya kuzaliwa Kwa sasa hakuna vipimo vya uchunguzi wa kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Watoto wote walio hai au waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa katika wanawake walio na kaswende wanapaswa kuchunguzwa kwa ushahidi wa kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Watoto waliozaliwa hai wanapaswa kuchunguzwa na kupimwa wakati wa kuzaliwa ili kufafanua mahitaji ya matibabu. Inapoonyeshwa, pia kwa vipindi vya kila mwezi kwa miezi 3 hadi serolojia ya mtoto mchanga iwe, na kubaki, hasi, kwani kingamwili za mama zinaweza kupitishwa kwa mtoto wakati wa ujauzito.
translations.hypothesis: Uchunguzi na majaribio ya haraka pia yanaweza kupatikana, na yanaweza kutoa matokeo kwa meno ya 20, na hivyo kuwezesha ugonjwa kuondolewa haraka. WHO inapendekeza kwamba ujambazi unaondolewa kwa kutumia penicillin inayotumika ndani (penicillin ya benzathine G) kwa dozi moja au tatu, kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. WHO ina miongozo kamili kuhusu ugonjwa wa ujambazi, na mojawapo ni maalum kwa uchunguzi na matibabu ya ujambazi wakati wa ujauzito. Ujambazi wa kuzaliwa Hapo sasa hakuna majaribio ya uchunguzi kwa ujambazi wa kuzaliwa. Watoto wote ambao wamezaliwa au ambao wamefariki, wa wanawake walio na ujambazi, lazima uchunguzwe ili kuthibitisha je, kuna ujambazi wa kuzaliwa. Watoto ambao wamezaliwa lazima uchunguzwe na jaribiwe wakati wa kuzaliwa ili kutathmini mahitaji ya matibabu. Wakati inahitajika, pia kwa miongozo ya kila mwezi kwa miezi 3, hadi matokeo ya serolojia ya mtoto yana, na yawe, hasi, kwani vitisho vya mama vinaweza kuhamishwa kwa mtoto wakati wa ujauzito.

translations.source: Taeniasis and cysticercosis Overview: Infection with the tapeworm Taenia solium can result in two distinct conditions: taeniasis and cysticercosis. Taeniasis is the intestinal infection with the tapeworm and it occurs when a person eats raw or undercooked, infected pork. Taeniasis has no major impact on human health. However, tapeworm eggs pass in the faeces of the tapeworm carrier and in the absence of a proper sanitation may contaminate the environment. While tapeworm eggs are infective for pigs, T. solium eggs may also infect humans if they are ingested by a person (via the fecal-oral route, or by ingesting contaminated food or water). Ingestion of tapeworm eggs causes infection with the larval parasite in the tissues such as muscles, skin, eyes and the central nervous system (human cysticercosis), with possible devastating effects on health. When cysts develop in the brain, the condition is referred to as neurocysticercosis. Symptoms: Taeniasis is usually characterized by mild and non-specific symptoms associated to an intestinal infection (abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhoea, etc.). These symptoms may continue until the tapeworm dies following treatment, otherwise it may live for years.
translations.reference: Taeniasis na cysticercosis Muhtasari: Kuambukizwa na minyoo Taenia solium kunaweza kusababisha hali mbili tofauti: taeniasis na cysticercosis. Taeniasis ni maambukizi ya matumbo na minyoo na hutokea wakati mtu anakula nyama ya nguruwe mbichi au ambayo haijaiva vizuri. Taeniasis haina athari kubwa kwa afya ya binadamu. Hata hivyo, mayai ya minyoo hupita kwenye kinyesi cha mtoaji wa minyoo na kukosekana kwa usafi wa mazingira kunaweza kuchafua mazingira. Ingawa mayai ya minyoo yanaambukiza nguruwe, mayai ya T. solium pia yanaweza kumwambukiza binadamu ikiwa yamemezwa na mtu (kupitia njia ya kinyesi-kwa mdomo, au kwa kumeza chakula au maji yaliyochafuliwa). Umezaji wa mayai ya minyoo husababisha kuambukizwa na vimelea vya buu kwenye tishu kama vile misuli, ngozi, macho na mfumo mkuu wa neva (human cysticercosis), pamoja na athari mbaya kwa afya. Wakati uvimbe hutokea kwenye ubongo, hali hiyo inaitwa neurocysticercosis. Dalili: Taeniasis kawaida huwa na dalili za upole na zisizo maalum zinazohusiana na maambukizi ya matumbo (maumivu ya tumbo, kichefuchefu, kuhara, nk). Dalili hizi zinaweza kuendelea hadi mdudu afe baada ya matibabu, vinginevyo anaweza kuishi kwa miaka.
translations.hypothesis: Maelezo kuhusu Taeniasis na Cysticercosis: Ugonjwa wa taenia solium unaweza kusababisha hali mbili tofauti: taeniasis na cysticercosis. Taeniasis ni ugonjwa wa matumbo unaosababishwa na taenia, na hutokea wakati mtu anapowa na nyama iliyo na taenia. Taeniasis haina athari kubwa kwa afya ya binadamu. Hata hivyo, yai za taenia hutoka kwenye matope za mtu aliyepata ugonjwa, na ikiwa hakuna usafi wa kawaida, zinaweza kutenda hatari kwenye mazingira. Ingawa yai za taenia zinaweza kuambukiza nyasi, yai za T. solium pia zinaweza kuambukiza binadamu ikiwa mtu huingiza yai hizo (kwa njia ya matope, au kwa kuingiza chakula au maji iliyo na taenia). Kuingiza yai za taenia husababisha ugonjwa wa paraziti katika tishu, kama vile misuli, ngozi, macho, na mfumo wa neva (cysticercosis kwa binadamu), na inaweza kusababisha athari kubwa kwa afya. Wakati vifaa hufungua kwenye ubongo, hali hiyo inaitwa neurocysticercosis. Ishara: Taeniasis kwa kawaida inaashiria ishara hafifu na zisipatikani zinazohusiana na ugonjwa wa matumbo (paina, ugonjwa, kuharisha, n.k.). Ishara hizi zinaweza kuendelea hadi taenia itakapokufa baada ya matibabu, au inaweza kuishi kwa miaka.

translations.source: For cysticercosis, the incubation period is variable, and infected people may remain asymptomatic for years. In some endemic regions, infected people may develop visible or palpable nodules (a small solid bump or node which is solid that can be detected by touch) beneath the skin. Neurocysticercosis is associated with a variety of signs and symptoms depending on the number, size, stage and location of the pathological changes as well as the host’s immune response and the parasite’s genotype, but it can also be clinically asymptomatic. Symptoms may include chronic headaches, blindness, seizures (epilepsy if they are recurrent), hydrocephalus, meningitis, dementia and symptoms caused by lesions occupying spaces of the central nervous system. Treatment: The treatment of taeniasis by Taenia solium is important to prevent neurocysticercosis and as a tool to assist in controlling or stopping the parasite transmission cycle. The treatment can be done on an individual bases, or as Mass Drug Administration depending on the local circumstances and the control approaches being implemented. Taeniasis can be treated using anthelmintics (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). In neurocysticercosis, since the destruction of cysts may lead to an inflammatory response, specialised treatment of active disease is required and may include long courses with high doses of anthelmintic, as well as supporting therapy with corticosteroids and/or anti-epileptic drugs, and possibly surgery. The dosage and the duration of treatment can vary greatly and depend mainly on the number, size, location and developmental stage of the cysts, their surrounding inflammatory edema, acuteness and severity of clinical symptoms or signs.
translations.reference: Kwa cysticercosis, kipindi cha incubation ni tofauti, na watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kubaki bila dalili kwa miaka. Katika baadhi ya maeneo yenye ugonjwa huo, watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kuendeleza vinundu vinavyoonekana au vinavyoweza kubalika (kitunguu kidogo kigumu au nodi ambayo ni dhabiti ambayo inaweza kutambuliwa kwa kuguswa) chini ya ngozi. Neurocysticercosis inahusishwa na aina mbalimbali za ishara na dalili kulingana na idadi, ukubwa, hatua na eneo la mabadiliko ya pathological pamoja na majibu ya kinga ya mwenyeji na genotype ya vimelea, lakini pia inaweza kuwa kliniki bila dalili. Dalili zinaweza kujumuisha maumivu ya kichwa ya muda mrefu, upofu, kifafa (kifafa ikiwa kinajirudia), hydrocephalus, meningitis, shida ya akili na dalili zinazosababishwa na vidonda vinavyochukua nafasi za mfumo mkuu wa neva. Matibabu: Matibabu ya taeniasis na Taenia solium ni muhimu ili kuzuia neurocysticercosis na kama chombo cha kusaidia katika kudhibiti au kusimamisha mzunguko wa maambukizi ya vimelea. Matibabu yanaweza kufanywa kwa misingi ya mtu binafsi, au kama Utawala wa Misa ya Dawa kulingana na hali ya eneo na mbinu za udhibiti zinazotekelezwa. Taeniasis inaweza kutibiwa kwa kutumia anthelmintics (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). Katika neurocysticercosis, kwa kuwa uharibifu wa cysts unaweza kusababisha majibu ya uchochezi, matibabu maalum ya ugonjwa hai inahitajika na inaweza kujumuisha kozi ndefu na kipimo cha juu cha anthelmintic, pamoja na tiba ya kusaidia na corticosteroids na/au dawa za kuzuia kifafa, na ikiwezekana upasuaji. Muda wa matibabu na kipimo inaweza kutofautiana sana na inategemea hasa idadi, ukubwa, eneo na hatua ya maendeleo ya cysts, edema yao ya uchochezi inayozunguka, ukali na ukali wa dalili za kliniki au ishara.
translations.hypothesis: Kwa ugonjwa wa cysticercosis, kipindi cha kuambukiza ni tofauti, na watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kuendelea bila dalili kwa miaka. Katika maeneo fulani ambapo ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida, watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kuendelea kuona au kugusa nodules (nodule ndogo, thabiti ambayo inaweza kugunduliwa kwa kugusa) chini ya ngozi. Ugonjwa wa neurocysticercosis unaambatana na ishara na dalili mbalimbali, kulingana na idadi, ukubwa, hatua na eneo la mabadiliko ya patholojia, pamoja na majibu ya kinga ya mwili na aina ya protozoa, lakini pia unaweza kuwa bila dalili. Ishara zinaweza kujumuisha maumivu ya mara kwa mara, ugonjwa wa macho, huchi (ukosefu wa udhibiti ikiwa huendelea), hydrocephalus, meningitis, ugonjwa wa akili, na ishara zinazosababishwa na magonjwa ambayo yanauza nafasi za mfumo wa neva. Matibabu: Matibabu ya taeniasis na Taenia solium ni muhimu ili kuzuia ugonjwa wa neurocysticercosis, na kama njia ya kusaidia kudhibiti au kuacha mzunguko wa usambazaji wa protozoa. Matibabu yanaweza kufanyika kwa mtu binafsi, au kama utawala wa dawa kwa umma, kulingana na hali za eneo na njia za udhibiti zinazotumiwa. Taeniasis inaweza kutibuliwa kwa kutumia dawa za kupunguza nyama (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). Katika ugonjwa wa neurocysticercosis, kwani uharibifu wa cysts unaweza kusababisha majibu ya uchochezi, ni muhimu kupata matibabu maalumu ya ugonjwa, ambayo inaweza kujumuisha kozi ndefu na dozi za juu za dawa za kupunguza nyama, pamoja na tiba ya kusaidia na corticosteroids na/au dawa za kupambana na huchi, na labda upasuaji. Dozi na muda wa matibabu yanaweza kutofautiana sana na inategemea hasa idadi, ukubwa, eneo na hatua ya maendeleo ya cysts, uchochezi wa karibu, ukali na umbile wa ishara au dalili za kliniki.

translations.source: Tetanus Overview: Tetanus is a serious illness contracted through exposure to the spores of the bacterium, Clostridium tetani, which live in soil, saliva, dust and manure. The bacteria can enter the body through a deep cuts, wounds or burns affecting the nervous system. The infection leads to painful muscle contractions, particularly of the jaw and neck muscle, and is commonly known as “lockjaw”. People of all ages can get tetanus but the disease is particularly common and serious in newborn babies and their mothers when the mother is unprotected from tetanus by the vaccine, tetanus toxoid. Tetanus occurring during pregnancy or within 6 weeks of the end of pregnancy is called maternal tetanus, while tetanus occurring within the first 28 days of life is called neonatal tetanus. The disease remains an important public health problem in many parts of the world, but especially in low-income countries or districts, where immunization coverage is low and unclean birth practices are common. WHO estimates that in 2018 (the latest year for which estimates are available), 25 000 newborns died from neonatal tetanus, 88% reduction from the situation in 2000. Symptoms: Nearly all cases of tetanus occur in people who have never been vaccinated or in adults who have not kept up to date on their booster shots. Signs and symptoms of tetanus appear anytime from 3 to 21 days after tetanus bacteria enter the body through a wound.
translations.reference: Pepopunda Muhtasari: Pepopunda ni ugonjwa hatari unaoambukizwa kwa kuambukizwa na vijidudu vya bakteria, Clostridium tetani, wanaoishi kwenye udongo, mate, vumbi na samadi. Bakteria wanaweza kuingia ndani ya mwili kupitia majeraha ya kina, majeraha au kuchomwa na kuathiri mfumo wa neva. Maambukizi husababisha mikazo ya misuli yenye uchungu, haswa ya misuli ya taya na shingo, na inajulikana kama "lockjaw". Watu wa rika zote wanaweza kupata pepopunda lakini ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida na mbaya zaidi kwa watoto wachanga na mama zao wakati mama hana kinga dhidi ya pepopunda kwa chanjo, pepopunda toxoid. Pepopunda inayotokea wakati wa ujauzito au ndani ya wiki 6 za mwisho wa ujauzito huitwa pepopunda ya mama, wakati pepopunda ikitokea ndani ya siku 28 za kwanza za maisha inaitwa pepopunda changa. Ugonjwa huu unasalia kuwa tatizo muhimu la afya ya umma katika sehemu nyingi za dunia, lakini hasa katika nchi za kipato cha chini au wilaya, ambapo chanjo ni ya chini na njia chafu za uzazi ni za kawaida. WHO inakadiria kuwa katika 2018 (mwaka wa hivi karibuni ambao makadirio yanapatikana), watoto wachanga 25,000 walikufa kutokana na pepopunda ya watoto wachanga, 88% walipungua kutoka hali hiyo mnamo 2000. Dalili: Takriban visa vyote vya pepopunda hutokea kwa watu ambao hawajawahi kupata chanjo au kwa watu wazima ambao hawajasasisha picha zao za nyongeza. Ishara na dalili za pepopunda huonekana wakati wowote kutoka siku 3 hadi 21 baada ya bakteria ya pepopunda kuingia mwilini kupitia kidonda.
translations.hypothesis: Muhtasari wa Tetano: Tetano ni ugonjwa mgumu ambao hutokea kupitia mfiduo kwa spore za bakteria, Clostridium tetani, ambazo hujitokeza kwenye udongo, kinywa, vumbi na mboji. Bakteria zinaweza kuingia kwenye mwili kupitia majeraha, majeraha au kuchoma ambazo zinaathiri mfumo wa neva. Ugonjwa huu unapelekea majeraha ya misuli, hasa misuli ya kafa na nyuma, na mara nyingi huitwa "kugeuza kafa". Watu wa umri wowote wanaweza kupata tetano, lakini ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida na mgumu, hasa kwa watoto wachanga na wao, wakati mama hajasimamishwa na chanjo ya tetano, tetanus toxoid. Tetano ambayo hutokea wakati wa ujauzito au ndani ya miezi 6 baada ya mwisho wa ujauzito, huitwa tetano la mama, wakati tetano ambayo hutokea ndani ya siku 28 za kwanza za maisha, huitwa tetano la mtoto. Ugonjwa huu bado ni tatizo muhimu katika afya ya umma katika sehemu nyingi za dunia, lakini hasa katika nchi au mitaa ambazo zina kipato cha chini, ambapo usimamizi ni mdogo na tabia za kuzaliwa ambazo hazina usafi ni za kawaida. WHO ina makadirio kwamba mwaka wa 2018 (sana zaidi ambao makadirio yanaweza kupatikana), watoto 25,000 walifariki kutokana na tetano la mtoto, kupungua kwa 88% kutoka kwa hali mwaka wa 2000. Athari: Kisa cha tetano kinatokea kwa karibu kesi zote katika watu ambao hawawezi, au watu wazima ambao hawawezi kupata chanjo za upimaji. Ishara na athari za tetano zinaonekana wakati wowote kuanzia siku 3 hadi 21 baada ya bakteria za tetano kuingia kwenye mwili kupitia jeraha.

translations.source: Most cases occur within 14 days. Symptoms can include: jaw cramping or the inability to open the mouth muscle spasms often in the back, abdomen and extremities sudden painful muscle spasms often triggered by sudden noises trouble swallowing seizures headache fever and sweating changes in blood pressure or fast heart rate.
translations.reference: Kesi nyingi hutokea ndani ya siku 14. Dalili zinaweza kujumuisha: kuuma kwa taya au kutoweza kufungua mdomo misuli ya spasms mara nyingi nyuma, ya tumbo na mwisho mshtuko wa ghafla wa misuli yenye uchungu mara nyingi huchochewa na kelele za ghafla shida kumeza mishtuko wa moyo maumivu ya kichwa homa na jasho mabadiliko katika shinikizo la damu au kasi ya moyo.
translations.hypothesis: Kutokana na hali nyingi, magonjwa yanaanza ndani ya siku 14. Athari zinaweza kujumuisha: kupigwa mara kwa mara kwenye mkono, au kutokuweza kufungua kinywa, majeraha, mara nyingi kwenye nyuma, tumbo na sehemu za mwili, majeraha ya tiba, mara nyingi yanayosababishwa na sauti za ghafla, ugumu katika kunywa, majeraha, maumivu, joto, na kuyeyuka. Mabadiliko katika shinikizo la damu au kasi ya moyo.

translations.source: In neonatal tetanus, symptoms include muscle spasms, which are often preceded by the newborn’s inability to suck or breastfeed, and excessive crying. Tetanus is diagnosed on the basis of clinical features and does not require laboratory confirmation. Tetanus requires treatment in a medical facility, often in a referral hospital. People who recover from tetanus do not have natural immunity and can be infected again and therefore need to be immunized. Prevention: Tetanus can be prevented through immunization with tetanus-toxoid-containing vaccines (TTCV), which are included in routine immunization programmes globally and administered during antenatal care contacts. To be protected throughout life, WHO recommends that an individual receives 6 doses (3 primary plus 3 booster doses) of TTCV. The 3-dose primary series should begin as early as 6 weeks of age, with subsequent doses given with a minimum interval of 4 weeks between doses. The 3 booster doses should preferably be given during the second year of life (12–23 months), at 4–7 years of age, and at 9–15 years of age. Ideally, there should be at least 4 years between booster doses. There are many kinds of vaccines used to protect against tetanus: diphtheria and tetanus (DT) vaccinesdiphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis (whooping cough) (DTaP) vaccinestetanus and diphtheria (Td) vaccinestetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis (Tdap) vaccines.
translations.reference: Katika tetanasi ya watoto wachanga, dalili ni pamoja na mshtuko wa misuli, ambayo mara nyingi hutanguliwa na kutoweza kwa mtoto mchanga kunyonya au kunyonyesha, na kulia sana. Tetanasi hugunduliwa kwa misingi ya vipengele vya kliniki na hauhitaji uthibitisho wa maabara. Pepopunda inahitaji matibabu katika kituo cha matibabu, mara nyingi katika hospitali ya rufaa. Watu wanaopona pepopunda hawana kinga ya asili na wanaweza kuambukizwa tena na hivyo wanahitaji kuchanjwa. Kinga: Pepopunda inaweza kuzuiwa kwa chanjo zenye pepopunda-toxoid (TTCV), ambazo zimejumuishwa katika programu za kawaida za chanjo ulimwenguni kote na kusimamiwa wakati wa mawasiliano ya utunzaji wa ujauzito. Ili kulindwa katika maisha yote, WHO inapendekeza kwamba mtu binafsi apokee dozi 6 (3 za msingi pamoja na dozi 3 za nyongeza) za TTCV. Mfululizo wa msingi wa dozi 3 unapaswa kuanza mapema kama wiki 6 za umri, na dozi zinazofuata zipewe na muda wa chini wa wiki 4 kati ya dozi. Dozi 3 za nyongeza zinafaa kutolewa katika mwaka wa pili wa maisha (miezi 12-23), katika umri wa miaka 4-7, na katika umri wa miaka 9-15. Kimsingi, kunapaswa kuwa na angalau miaka 4 kati ya dozi za nyongeza. Kuna aina nyingi za chanjo zinazotumiwa kulinda dhidi ya pepopunda: chanjo ya diphtheria na pepopunda (DT) chanjo ya diphtheria, pepopunda na kifaduro (DTaP) chanjo ya chanjo ya dondakoo na diphtheria (Td) chanjo ya diphtheria, diphtheria na pertussis (Tdap).
translations.hypothesis: Katika tetanus ya watoto, dalili ni pamoja na majeraha, ambazo mara nyingi huwa na utata, ambapo mtoto hawezi kununua au kununulia, na kuweka. Tetanus huagizwa kwa kuzingatia dalili za kliniki, na si lazima kuthibitishwa katika лаборатория. Tetanus inahitaji matibabu katika hospitali, mara nyingi katika hospitali inayotumika. Watu ambao wamepona kutoka kwa tetanus hawana kinga asilia, na wanaweza kuambukizwa tena, kwa hivyo wanahitaji kupata chanjo. Mipingo: Tetanus inaweza kuzuwa kupitia chanjo, ambazo zina TTCV (vacines zinazoleta toxin ya tetanus), ambazo ni sehemu ya programu za chanjo za kawaida duniani, na zinatozwa wakati wa huduma za ujauzito. Ili kulinda kwa muda wote, WHO inapendekeza kwamba mtu apoke chakula cha 6 (3 chanjo za kwanza pamoja na 3 chanjo za ziada) za TTCV. Chanjo za kwanza za 3 zinapaswa kuanza mapema, kuanzia umri wa miezi 6, na chanjo za ziada zinapaswa kutolewa na kitengo cha miezi 4 kati ya chanjo. Chanjo za ziada za 3 zinapaswa kutozwa hasa katika mwaka wa pili wa maisha (12–23 miezi), umri wa miaka 4–7, na umri wa miaka 9–15. Kwa kweli, lazima iwe miezi 4 au zaidi kati ya chanjo za ziada. Kuna aina nyingi za chanjo zinazotumika kulinda dhidi ya tetanus: chanjo za diphteria na tetanus (DT), chanjo za diphteria, tetanus, na pertussis (kugeuka) (DTaP), chanjo za tetanus na diphteria (Td), na chanjo za tetanus, diphteria, na pertussis (Tdap).

translations.source: Neonatal tetanus can be prevented by immunizing women of reproductive age with TTCV, either during pregnancy or outside of pregnancy. Additionally, robust medical practices can also prevent tetanus disease including clean delivery and cord care during childbirth, and proper wound care for surgical and dental procedures.
translations.reference: Pepopunda ya watoto wachanga inaweza kuzuiwa kwa kuwapa chanjo ya TTCV wanawake walio katika umri wa kuzaa, wakati wa ujauzito au nje ya ujauzito. Zaidi ya hayo, mbinu thabiti za matibabu zinaweza pia kuzuia ugonjwa wa pepopunda ikiwa ni pamoja na kujifungua safi na utunzaji wa kamba wakati wa kuzaa, na utunzaji sahihi wa jeraha kwa upasuaji na meno.
translations.hypothesis: Tetano katika hatua ya awali ya maisha inaweza kuzuwa kwa kuwapa wanawake walio na uwezo wa kuzaliwa chanjo ya TTCV, au wakati wa ujauzito au nje ya ujauzito. Pia, mbinu bora za matibabu zinaweza pia kuzuia ugonjwa wa tetano, pamoja na uzazi salama na utunzaji wa kamba wakati wa kuzaliwa, na utunzaji sahihi wa majeraha kwa upimishaji na tiba za meno.

translations.source: Tick-borne encephalitis Overview: Tick-borne encephalitis (TBE) is an important cause of viral infections of the central nervous system in eastern, central, northern and increasingly western European countries, and in northern China, Mongolia, and the Russian Federation. Tick-borne encephalitis virus is a member of the family Flaviviridae. Approximately 10 000–12 000 clinical cases of tick-borne encephalitis are reported each year, but this figure is believed to be significantly lower than the actual total number of clinical cases. The vast majority of infections with the virus result from infected ticks, which often remain firmly attached to the skin for days. On rare occasions, infection can result from consumption of unpasteurized milk from infect goats, sheep or cows. People come in contact with the ticks during outdoor activities in forested areas up to an altitude of about 2000 meters. There is no direct person-to-person transmission. Symptoms: Most infections remain asymptomatic. In case of clinical illness, the incubation period for tick-borne encephalitis lasts 2–28 days (most commonly 7–14 days) and is followed by 1–8 days of general cold symptoms, such as fatigue, headache and general malaise, usually combined with fever of ≥38 °C.
translations.reference: Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoenezwa na Jibu Muhtasari: Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoenezwa na Jibu (TBE) ni sababu muhimu ya maambukizo ya virusi ya mfumo mkuu wa neva katika nchi za mashariki, kati, kaskazini na inazidi kuongezeka magharibi mwa Ulaya, na kaskazini mwa Uchina, Mongolia, na Shirikisho la Urusi. Virusi vya encephalitis vinavyoenezwa na Jibu ni mwanachama wa familia ya Flaviviridae. Takriban visa 10 000-12 000 vya ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoenezwa na kupe huripotiwa kila mwaka, lakini takwimu hii inaaminika kuwa chini sana kuliko idadi halisi ya kesi za kimatibabu. Idadi kubwa ya maambukizo na virusi hutokana na kupe walioambukizwa, ambao mara nyingi hubakia kushikamana na ngozi kwa siku. Katika matukio machache, maambukizi yanaweza kutokana na unywaji wa maziwa ambayo hayajasafishwa kutoka kwa mbuzi, kondoo au ng'ombe. Watu hugusana na kupe wakati wa shughuli za nje katika maeneo ya misitu hadi mwinuko wa mita 2000. Hakuna maambukizi ya moja kwa moja kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu. Dalili: Maambukizi mengi hubaki bila dalili. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa wa kliniki, kipindi cha incubation cha encephalitis inayoenezwa na kupe huchukua siku 2-28 (mara nyingi siku 7-14) na hufuatiwa na siku 1-8 za dalili za jumla za baridi, kama vile uchovu, maumivu ya kichwa na malaise ya jumla kawaida hujumuishwa na homa ya ≥38 °C.
translations.hypothesis: Muhtasari kuhusu encephalitis inayowashtaki panya: Encephalitis inayowashtaki panya (TBE) ni sababu muhimu ya magonjwa ya virusi kwenye mfumo wa neva katika nchi za Mashariki, kati, kaskazini, na pia katika nchi za Magharibi, pamoja na nchi za Kaskazini mwa China, Mongolia, na Jamhuri ya Russia. Virusi vya encephalitis inayowashtaki panya ni sehemu ya familia ya Flaviviridae. Kila mwaka, kuna takriban kesi 10,000–12,000 za encephalitis inayowashtaki panya, lakini idadi hii inaaminiwa kuwa ni chini sana kuliko idadi halisi ya kesi. Viongozi wengi wa magonjwa vinatokana na panya zilizowekwa virusi, ambazo mara nyingi huwa imepangwa vizuri kwenye ngozi kwa siku. Katika matukio nadra, ugonjwa unaweza kutokana na matumizi ya maziwa ambayo hayajafanyiwa utulivu kutoka kwa ng'ombe, mbuzi, au ng'ombe. Watu hufanya mawasiliano na panya wakati wa shughuli za nje katika maeneo yenye miti hadi kimo cha takriban 2000 m. Hakuna uenezi moja kwa moja. Athari: Viongozi wengi hawana athari. Katika kesi za magonjwa, kipindi cha matarajio cha encephalitis inayowashtaki panya ni siku 2–28 (mara nyingi siku 7–14), na kufuatia kwa siku 1–8 za athari za kawaida, kama vile uchovu, maumivu ya kichwa, na hisia ya dhiki, mara nyingi pamoja na joto la ≥38 °C.

translations.source: After an asymptomatic interval of 1–20 days up to 15% of patients experience a second phase of the disease characterized by fever frequently exceeding 40 °C and signs of central nervous system involvement, such as meningitis (e.g. fever, headache, and a stiff neck), encephalitis (e.g., drowsiness, confusion, and sensory disturbances), myelitis or radiculitis. Encephalitis developing during this second phase may result in paralysis, permanent sequelae or death. About 1% of cases with neurological pathologies may die; higher fatality rates have been reported from the Russian federation, which may be related to a different virus subtype. Severity of illness increases with age of the patient, but fatalities have been reported from all age groups. There is no specific treatment for tick-borne encephalitis. Prevention: People can protect themselves from ticks by wearing appropriate clothing, including long trousers and closed footwear, when hiking or camping in countries or areas at risk. The whole body should be inspected daily and attached ticks removed as soon as possible. The consumption of unpasteurized dairy products should also be avoided in those areas. Immunization offers the most effective protection. Currently, there are 4 widely used vaccines of assured quality:
translations.reference: Baada ya muda usio na dalili wa siku 1-20 hadi 15% ya wagonjwa hupata awamu ya pili ya ugonjwa huo na homa inayozidi 40 ° C mara kwa mara na ishara za kuhusika kwa mfumo mkuu wa neva, kama vile meninjitisi (k.m. homa, maumivu ya kichwa na kukakamaa), encephalitis (kwa mfano, kusinzia, kuchanganyikiwa, na usumbufu wa hisia), myelitis au radiculitis. Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoendelea katika awamu hii ya pili unaweza kusababisha kupooza, matokeo ya kudumu au kifo. Karibu 1% ya kesi zilizo na patholojia za neva zinaweza kufa; viwango vya juu vya vifo vimeripotiwa kutoka kwa shirikisho la Urusi, ambalo linaweza kuhusishwa na aina tofauti ya virusi. Ukali wa ugonjwa huongezeka na umri wa mgonjwa, lakini vifo vimeripotiwa kutoka kwa vikundi vyote vya umri. Hakuna matibabu maalum kwa ugojwa tick-borne encephalitis. Kinga: Watu wanaweza kujilinda dhidi ya kupe kwa kuvaa mavazi yanayofaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na suruali ndefu na viatu vilivyofungwa, wanapopanda au kupiga kambi katika nchi au maeneo yaliyo hatarini. Mwili mzima unapaswa kuchunguzwa kila siku na kupe zilizounganishwa kuondolewa haraka iwezekanavyo. Matumizi ya bidhaa za maziwa ambazo hazijasafishwa pia zinapaswa kuepukwa katika maeneo hayo. Kinga hutoa ulinzi bora zaidi. Hivi sasa, kuna chanjo 4 zinazotumiwa sana za ubora uliohakikishwa:
translations.hypothesis: Baada ya kipindi cha 1–20 siku bila dalili, hadi 15% ya wagonjwa wanatishia hatua nyingine ya ugonjwa, inayoelekezwa na joto, ambapo joto mara nyingi linaweza kuwa zaidi ya 40 °C, na dalili za uingiliaji wa mfumo wa ubongo, kama vile meningitis (kwa mfano, joto, maumivu ya kichwa, na nyuma iliyo ngumu), encephalitis (kwa mfano, ugonjwa, usawa, na matatizo ya hisia), myelitis au radiculitis. Encephalitis inayotokea katika hatua hii inayoweza kusababisha ugonjwa, matokeo endelevu, au kifo. Kwenye kesi takribani 1%, wagonjwa wenye matatizo ya neva wanaweza kufariki; kasi ya kifo imeonekana katika jamii ya Urusi, ambayo inaweza kuhusiana na aina tofauti ya virusi. Athari ya ugonjwa inazidi na umri wa mgonjwa, lakini kifo zimeelezwa katika kila umri. Hakuna matibabu maalum kwa encephalitis inayotokana na nyama. Mipango: Watu wanaweza kujilinda dhidi ya nyama kwa kuvaa mavuli, pamoja na nguo za pana na vifaa vya kupiga, wakati wa kwenda kwenye mbuga au kampeni katika nchi au maeneo ambapo kuna hatari. Zaidi ya mwili lazima husafishwe kila siku, na nyama zinazopatikana lazima zionolewe haraka iwezekanavyo. Pia, unyenyekevu wa bidhaa za maziwa ambazo hazijatibuliwa lazima kuepukwa katika maeneo hayo. Uchunguzi ni njia bora zaidi ya ulinzi. Hivi sasa, kuna chanjo 4 za kawaida ambazo zina ubora wa uhakika:

translations.source: FSME-Immun and Encepur, manufactured in Austria and Germany respectively, and based on European strains of the virus; and TBE-Moscow and EnceVir, manufactured in the Russian Federation and based on Far-Eastern strains. The 4 vaccines are considered to be safe and effective. In areas where the disease is highly endemic, WHO recommends that vaccination be offered to all age groups, including children. Ticks also transmit Borreliosis (Lyme disease), which is a bacterial infection. TBE vaccination is not effective against this disease, which however is treatable with antimicrobials.
translations.reference: FSME-Immun na Encepur, iliyotengenezwa nchini Austria na Ujerumani ipasavyo, na kulingana na aina za virusi za Ulaya ; na TBE-Moscow na EnceVir, iliyotengenezwa katika Shirikisho la Urusi na kwa kulingana na matatizo ya Mashariki ya Mbali. Chanjo 4 zinachukuliwa kuwa salama na zenye ufanisi. Katika maeneo ambayo ugonjwa huo umeenea sana, WHO inapendekeza kwamba chanjo itolewe kwa vikundi vyote vya umri, pamoja na watoto. Kupe pia huambukiza Borreliosis (ugonjwa wa Lyme), ambao ni maambukizi ya bakteria. Chanjo ya TBE haifai dhidi ya ugonjwa huu, ambao hata hivyo unatibika kwa dawa za kuua viini.
translations.hypothesis: FSME-Immun na Encepur, ambazo zinazalishwa Austria na Ujerumani, kwa mtazamo wa aina za virusi za Ulaya; na TBE-Moscow na EnceVir, ambazo zinazalishwa katika Jamhuri ya Urusi, kwa mtazamo wa aina za virusi za Mashariki mwa Ulaya. Wageni hao nne inaelezwa kuwa salama na na ufanisi. Katika maeneo ambapo ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida sana, WHO inapendekeza kwamba wagonjwa wote, pamoja na watoto, wapate chanjo. Pia, nyadi zinaweza kueneza magonjwa ya Borreliosis (ugonjwa wa Lyme), ambao ni magonjwa ya bakteria. Chanjo ya TBE si na ufanisi dhidi ya ugonjwa huu, lakini inaweza kutibwa kwa matibabu ya bakteria.

translations.source: Tobacco Overview: Nicotine contained in tobacco is highly addictive and tobacco use is a major risk factor for cardiovascular and respiratory diseases, over 20 different types or subtypes of cancer, and many other debilitating health conditions. Every year, more than 8 million people die from tobacco use. Most tobacco-related deaths occur in low- and middle-income countries, which are often targets of intensive tobacco industry interference and marketing. Tobacco can also be deadly for non-smokers. Second-hand smoke exposure has also been implicated in adverse health outcomes, causing 1.2 million deaths annually. Nearly half of all children breathe air polluted by tobacco smoke and 65 000 children die each year due to illnesses related to second-hand smoke. Smoking while pregnant can lead to several life-long health conditions for babies. Heated tobacco products (HTPs) contain tobacco and expose users to toxic emissions, many of which cause cancer and are harmful to health. Electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS) and electronic non-nicotine delivery systems (ENNDS), commonly known as e-cigarettes, do not contain tobacco and may or may not contain nicotine, but are harmful to health and undoubtedly unsafe.
translations.reference: Tumbaku Muhtasari: Nikotini iliyo katika tumbaku inalevya sana na utumiaji wa tumbaku ni sababu kuu ya hatari kwa magonjwa ya moyo na mishipa na ya kupumua, zaidi ya aina 20 tofauti za saratani, na hali zingine nyingi za kiafya zinazodhoofisha. Kila mwaka, zaidi ya watu milioni 8 hufa kutokana na matumizi ya tumbaku. Vifo vingi vinavyohusiana na tumbaku hutokea katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati, ambazo mara nyingi hulengwa na kuingiliwa kwa sekta ya tumbaku na masoko. Tumbaku pia inaweza kuwa mbaya kwa wasiovuta sigara. Uvutaji wa moshi wa sigara pia umehusishwa katika matokeo mabaya ya kiafya, na kusababisha vifo milioni 1.2 kila mwaka. Takriban nusu ya watoto wote huvuta hewa iliyochafuliwa na moshi wa tumbaku na watoto 65,000 hufa kila mwaka kutokana na magonjwa yanayohusiana na moshi wa sigara. Kuvuta sigara wakati wa ujauzito kunaweza kusababisha hali kadhaa za kiafya kwa watoto. Bidhaa za tumbaku zinazopashwa joto (HTPs) zina tumbaku na huwaweka watumiaji kwenye hewa chafu zenye sumu, nyingi ambazo husababisha saratani na ni hatari kwa afya. Mifumo ya kielektroniki ya utoaji wa nikotini (ENDS) na mifumo ya kielektroniki ya uwasilishaji isiyo ya nikotini (ENNDS), inayojulikana kama sigara za kielektroniki, haina tumbaku na inaweza au isiwe na nikotini, lakini inadhuru kwa afya na bila shaka si salama.
translations.hypothesis: Muhtasari wa sigara: Nikotini iliyomo katika sigara ni ya kusumbua sana, na matumizi ya sigara ni sababu kubwa ya magonjwa ya moyo na maradhi, pamoja na aina zaidi ya 20 za saratani, na magonjwa mengine hatari. Kila mwaka, zaidi ya watu milioni 8 wafariki kutokana na matumizi ya sigara. Kasi kubwa ya vifo vinavyohusiana na sigara hutokea katika nchi za chini na katikati, ambazo mara nyingi ni lengo la mzozo mkubwa wa tasnia ya sigara na uuzaji. Sigara pia inaweza kuwa hatari kwa watu ambao hawatotuma. Exposure kwa sigara ya pili pia imegawanywa na matokeo hatari, na kusababisha vifo milioni 1.2 kila mwaka. Kiasi kubwa la watoto (kwa takriban nusu) huchukua hewa iliyoangamizwa na sigara, na watoto 65,000 wafariki kila mwaka kutokana na magonjwa yanayohusiana na sigara ya pili. Kutuma sigara wakati wa ujauzito inaweza kusababisha magonjwa kadhaa hatari kwa watoto. Bidhaa za sigara zilizochimbwa (HTPs) zina sigara na huwezesha watumiaji kupata emisyon kali, ambazo nyingi husababisha saratani na hazina afya. Mifumo ya kutoa nikotini ya kidijitali (ENDS) na mifumo ya kutoa nikotini isiyo na nikotini (ENNDS), inayojulikana kama "e-cigarettes", hazina sigara na zinaweza au si zina nikotini, lakini zina hatari kwa afya na kwa hakika hazina salama.

translations.source: However, it is too early to provide a clear answer on the long-term impact of HTPs and/or e-cigarette use. Impact: An estimated 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, 80% of whom are in low- and middle-income countries. Tobacco use contributes to poverty by diverting household spending from basic needs, such as food and shelter, to tobacco. This spending behaviour is difficult to curb because tobacco is so addictive. It also causes premature death and disability of productive age adults in households thus leading to reduced household income and increased healthcare costs. In addition to the detrimental impact of tobacco on health, the total economic cost of smoking (from health expenditures and productivity losses together) are estimated to be around US$ 1.4 trillion per year, equivalent in magnitude to 1.8% of the world's annual gross domestic product (GDP). Almost 40% of this cost occurred in developing countries, highlighting the substantial burden these countries suffer. Tobacco taxes are applied in part to combat this effect and are seen as the most cost-effective way of curbing tobacco use, particularly among youth and low-income populations. A tax increase that increases tobacco prices by 10% decreases tobacco consumption by about 4% in high-income countries and about 5% in low- and middle-income countries. WHO response: The scale of this human and economic tragedy is shocking, but it’s also preventable.
translations.reference: Hata hivyo, ni mapema mno kutoa jibu wazi kuhusu athari za muda mrefu za HTPs na/au matumizi ya sigara za kielektroniki. Athari: Inakadiriwa kuwa watu bilioni 1.3 duniani kote wanatumia bidhaa za tumbaku, 80% kati yao wakiwa katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Utumiaji wa tumbaku huchangia umaskini kwa kubadilisha matumizi ya kaya kutoka kwa mahitaji ya kimsingi, kama vile chakula na malazi, hadi tumbaku. Tabia hii ya matumizi ni ngumu kudhibiti kwa sababu tumbaku ina uraibu sana. Pia husababisha vifo vya mapema na ulemavu wa watu wazima wenye umri wa kuzalisha katika kaya hivyo kusababisha kupungua kwa mapato ya kaya na kuongezeka kwa gharama za huduma za afya. Pamoja na athari mbaya za tumbaku kwa afya, jumla ya gharama za kiuchumi za uvutaji wa sigara (kutokana na matumizi ya afya na upotevu wa tija kwa pamoja) inakadiriwa kuwa karibu dola za Marekani trilioni 1.4 kwa mwaka, sawa na asilimia 1.8 ya pato la taifa kwa mwaka (GDP). Takriban 40% ya gharama hii ilitokea katika nchi zinazoendelea, ikionyesha mzigo mkubwa ambao nchi hizi zinakabiliwa. Ushuru wa tumbaku hutumiwa kwa kiasi ili kukabiliana na athari hii na inaonekana kama njia ya gharama nafuu zaidi ya kuzuia matumizi ya tumbaku, hasa miongoni mwa vijana na watu wa kipato cha chini. Ongezeko la ushuru ambalo linaongeza bei ya tumbaku kwa 10% hupunguza matumizi ya tumbaku kwa takriban 4% katika nchi zenye mapato ya juu na karibu 5% katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati. Jibu la WHO: Kiwango cha janga hili la kibinadamu na kiuchumi ni la kushangaza, lakini pia linaweza kuzuilika.
translations.hypothesis: Hata hivyo, ni mapema sana kutoa majibu dhahiri kuhusu madhara endelevu ya matumizi ya bidhaa za sigara na/au sigara za kielektroniki. Madhara: Wahakika kwamba watu milioni 1.3 duniani kote hutumia bidhaa za sigara, na asilimia 80 wao wanaishi katika nchi za chini na katikati. Matumizi ya sigara yanaunga mkono umaskini kwa kusafirisha matumizi ya kaya kutoka kwenye mahitaji muhimu, kama vile chakula na makazi, kwenda kwenye sigara. Tabia hii ya matumizi ni ngumu kuendela kwa sababu sigara ni ya kusumbua sana. Pia, husababisha vifo mapema na ulemavu kwa watu wa umri wa uzalishaji katika kaya, na hivyo kusababisha kupungua kwa mapato ya kaya na kuongezeka kwa gharama za afya. Mbali na madhara ya sigara kwenye afya, gharama za jumla za kiuchumi za kupiga sigara (kwa jumla ya gharama za afya na upotezaji wa ufanisi) zinatarajiwa kuwa takriban dola za Marekani 1.4 triliuni kwa mwaka, ambayo ni sawa na asilimia 1.8 ya bidhaa ya jumla ya kitaifa ya dunia (GDP). Kitaribuni asilimia 40 ya gharama hii ilitokea katika nchi zinazopanda, na hii inaonyesha mzigo mkubwa ambao nchi hizi zinakumbana nalo. Kodi za sigara hutumika kwa sehemu kupambana na athari hii, na zinaonekana kama njia bora ya kuondoa matumizi ya sigara, hasa kwa vijana na watu wa kaya. Kuongeza kodi ambapo bei za sigara zinaongezeka kwa asilimia 10, inaongeza matumizi ya sigara kwa takriban asilimia 4 katika nchi za kaya na asilimia 5 katika nchi za chini na katikati. Majibu ya WHO: Ukubwa wa uhaba huu wa kimbinadamu na kiuchumi ni wa kushangaza, lakini pia unaweza kuzuwa.

translations.source: Big Tobacco — along with all manufacturers of tobacco products — is fighting to ensure the dangers of their products are concealed, but we are fighting back: In 2003, WHO Member States unanimously adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the only public health treaty under the auspices of WHO. In force since 2005, it has currently 181 Parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO introduced MPOWER, a package of technical measures and resources, each of which corresponds to at least one provision of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER builds the capacity of countries to implement 6 measures to reduce the demand for tobacco products: monitor tobacco use and prevention policies, protect people from tobacco use, offer help to quit tobacco use, warn about the dangers of tobacco, enforce bans on tobacco advertising, promotion and sponsorship and raise taxes on tobacco.
translations.reference: Tumbaku Kubwa - pamoja na watengenezaji wote wa bidhaa za tumbaku - inapigania kuhakikisha hatari za bidhaa zao zinafichwa, lakini tunapambana: Mnamo 2003, Nchi Wanachama wa WHO zilipitisha kwa kauli moja Mkataba wa Mfumo wa WHO wa Kudhibiti Tumbaku (WHO FCTC), mkataba pekee wa afya ya umma chini ya udhamini wa WHO. Imetumika tangu 2005, kwa sasa ina Vyama 181. Ili kusaidia nchi kutekeleza WHO FCTC, WHO ilianzisha MPOWER, kifurushi cha hatua za kiufundi na rasilimali, ambayo kila moja inalingana na angalau kifungu kimoja cha WHO FCTC. MPOWER inajenga uwezo wa nchi kutekeleza hatua 6 za kupunguza mahitaji ya bidhaa za tumbaku: kufuatilia matumizi ya tumbaku na sera za kuzuia, kulinda watu dhidi ya matumizi ya tumbaku, kutoa msaada wa kuacha matumizi ya tumbaku, kuonya juu ya hatari ya tumbaku, kutekeleza marufuku ya utangazaji wa tumbaku kukuza na kufadhili na kuongeza kodi kwenye tumbaku.
translations.hypothesis: Kampuni kubwa za sigara – pamoja na watengenezaji wote wa bidhaa za sigara – wanaendelea na juhudi za kuficha hatari za bidhaa zao, lakini sisi tunawahamasisha: Mnamo 2003, Mataifa ya WHO walipata kwa pamoja Mkataba wa WHO kuhusu Udhibiti wa Sigara (WHO FCTC), ambayo ni mkataba pekee wa afya wa WHO. Mkataba huu, ambao ulianzishwa mnamo 2005, una mataifa 181. Ili kusaidia nchi kutekeleza WHO FCTC, WHO ilianzisha MPOWER, ambayo ni paketi la hatua za kiufundi na rasilimali, ambazo kila moja inaambatana na angalau moja ya matakwa ya WHO FCTC. MPOWER inawezesha nchi kutekeleza hatua 6 za kupunguza matumizi ya bidhaa za sigara:wachungilia sera za matumizi na kupunguza sigara, kulinda watu dhidi ya matumizi ya sigara, kutoa msaada kwa kuacha matumizi ya sigara, kuonya kuhusu hatari za sigara, kutekeleza vizuizi za matangazo, usambazaji na ushiriki wa sigara, na kuwezesha kodi za sigara.

translations.source: Trachoma Overview: Trachoma is a disease of the eye and the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. It is caused by an obligate intracellular bacterium called Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person by direct or indirect transfer of ocular and nasal discharges of infected people; indirect transfer includes carriage on the body of species of flies. Preschool-age children harbour the principal reservoir of infection. Models suggest that an individual requires more than 150 lifetime infections to develop the blinding complications of trachoma. Trachoma is endemic in some of the world’s poorest populations, who live in rural and remote areas and have highly inadequate access to water, sanitation and healthcare. Africa is the most affected continent, but it also has the most widespread control efforts. The disease is also found in Central and South America, Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. As of 2 January 2020, 13 countries had reported achieving elimination goals.
translations.reference: Trakoma Muhtasari: Trakoma ni ugonjwa wa macho na unaongoza kwa kusababisha upofu duniani kote. Inasababishwa na bakteria ya lazima ndani ya seli inayoitwa Chlamydia trachomatis. Maambukizi hupitishwa kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kwa uhamisho wa moja kwa moja au usio wa moja kwa moja wa kutokwa kwa macho na pua ya watu walioambukizwa; uhamisho usio wa moja kwa moja unajumuisha kubeba kwenye mwili wa aina za nzi. Watoto wa umri wa shule ya mapema huhifadhi hifadhi kuu ya maambukizi. Mifano zinaeleza kuwa mtu anahitaji zaidi ya maambukizi 150 kwa maisha yote ili kuendeleza matatizo ya upofu ya trakoma. Trakoma imeenea katika baadhi ya watu maskini zaidi duniani, ambao wanaishi vijijini na maeneo ya mbali na hawana upatikanaji wa kutosha wa maji, usafi wa mazingira na huduma za afya. Afrika ndilo bara lililoathiriwa zaidi, lakini pia lina juhudi kubwa zaidi za kudhibiti. Ugonjwa huo pia hupatikana Amerika ya Kati na Kusini, Asia, Australia, na Mashariki ya Kati. Kufikia 2 Januari 2020, nchi 13 zilikuwa zimeripoti kufikia malengo ya kuondoa.
translations.hypothesis: Maelezo kuhusu Trachoma: Trachoma ni ugonjwa wa macho, na ni sababu kuu ya ugonjwa wa kuona duniani. Ni sababu ya bakteria inayopatikana ndani ya mwili, inayojulikana kama Chlamydia trachomatis. Ugonjwa unaenea kutoka mtu hadi mtu kupitia mtiririko wa macho na pua wa watu walioambukizwa; mtiririko wa moja una ni kuendesha nyuki kwenye mwili. Watoto wa umri wa shule ya msingi ni chanzo kuu la ugonjwa. Tafiti zinaonyesha kwamba mtu anaweza kupata zaidi ya 150 matukio ya ugonjwa katika maisha yake ili kupata matatizo ya kuona yanayosababishwa na Trachoma. Ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida katika idadi kubwa ya watu duniani, ambao wanaishi katika maeneo ya vijijini na farini, na wana upatikanaji mdogo wa maji, usafi na huduma za afya. Afrika ni eneo lililovamiwa zaidi, lakini pia ina juhudi kubwa za kudhibiti ugonjwa. Ugonjwa huu pia unaenea katika Amerika, Asia, Australia, na Mashariki ya Kati. Hadi tarehe 2 Januari 2020, nchi 13 zimeiripoti kuwa zimepata malengo ya kuondoa ugonjwa.

translations.source: In 2019 alone, 92 622 people received surgical treatment for advanced trachoma and 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a serious public health concern, with an estimated US$ 8 billion annual loss in productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with inflammatory changes in the conjunctiva known as “active trachoma”. An individual’s immune system can clear a single episode of infection, but in endemic communities, re-acquisition of the organism occurs frequently. After years of repeated episodes of active trachoma, the inside of the eyelid can become so severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) that it turns inwards and causes the eyelashes to rub against the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), resulting in constant pain and light intolerance; this and other alterations of the eye can lead to scarring of the cornea. Left untreated, this condition leads to the formation of irreversible opacities, with resulting visual impairment or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors including local transmission intensity. In very highly endemic communities, it can occur in childhood, though onset of visual impairment between the ages of 30 and 40 years is more typical. Women are blinded up to four times as often as men, probably due to more frequent contact with infected children. Individuals living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America and the Pacific Islands are at risk.
translations.reference: Katika mwaka wa 2019 pekee, watu 92 622 walipata matibabu ya upasuaji kwa trakoma ya hali ya juu na milioni 95.2 walitibiwa kwa viua vijasumu. Licha ya mafanikio hayo, ugonjwa huu bado ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya jamii, huku kukiwa na wastani wa hasara ya dola za Marekani bilioni 8 kwa mwaka katika uzalishaji kutokana na upofu na ulemavu wa macho. Dalili: Maambukizi yanahusishwa na mabadiliko ya uchochezi katika kiwambo cha sikio inayojulikana kama "trakoma hai". Kinga ya mtu binafsi inaweza kufuta sehemu moja ya maambukizi, lakini katika jumuiya za endemic, kupatikana tena kwa viumbe hutokea mara kwa mara. Baada ya miaka ya matukio ya mara kwa mara ya trakoma hai, ndani ya kope inaweza kuwa na kovu kali sana (kovu la trachomatous conjunctival scarring) kwamba hugeuka ndani na kusababisha kope kusugua kwenye mboni ya jicho (trachomatous trichiasis), na kusababisha maumivu ya mara kwa mara na kutovumilia mwanga; mabadiliko haya na mengine ya jicho yanaweza kusababisha kovu ya konea. Ikiachwa bila kutibiwa, hali hii husababisha kuundwa kwa opacities isiyoweza kutenduliwa, na kusababisha uharibifu wa kuona au upofu. Umri ambao hii hutokea inategemea mambo kadhaa ikiwa ni pamoja na ukubwa wa maambukizi ya ndani. Katika jamii zenye magonjwa mengi, inaweza kutokea utotoni, ingawa mwanzo wa ulemavu wa kuona kati ya umri wa miaka 30 na 40 ni kawaida zaidi. Wanawake hupofushwa hadi mara nne zaidi ya wanaume, labda kutokana na kuwasiliana mara kwa mara na watoto walioambukizwa. Watu wanaoishi katika jamii maskini zaidi na za mbali zaidi za Afrika, Asia, Australia, Mashariki ya Kati, Amerika ya Kati na Kusini na Visiwa vya Pasifiki wako hatarini.
translations.hypothesis: Mnamo mwaka wa 2019 pekee, watu 92,622 walipokea matibabu ya upasuaji kwa matatizo ya jeye, na watu 95.2 milioni walipokea matibabu na dawa za bakteria. Licha ya mafanikio haya, ugonjwa huo bado ni suala muhimu katika afya ya umma, na makadirio ya hasara ya wastani ya $8 bilioni kila mwaka kutokana na ugonjwa na ulemavu wa maono. Athari: Ugonjwa unaambatana na mabadiliko ya uchochezi kwenye conjunctiva, inayojulikana kama "jeye ya kuambukiza". Mfumo wa kinga wa mtu anaweza kupunguza ugonjwa, lakini katika jamii ambazo ugonjwa huo ni wa kawaida, ugonjwa unaweza kuenea tena. Baada ya miaka kadhaa ya matukio ya jeye, sehemu ya ndani ya jicho inaweza kuwa na majeraha makubwa (majeraha ya conjunctiva), ambayo yanaweza kusababisha jicho kuogesha (jeye ya trichiasis), na kusababisha maumivu na ulemavu wa mwanga; na mabadiliko mengine ya jicho yanaweza kusababisha majeraha kwenye kornea. Ikiwa haitibuliwa, hali hii inaweza kusababisha ulemavu wa mwongozo, na ugonjwa wa jicho. Umri ambao hii hutokea hutegemea kadri mambo mengine, pamoja na nguvu ya uenezi wa ugonjwa. Katika jamii ambazo ugonjwa huo ni wa kawaida sana, inaweza kutokea katika utoto, lakini ulemavu wa mwongozo kati ya umri wa miaka 30 na 40 ni zaidi ya kawaida. Wanawake huwa wamelemwa mara kadhaa zaidi kuliko wanaume, labda kwa sababu ya mawasiliano mara kwa mara na watoto waliopata ugonjwa. Watu wanaishi katika jamii za masaa, na mitaa, Afrika, Asia, Australia, Mashariki ya Kati, Amerika ya Mashariki na Kusini, na Visi vya Pasifiki wana hatari.

translations.source: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by limiting environmental risk factors. These include inadequate hygiene, crowded households, inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation. Infections usually begin at an early age and grow less frequent and shorter in duration with increasing age. Infections are most often acquired through family and contact with others with active disease. Repeated infections cause the most serious symptoms of the disease. Trachoma can be eliminated as a public health problem using the SAFE strategy, comprising: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the blinding stage of the disease; Antibiotics to clear infection, particularly mass drug administration of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is donated by the manufacturer to national programmes through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleanliness; and Environmental improvement, particularly better access to water and sanitation.
translations.reference: Matibabu: Trakoma inaweza kuzuiwa kwa kupunguza mambo hatarishi ya kimazingira. Haya ni pamoja na ukosefu wa usafi wa mazingira, kaya zenye msongamano wa watu, ukosefu wa maji na uhaba wa upatikanaji na matumizi ya vyoo. Maambukizi kwa kawaida huanza katika umri mdogo na kukua chini ya mara kwa mara kwa muda mfupi na kuongezeka kwa umri. Maambukizi mara nyingi hupatikana kwa njia ya familia na kuwasiliana na wengine wenye ugonjwa hai. Maambukizi ya kujirudia husababisha dalili mbaya zaidi za ugonjwa huo. Trakoma inaweza kuondolewa kama tatizo la afya ya umma kwa kutumia mkakati SALAMA, unaojumuisha: Upasuaji wa kutibu trichiasis ya trachomatous, hatua ya upofu ya ugonjwa huo; Dawa za antibiotiki za kuondoa maambukizi, hasa usimamizi wa dawa nyingi za antiniotiki azithromycin, ambayo hutolewa na mtengenezaji kwa programu za kitaifa kupitia Mpango wa Kimataifa wa Trakoma; usafi wa uso; na Uboreshaji wa mazingira, hasa upatikanaji bora wa maji na usafi wa mazingira.
translations.hypothesis: Matibabu: Ugonjwa wa Trachoma unaweza kuzuwa kwa kupunguza hatari za mazingira. Hizi ni pamoja na usafi usiofaa, makazi yenye watu wengi, upatikanaji usiofaa wa maji, na upatikanaji na matumizi usiofaa ya huduma za usafi. Ugonjwa mara nyingi huwa na mwanzo mapema na huwa chini zaidi na muda mfupi kwa miaka. Ugonjwa mara nyingi huwa unaendelea kupitia familia na mawasiliano na watu wengine wenye ugonjwa. Ugonjwa wa mara kwa mara unaweza kusababisha dalili kubwa za ugonjwa. Ugonjwa wa Trachoma unaweza kuondolewa kama tatizo la afya ya umma kwa kutumia mkakati wa SAFE, ambao una: Upasuaji wa kutibu Trachomatous trichiasis, hatua inayosababisha ugonjwa; Antibiotics za kuondoa ugonjwa, hasa utawala wa tiba ya antibiotic, azithromycin, ambayo huzawekea na kampuni kwa programu za kitaifa kupitia Initiative ya Kimataifa ya Trachoma; Usafi wa uso; na uboreshaji wa mazingira, hasa upatikanaji bora wa maji na huduma za usafi.

translations.source: WHO adopted the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues in its mandate to provide leadership and coordination of international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.
translations.reference: WHO ilipitisha mkakati wa SALAMA mnamo 1993 na inaendelea katika jukumu lake la kutoa uongozi na uratibu wa juhudi za kimataifa za kuondoa trakoma kama shida ya afya ya umma.
translations.hypothesis: WHO ilianzisha mkakati wa SAFE mnamo 1993 na inaendelea katika jukumu lake la kuongoza na ushirikishwaji wa juhudi za kimataifa za kuondoa magonjwa ya mata katika afya ya umma.

translations.source: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history. It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.reference: Dawa ya asili, Nyongeza na Shirikishi Muhtasari: Dawa ya jadi Dawa ya jadi ina historia ndefu. Ni jumla ya maarifa, ustadi, na mazoea kulingana na nadharia, imani, na uzoefu wa asili wa tamaduni tofauti, iwe ya kuelezeka au la, inayotumika katika kudumisha afya na vile vile katika kuzuia, utambuzi, uboreshaji au matibabu ya magonjwa ya kimwili na kiakili. Dawa ya ziada Maneno "dawa ya ziada" au "dawa mbadala" yanarejelea seti pana ya mazoea ya utunzaji wa afya ambayo si sehemu ya mila ya nchi hiyo au tiba ya kawaida na haijajumuishwa kikamilifu katika mfumo mkuu wa huduma ya afya. Zinatumika kwa kubadilishana na dawa za jadi katika nchi zingine. Dawa za mitishamba Dawa za mitishamba ni pamoja na mimea, mimea, maandalizi ya mitishamba na bidhaa za mitishamba zilizomalizika, ambazo zina viungo hai vya sehemu za mimea, au vitu vingine vya mimea, au mchanganyiko. Athari: Mkakati wa Tiba Asilia wa WHO 2014–2023 ulitayarishwa na kuzinduliwa kwa kuitikia azimio la Bunge la Afya Ulimwenguni kuhusu tiba asilia (WHA62.13).
translations.hypothesis: Maarifa ya dawa za jadi, dawa za ziada, na dawa za umoja: Dawa za jadi, ina historia ndefu. Ni muhtasari wa maarifa, ujuzi, na mbinu zozote ambazo zinalenga katika mitindo, imani, na uzoefu wa tamaduni mbalimbali, iwe zinaweza kufahamika au la, zinatumika katika kudumisha afya, pamoja na katika kuzuia, kutambua, kuboresha, au kutibu magonjwa ya kimwili na akili. Dawa za ziada, terminolojia "dawa za ziada" au "dawa mbadala" zina maana ya mbinu za huduma za afya ambazo hazijojumuishwa katika utamaduni au dawa za kawaida za nchi, na hazijojumuishwa kikamilifu katika mfumo wa huduma za afya unaoongoza. Katika nchi fulani, hutumika sawa na dawa za jadi. Dawa za mimea, Dawa za mimea ni pamoja na mimea, vifaa vya mimea, matayarisho ya mimea, na bidhaa za mimea zilizokamilika, ambazo zina vipengele vya mimea, au vifaa vingine vya mimea, au mchanganyiko. Athari: Mfumo wa Dawa za Jadi wa WHO wa 2014–2023 uloundwa na kuwasilishwa kama jibu kwa azimio la Kamati ya Afya ya Dunia kuhusu dawa za jadi (WHA62.13).

translations.source: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners; To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.reference: Mkakati huo unalenga kusaidia Nchi Wanachama katika kuunda sera tendaji na kutekeleza mipango ya utekelezaji ambayo itaimarisha jukumu la tiba asilia katika kuweka idadi ya watu kuwa na afya bora. Kushughulikia changamoto, kukabiliana na mahitaji yaliyoainishwa na Nchi Wanachama na kuendeleza kazi iliyofanywa chini ya mkakati wa tiba asilia wa WHO: 2002-2005, mkakati uliosasishwa wa kipindi cha 2014-2023 unatoa kipaumbele zaidi kuliko mtangulizi wake katika kuweka kipaumbele huduma za afya na mifumo, ikiwa ni pamoja na bidhaa za tiba asilia na nyongeza, mazoea na watendaji. Malengo ya kimkakati ni: Kujenga msingi wa maarifa kwa usimamizi hai wa T&CM kupitia sera zinazofaa za kitaifa; Kuimarisha uhakikisho wa ubora, usalama, matumizi sahihi na ufanisi wa T&CM kwa kudhibiti bidhaa, mazoea na watendaji; Kukuza huduma ya afya kwa wote kwa kuunganisha huduma za T&CM katika utoaji wa huduma za afya na kujihudumia. Jibu la WHO: Ripoti ya kimataifa ya WHO kuhusu tiba asilia na nyongeza ya 2019 ilitengenezwa ili kushughulikia pengo la data za kuaminika, zinazoaminika na rasmi kutoka kwa Nchi Wanachama katika eneo la T&CM. Ripoti hii hukagua maendeleo ya kimataifa katika T&CM katika kipindi cha miongo miwili iliyopita na inategemea michango kutoka Nchi 179 Wanachama wa WHO. Inatoa taarifa muhimu kwa watunga sera, wataalamu wa afya na umma kwa kutumia mchango unaowezekana wa T&CM kwa afya na ustawi.
translations.hypothesis: Mbinu hii ina lengo la kusaidia mataifa wanachama katika kuendeleza sera za pro-aktivi na kupanga mipango ya matendo ambayo itasaidia kuimarisha jukumu amlo dawa za jadi zina katika kudumisha afya ya watu. Kwa kutambua changamoto, kujibu mahitaji iliyobainishwa na mataifa wanachama, na kujenga kwenye kazi iliyofanyika chini ya mkakati wa WHO wa dawa za jadi: 2002–2005, mkakati mpya wa kipindi cha 2014–2023 unawezesha zaidi kuliko mkakati wa awali, na unaangazia zaidi utumiaji wa huduma na mifumo za afya, pamoja na bidhaa, mbinu na wataalamu wa dawa za jadi. Lengo la mkakati ni: Kuendeleza msingi wa ujuzi wa usimamizi pro-aktivi wa dawa za jadi kupitia sera za kitaifa; Kuimarisha ubora, usalama, matumizi sahihi na ufanisi wa dawa za jadi kwa kupitia udhibiti wa bidhaa, mbinu na wataalamu; Kukuza huduma za afya za kiujumla kwa kuunganisha huduma za dawa za jadi katika utoaji wa huduma za afya na huduma za kujitunza. Jibu la WHO: Ripoti ya kimataifa ya WHO kuhusu dawa za jadi na kamili mwaka 2019 ilitengenezwa ili kutatua pengo katika data za kuaminika, za tozo na rasmi kutoka kwa mataifa wanachama katika eneo la dawa za jadi. Ripoti hii inaangazia maendeleo ya kimataifa katika dawa za jadi katika miaka miwili iliyopita, na ina msingi katika michango kutoka mataifa 179 za WHO. Inatoa taarifa muhimu kwa watawala, wataalamu wa afya na umma ili kutumia uwezo wa dawa za jadi katika afya na ustawi.

translations.source: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs. The ethical aspects of transplantation are at the forefront. In particular, the unmet patients’ needs and the shortage of transplants lead to the temptation of trafficking in human body components for transplantation. Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;
translations.reference: Kupandikiza Muhtasari: Kupandikiza seli za binadamu, tishu na viungo Kupandikiza seli za binadamu, tishu au viungo huokoa maisha ya watu wengi na kurejesha kazi muhimu ambapo hakuna njia mbadala za ufanisi kulinganishwa zilizopo. Katika miaka 50, upandikizaji umekuwa jambo la mafanikio duniani kote. Hata hivyo, kuna tofauti kubwa kati ya nchi katika upatikanaji wa upandikizaji unaofaa na katika kiwango cha usalama, ubora, ufanisi wa mchango na upandikizaji wa seli za binadamu, tishu na viungo. Vipengele vya maadili vya upandikizaji viko mbele. Hasa, mahitaji ya wagonjwa ambayo hayajafikiwa na uhaba wa vipandikizi husababisha jaribu la usafirishaji wa viungo vya mwili wa binadamu kwa ajili ya upandikizaji. Kupandikiza binadamu Seli za binadamu na tishu za kupandikiza zinaweza kuokoa maisha au kurejesha kazi muhimu. Kwa mfano pandikizi la corneal linaweza kurejesha kuona kwa upofu wa corneal;
translations.hypothesis: Maelezo kuhusu Uplantaji: Uplantaji wa seli, tishu na viungo vya binadamu. Uplantaji wa seli, tishu au viungo vya binadamu huzuisha maisha mengi na kurudisha kazi muhimu ambapo hakuna njia nyingine sawa. Katika miaka 50, uplantaji umeendelea kuwa utumiaji unaofanikiwa duniani. Hata hivyo, kuna tofauti kubwa kati ya nchi katika upatikanaji wa uplantaji unafaa, na katika kiwango cha usalama, ubora, na ufanisi wa upatikanaji na uplantaji wa seli, tishu na viungo vya binadamu. Masuala etika ya uplantaji ni muhimu sana. Hasa, mahitaji ya wagonjwa ambaye hawawezi kupata uplantaji, na ukosefu wa uplantaji, husababisha tamaa ya kuuza sehemu za mwili wa binadamu kwa ajili ya uplantaji. Uplantaji wa binadamu: Seli na tishu za binadamu kwa ajili ya uplantaji zinaweza kuokoa maisha au kurudisha kazi muhimu. Kwa mfano, uplantaji wa macho inaweza kurudisha umma kwa wagonjwa waliopata ugonjwa wa umama.

translations.source: the transplantation of haematopoietic stem cells can cure congenital or acquired diseases including some leukaemias See further information on Haematopoeitic Stem Cells the transplantation of a human heart valve often constitutes the best replacement situation and recipient patients do not require long term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, animal to human, defined as living cells, tissues or organs of animal origin and human body fluids, cells, tissues or organs that have ex vivo with these living, xenogeneic materials, has the potential to constitute an alternative to material of human origin and bridge the shortfall in human material for transplantation. Donation: Donations from deceased persons should be developed to their maximum therapeutic potential avoiding, whenever possible, the inherent risks to live donors, as stated in WHO Guiding Principle 3. There is a recognized need for communities, and health professionals, to become better educated about donation and transplantation and that is the key to the success of deceased donation programmes. However, despite the frequent use of materials donated from deceased donors, the donations from living donors are necessary for some types of transplants or to compensate for the limited supply of material available from deceased donors in order to meet patient needs. Living donation is thus practised despite the fact that it involves risks for the donor that may not be negligible. As the procurement of human material for transplantation from deceased or living donors and the subsequent allogeneic transplantation may entail ethical and safety risks for both the recipient and the donor, strict controls and effective oversight should be carried out by the health authorities to protect them.
translations.reference: upandikizaji wa seli za shina za hematopoietic unaweza kuponya magonjwa ya kuzaliwa au kupatikana ikiwa ni pamoja na baadhi ya leukemia. Tazama habari zaidi juu ya Seli za Shina za Hematopoeitic upandikizaji wa vali ya moyo wa binadamu mara nyingi hujumuisha hali bora ya uingizwaji na wagonjwa wanaopokea hawahitaji tiba ya muda mrefu ya kuzuia mgando. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, mnyama kwa binadamu, inayofafanuliwa kama chembe hai, tishu au viungo vya asili ya wanyama na maji maji ya mwili wa binadamu, seli, tishu au viungo ambavyo vina ex vivo na nyenzo hizi hai, xenogeneic, ina uwezo wa kuunda nyenzo mbadala ya asili ya mwanadamu na kuziba upungufu katika nyenzo za binadamu kwa ajili ya upandikizaji. Uchangiaji: Michango kutoka kwa watu walioaga dunia inapaswa kuendelezwa kwa uwezo wao wa juu zaidi wa kimatibabu ili kuepuka, inapowezekana, hatari za asili kwa wafadhili hai, kama ilivyoelezwa katika Kanuni ya 3 ya Mwongozo wa WHO. Kuna hitaji linalotambulika kwa jamii, na wataalamu wa afya, kuelimishwa vyema kuhusu uchangiaji na upandikizaji na huo ndio ufunguo wa mafanikio ya mipango ya uchangiaji wa marehemu. Hata hivyo, licha ya matumizi ya mara kwa mara ya vifaa vinavyotolewa na wafadhili waliofariki, michango kutoka kwa wafadhili wanaoishi ni muhimu kwa baadhi ya aina za upandikizaji au kufidia ugavi mdogo wa nyenzo zinazopatikana kutoka kwa wafadhili waliofariki ili kukidhi mahitaji ya mgonjwa. Utoaji hai unatekelezwa licha ya ukweli kwamba unahusisha hatari kwa wafadhili ambazo haziwezi kupuuzwa. Kwa vile ununuzi wa nyenzo za binadamu kwa ajili ya kupandikiza kutoka kwa wafadhili waliokufa au wanaoishi na upandikizaji wa alojeneki unaofuata unaweza kuhusisha hatari za kimaadili na kiusalama kwa mpokeaji na mfadhili, udhibiti mkali na uangalizi unaofaa unafaa kutekelezwa na mamlaka za afya ili kuwalinda.
translations.hypothesis: uwekezaji wa seli za tishu za damu inaweza kuponya magonjwa asili au yaliyopatikana, pamoja na aina fulani za magonjwa ya damu. Angalia maelezo zaidi kuhusu seli za tishu za damu,uwekezaji wa valve ya moyo wa binadamu mara nyingi ni hali bora ya ubadili, na wagonjwa wanaohitajika hawahitaji tiba ya kudumisha damu kwa muda mrefu. Uwekezaji wa wanyama: Uwekezaji wa wanyama kwa binadamu, ambao una maana ya seli, tishu, au viungo vya asili ya wanyama na viwango vya mwili wa binadamu, pamoja na seli, tishu, au viungo ambavyo vimekuwa na viunga vya wanyama, ina uwezekano wa kuwa njia mbadala ya viungo vya asili ya binadamu na kuondoa upungufu wa viungo vya binadamu kwa uwekezaji. Utoaji: Utoaji kutoka kwa watu waliofariki lazima utumike kwa uwezo wake wa matibabu, na wakati mwingine, ikiogelea hatari zinazotokana na watu wanaotoa, kama ilivyo katika Miongozo ya WHO, 3. Kuna haja ya moja kwa moja, na wataalamu wa afya, kuwa na elimu bora kuhusu utoaji na uwekezaji, na hii ndiyo njia muhimu ya mafanikio ya programu za utoaji kutoka kwa watu waliofariki. Hata hivyo, licha ya matumizi mara kwa mara ya viungo vilivyotolewa na watu waliofariki, utoaji kutoka kwa watu wanaotoa ni muhimu kwa aina fulani za uwekezaji, au ili kukaribisha upungufu wa viungo ambavyo vinapatikana kutoka kwa watu waliofariki, ili kukidhi mahitaji ya wagonjwa. Kwa hiyo, utoaji kutoka kwa watu wanaotoa unaendeshwa, licha ya kwamba ina hatari kwa mtu anayetoa, ambazo hazipitwi. Kwa sababu, utoaji wa viungo vya binadamu kwa uwekezaji kutoka kwa watu waliofariki au wanaotoa, na uwekezaji wa kufuata, unaweza kuelezea hatari za kitaifa na za usalama, kwa hivyo, vyombo vya afya lazima vitendesha udhibiti thabiti na usimamizi bora ili kulinda.

translations.source: The Guiding Principles mandate optimal care for donors and recipients. The transparent oversight of the health authorities over donation and transplantation activities is also essential to increase the trust of the public in the system. In addition, the decision to be a donor is often based on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources may someday benefit the health needs of the donor’s family.
