If we are prepared to receive a transplant should we need one, then we should be ready to give Ethics: WHO Guiding Principles on Human Cell, Tissue and Organ Transplantation In 1987 the fortieth World Health Assembly, concerned at the trade for profit in human organs, initiated the preparation of the first WHO Guiding Principles on Transplantation, endorsed by the Assembly in 1991 in resolution WHA44.25. These Guiding Principles have greatly influenced professional codes and practices as well as legislation around the world during almost two decades. After a consultation process that took several years, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guiding Principles and identifying areas of progress to optimize donation and transplantation practices. The Declaration of Istanbul Professionals of donation and transplantation from all regions, through many of their organizations and institutions, are endorsing the Declaration of Istanbul on organ trafficking and transplant tourism developed under the leadership of the Transplantation Society and the International Society of Nephrology in May 2008.
translations.reference: Kanuni Elekezi huamuru utunzaji bora kwa wafadhili na wapokeaji. Uangalizi wa uwazi wa mamlaka za afya juu ya shughuli za mchango na upandikizaji pia ni muhimu ili kuongeza imani ya umma katika mfumo. Kwa kuongezea, uamuzi wa kuwa mfadhili mara nyingi hutegemea ufahamu kwamba mchango katika upatikanaji wa rasilimali za upandikizaji unaweza kufaidisha mahitaji ya afya ya familia ya mfadhili siku moja. Ikiwa tumejiandaa kupokea upandikizaji tukihitaji, basi tunapaswa kuwa tayari kutoa Maadili: Kanuni Mwongozo wa WHO kuhusu Seli ya Binadamu, Tishu na Upandikizaji wa Kiungo Mnamo 1987 Mkutano wa Arobaini wa Afya Ulimwenguni, unaohusika na biashara ya faida katika viungo vya binadamu, ulianzisha utayarishaji wa Kanuni za Kwanza za Mwongozo wa WHO juu ya Uhamishaji, iliyoidhinishwa na Bunge mnamo 1991 katika azimio la WHA44.25. Kanuni hizi Elekezi zimeathiri pakubwa kanuni na taratibu za kitaaluma pamoja na sheria kote ulimwenguni takriban miongo miwili. Baada ya mchakato wa mashauriano uliochukua miaka kadhaa, Bunge la Sitini na tatu la Afya Duniani lilipitisha azimio WHA63.22 tarehe 21 Mei 2010, likiidhinisha Kanuni za Mwongozo wa WHO zilizosasishwa na kubainisha maeneo ya maendeleo ili kuboresha uchangiaji na upandikizaji. Azimio la Istanbul Wataalamu wa mchango na upandikizaji kutoka mikoa yote, kupitia mashirika na taasisi zao nyingi, wanaidhinisha Azimio la Istanbul kuhusu usafirishaji haramu wa viungo na utalii wa kupandikiza ulioendelezwa chini ya uongozi wa Jumuiya ya Upandikizaji na Jumuiya ya Kimataifa ya Nephrology mnamo Mei 2008.
translations.hypothesis: Kanuni za mwongozo zinaagiza utunzaji bora kwa washiriki na wale wanapewa. Ufuatiliaji wa uwazi wa vyombo vya afya katika shughuli za uhuru na upimaji pia ni muhimu ili kuongeza imani ya umma katika mfumo. Zaidi ya hayo, uamuzi wa kuwa mshiriki mara nyingi una msingi katika uelewa kwamba contribution kwa kopo za upimaji inaweza siku moja kunufaisha mahitaji ya kiafya ya familia ya mshiriki. Kama tuna tayari kupokea upimaji, tunapaswa kuwa tayari kutoa. Etika: Kanuni za mwongozo za WHO kuhusu upimaji wa seli, tishu na viungo za binadamu. Katika mwaka wa 1987, Kamati ya Kwanza ya Afya ya Dunia, iliyo na wasiwasi kuhusu biashara ya faida katika viungo za binadamu, ilianzisha maandalizi ya kanuni za mwongozo za kwanza za WHO kuhusu upimaji, ambazo zimeidhinishwa na Kamati mwaka wa 1991 katika azimio WHA44.25. Kanuni hizi za mwongozo zimeathiri sana kanuni na utumiaji wa kitaalamu, pamoja na sheria duniani kote kwa takribu miaka miwili. Baada ya mchakato wa mashauriano ambao ulichukua miaka kadhaa, Kamati ya Kwanza ya Afya ya Dunia ilianzisha azimio WHA63.22 tarehe 21 Mei 2010, ikiidhinisha kanuni za mwongozo zilizosasishwa za WHO na kutambua maeneo ya maendeleo ili kuboresha utumiaji wa uhuru na upimaji. Taarifa ya Wataalamu wa Uhuru na Upimaji kutoka kila eneo, kupitia mashirika na taasisi zao, zinaidhinisha Taarifa ya Istanbul kuhusu unywaji na uturishaji wa viungo, iliyoundwa chini ya uongozi wa Chama cha Upimaji na Chama cha Kimataifa cha Nefroloji mnamo Mei 2008.

translations.source: Travel and health Overview: International travel can pose various risks to health, depending on the characteristics of both the traveller and the travel. Travellers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, microbes, and temperature, which can result in ill-health. In addition, serious health risks may arise in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, hygiene and sanitation are inadequate, medical services are not well developed and clean water is unavailable. International travel is on the rise, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational and humanitarian purposes each year. All travellers must prepare for the variety of health risks they can be exposed to in unfamiliar environments before, during and after they travel. People planning to travel should seek advice on the potential hazards in their chosen destinations and understand how best to protect their health and minimize the risk of acquiring disease. Forward planning, appropriate preventive measures and careful precautions can protect the health of travellers, and minimize the risks of accident and of acquiring disease. Although the medical profession and the travel industry can provide extensive help and sound advice, it remains the traveller’s responsibility to seek information, to understand the risks involved and to take the necessary precautions to protect their health while travelling. Risk: When determining the health risks of international travel, travellers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:
translations.reference: Usafiri na afya Muhtasari: Usafiri wa kimataifa unaweza kusababisha hatari mbalimbali kwa afya, kulingana na sifa za msafiri na wasafiri. Wasafiri wanaweza kukutana na mabadiliko ya ghafla na makubwa katika urefu, unyevu, vijidudu, na joto, ambayo inaweza kusababisha afya mbaya. Aidha, hatari kubwa za kiafya zinaweza kutokea katika maeneo ambayo malazi hayana ubora, usafi na usafi wa mazingira hautoshi, huduma za matibabu hazijaendelezwa vizuri na maji safi hayapatikani. Usafiri wa kimataifa unaongezeka, kwani mamilioni ya watu husafiri kwa madhumuni ya kitaaluma, kijamii, burudani na kibinadamu kila mwaka. Wasafiri wote lazima wajitayarishe kwa aina mbalimbali za hatari za kiafya wanazoweza kukabiliwa nazo katika mazingira yasiyofahamika kabla, wakati na baada ya kusafiri. Watu wanaopanga kusafiri wanapaswa kutafuta ushauri kuhusu hatari zinazoweza kutokea katika maeneo waliyochagua na kuelewa jinsi bora ya kulinda afya zao na kupunguza hatari ya kupata magonjwa. Kupanga mbele, hatua zinazofaa za kuzuia na tahadhari makini zinaweza kulinda afya ya wasafiri, na kupunguza hatari za ajali na kupata magonjwa. Ingawa taaluma ya matibabu na sekta ya usafiri inaweza kutoa usaidizi wa kina na ushauri mzuri, inasalia kuwa jukumu la msafiri kutafuta maelezo, kuelewa hatari zinazohusika na kuchukua tahadhari zinazohitajika ili kulinda afya zao wakati wa kusafiri. Hatari: Wakati wa kubainisha hatari za kiafya za usafiri wa kimataifa, wasafiri na wataalamu wa afya wanapaswa kuzingatia mambo muhimu yafuatayo:
translations.hypothesis: Maelezo ya Safari na Afya: Safari za kimataifa zinaweza kuleta hatari mbalimbali kwa afya, kulingana na sifa za abiria na safari. Abiria wanaweza kukutana na mabadiliko ghafla na kubwa katika kimo, umaslamo, vijinsari, na halali, ambazo zinaweza kusababisha magonjwa. Zaidi ya hayo, hatari kubwa za afya zinaweza kutokea katika maeneo ambapo hali ya makazi ni mbaya, usafi na sanitasi si vya kutosha, huduma za matibabu si imara, na maji safi si yana. Safari za kimataifa zinaongezeka, kwani mamilioni ya watu kusafiri kwa madhumuni ya kitaalamu, kijamii, ya burudani, na wa kiraia kila mwaka. Abiria wote wanapaswa kupanga kwa usahihi hatari mbalimbali za afya ambazo wanaweza kukabili, kabla, wakati, na baada ya kusafiri. Watu wanaopanga kusafiri wanapaswa kuuliza kuhusu hatari zinazowezekana katika maeneo wanayochagua, na kuelewa jinsi bora ya kulinda afya yao na kupunguza hatari ya kupata magonjwa. Mpango wa awali, hatua za kuzuia, na tahadhari za makini zinaweza kulinda afya ya abiria, na kupunguza hatari za ajali na za kupata magonjwa. Ingawa jamii ya matibabu na sekta ya usafiri zinaweza kutoa msaada na ushauri, ni jukumu la abiria kuuliza kuhusu habari, kuelewa hatari, na kuchukua tahadhari muhimu ili kulinda afya yao wakati wa kusafiri.

translations.source: mode of transport destination(s) duration and season of travel purpose of travel standards of accommodation, food hygiene and sanitation behaviour of the traveller underlying health of the traveller Travellers may encounter the following health risks depending on their destination: animal and insect bites, including mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, and some flies, which can spread diseases such as malaria exposure to blood or other bodily fluids
translations.reference: mfumo wa usafiri Mwisho(hatima) muda na msimu wa safari madhumuni ya kusafiri viwango vya malazi, usafi wa chakula na usafi wa mazingira tabia ya msafiri afya ya msingi ya msafiri Wasafiri wanaweza kukumbana na hatari zifuatazo za kiafya kulingana na hatima yao: kuumwa na wanyama na wadudu wakiwemo mbu, kupe, viroboto na baadhi ya nzi ambao wanaweza kueneza magonjwa kama malaria. yatokanayo na damu au maji maji mengine ya mwili
translations.hypothesis: Njia ya usafiri, marudio, muda wa safari, na msimu wa safari, sababu ya safari, viwango vya makazi, usafi wa chakula, na usafi, tabia za msafiri, afya ya msafiri. Watu wanaosafiri wanaweza kukabiliana na hatari za kiafya zifuata: uchimbaji na hatari za wadudu, pamoja na nyoka, nyuki, na aina fulani za ndege, ambazo zinaweza kusababisha magonjwa kama malaria, na uingizaji wa damu au viwango vingine vya mwili.

translations.source: environmental changes, such as altitude, humidity, temperature foodborne and waterborne diseases inadequate medical services injuries, including from road traffic accidents or recreational activities psychological health, including stress and culture shock Travellers may also be exposed to a number of infectious diseases, and many of these can be prevented by vaccination. However, there are some infectious diseases, including some of the most dangerous, for which no vaccines exist. Destinations where accommodation, hygiene and sanitation, medical care and water quality are of a high standard pose relatively few serious risks to the health of travellers. Where these services are not provided, travellers should take stringent precautions to avoid illness. Travel warnings from governmental sources should be taken seriously.
translations.reference: mabadiliko ya mazingira, kama vile urefu, unyevu, joto magonjwa yatokanayo na chakula na maji huduma duni za matibabu majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na ajali za barabarani au shughuli za burudani afya ya kisaikolojia, pamoja na mafadhaiko na mshtuko wa kitamaduni Wasafiri wanaweza pia kukabiliwa na magonjwa kadhaa ya kuambukiza, na mengi ya haya yanaweza kuzuiwa kwa chanjo. Hata hivyo, kuna baadhi ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza, ikiwa ni pamoja na baadhi ya hatari zaidi, ambayo hakuna chanjo zilizopo. Maeneo ambayo malazi, usafi na usafi wa mazingira, huduma ya matibabu na ubora wa maji ni wa kiwango cha juu husababisha hatari chache kwa afya ya wasafiri. Pale ambapo huduma hizi hazitolewi, wasafiri wanapaswa kuchukua tahadhari kali ili kuepuka maradhi. Maonyo ya usafiri kutoka kwa vyanzo vya serikali yanapaswa kuchukuliwa kwa uzito.
translations.hypothesis: mabadiliko ya mazingira, kama vile kimo, umwagiliaji, halijoto, magonjwa yanayotokana na chakula na maji, huduma za matibabu zisizo vya kutosha, majeraha, pamoja na majeraha kutoka kwenye ajali za barabara au shughuli za burudani, afya ya akili, pamoja na mvutano na mabadiliko ya utamaduni. Watalii pia wanaweza kukabiliwa na magonjwa mengine, na wengi yao yanaweza kuzuwa kupitia chanjo. Hata hivyo, kuna magonjwa fulani, pamoja na matumbo, ambayo hakuna chanjo. Maeneo ambapo makazi, usafi na usafi, huduma za matibabu na ubora wa maji ni wa kiwango cha juu, huleta hatari ndogo kwa afya ya watalii. Katika maeneo ambapo huduma hizi hazipatikani, watalii wanapaswa kuwa makini ili kuepuka magonjwa. Tahadhari kutoka kwa vyombo vya serikali zinapaswa kuchukuliwa kwa uzito.

translations.source: Prevention: Before travel: contact a travel medicine centre or a physician, preferably 4–8 weeks before departure to receive any required vaccinations; read up on local diseases related to your destination; request information on malaria risk and prevention of mosquito bites. This may include purchasing appropriate preventive medication and packing a bednet and insect repellent; get a medical and dental check-up; obtain prescriptions for medication according to length of stay and assemble a suitable medical kit; purchase medical insurance with appropriate cover abroad During travel: eat only thoroughly cooked food and drink only bottled or packaged cold drinks. Boil drinking-water if safety is doubtful.
translations.reference: Kinga: Kabla ya kusafiri: wasiliana na kituo cha dawa za usafiri au daktari, ikiwezekana wiki 4-8 kabla ya kuondoka ili kupokea chanjo zinazohitajika; soma kuhusu magonjwa ya eneo lako yanayohusiana na unakoenda; omba taarifa kuhusu hatari ya malaria na uzuiaji wa kuumwa na mbu. Hii inaweza kujumuisha kununua dawa zinazofaa za kuzuia na kufunga chandarua na dawa ya kufukuza wadudu; pata uchunguzi wa matibabu na meno; kupata maagizo ya dawa kulingana na urefu wa kukaa na kukusanya kit cha matibabu kinachofaa; nunua bima ya matibabu na bima inayofaa nje ya nchi Wakati wa kusafiri: kula tu chakula kilichopikwa vizuri na kunywa tu vinywaji baridi vya chupa au vifungashio. Chemsha maji ya kunywa ikiwa usalama ni wa shaka.
translations.hypothesis:
Mipango: Kabla ya kusafiri: wasiliana na kituo cha dawa za safari au daktari, kipa, miezi 4-8 kabla ya safari ili kupata chanjo zinazohitajika; soma kuhusu magonjwa ya eneo lako; piga swali kuhusu hatari ya malaria na njia za kuzuia ugonjwa. Hii inaweza kujumuisha kununua dawa za kinga, na kupaka neti ya kulala na dawa za kupunguza wadudu; pima afya yako na ya meno; pata dawa za matibabu kulingana na muda wa kukaa, na ujenge kit ni cha matibabu chenye vifaa muhimu; nunua bima ya matibabu na kingo inayofaa nje ya nchi.

Wakati wa kusafiri: kula tu chakula kilichopangwa vizuri na kunywa tu maji yaliyopangwa au yaliyopakwa. Chemsha maji kwa matumizi ikiwa kuna shaka kuhusu usalama.

translations.source: be aware of accidents or problems related to traffic, animals, allergies, sun and sport; carry a card showing your blood group; wear a medical alert bracelet, if you have allergies or a chronic disease Travellers should obtain a medical examination on their return if they: return with a fever from a country where malaria is or may be present suffer from a chronic disease experience illness in the weeks following their return home received treatment for malaria while travelling may have been exposed to a serious infectious disease spent more than 3 months in a developing country
translations.reference: kuwa na ufahamu wa ajali au matatizo yanayohusiana na trafiki, wanyama, mizio, jua na michezo; kubeba kadi inayoonyesha kundi lako la damu; kuvaa bangili ya tahadhari ya matibabu, ikiwa una mzio au ugonjwa sugu. Wasafiri wanapaswa kufanyiwa uchunguzi wa kimatibabu wanaporudi ikiwa: kurudi na homa kutoka nchi ambako malaria iko au inaweza kuwepo kuteseka na ugonjwa sugu hupata ugonjwa kwa wiki zinazofuata baada ya kurudi nyumbani alipata matibabu ya malaria akiwa safarini inaweza kuwa wazi kwa ugonjwa mbaya wa kuambukiza alitumia zaidi ya miezi 3 katika nchi inayoendelea
translations.hypothesis: elekeza kuhusu ajali au matatizo zinazohusiana na trafiki, wanyama, mzio, jua na michezo; wapeleke kadi inayoeleza aina yako ya damu; jivuli na pete ya tahadhari ya matibabu, ikiwa una mzio au ugonjwa wa kudumu. Watoaji safari wanapaswa kupata uchunguzi wa matibabu baada ya kurudi ikiwa: wameanza safari na joto kutoka nchi ambapo malaria kunaweza kuwa; wana ugonjwa wa kudumu; wamepata ugonjwa katika wiki baada ya kurudi nyumbani; wamepata matibabu ya malaria wakati wa safari; wamepata ugonjwa hatari; wamekuwa katika nchi inayokua kwa zaidi ya miezi 3

translations.source: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB). Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.reference: Kifua kikuu Muhtasari: Kifua kikuu husababishwa na bakteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) na mara nyingi huathiri mapafu. Kifua kikuu huenezwa kwa njia ya hewa wakati watu wenye TB ya mapafu wanapokohoa, kupiga chafya au kutema mate. Mtu anahitaji kuvuta vijidudu vichache tu ili kuambukizwa. Kila mwaka, watu milioni 10 wanaugua kifua kikuu (TB). Licha ya kuwa ni ugonjwa unaozuilika na unaotibika, watu milioni 1.5 hufa kutokana na TB kila mwaka - na kuifanya kuwa muuaji mkuu wa kuambukiza duniani. Kifua kikuu ndicho kisababishi kikuu cha vifo vya watu walio na VVU na pia mchangiaji mkubwa katika ukinzani wa viua viini Watu wengi wanaougua TB wanaishi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati, lakini TB ipo duniani kote. Takriban nusu ya watu wote walio na TB wanaweza kupatikana katika nchi 8: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Ufilipino na Afrika Kusini. Takriban robo ya idadi ya watu duniani inakadiriwa kuwa wameambukizwa bakteria wa TB, lakini watu wengi hawataendelea kupata ugonjwa wa TB na wengine wataondoa maambukizi.
translations.hypothesis: Muhtasari wa Majeraha: Majeraha huasababishwa na bakteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) na mara nyingi husababisha matumbo. Majeraha husambaa hewani wakati watu wenye majeraha kwenye matumbo hushikilia, kutapika, au kutapika. Mtu anaweza kuambukizwa kwa kuingia kwa bakteria chache. Kila mwaka, milioni 10 wa watu wanakuwa na majeraha. Ingawa ni ugonjwa unaoweza kuzuia na kupona, milioni 1.5 wa watu wanufaika kila mwaka – na hivyo kuwa mojawapo ya sababu kuu za vifo duniani. Majeraha ndiyo sababu kuu ya vifo kwa watu wenye ugonjwa wa HIV, na pia ni sababu kubwa ya upinzani wa bakteria. Watu wengi ambao wanakuwa na majeraha wanaishi katika nchi za kiuchumi cha chini na katikati, lakini majeraha yanaenea duniani kote. Kitaro cha watu wote wenye majeraha, takriban watu 8: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines na Afrika Kusini. Takriban nusu ya idadi ya watu duniani, inatarajiwa kuwa wameambukizwa na bakteria za majeraha, lakini watu wengi hawataendelea na ugonjwa wa majeraha, na wengine watapona.

translations.source: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others. If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria. For non-lung TB disease, samples of affected body fluids and tissue can be tested. WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as initial tests for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools can include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.
translations.reference: Wale ambao wameambukizwa lakini sio (bado) wagonjwa na ugonjwa huo hawawezi kuusambaza. Watu walioambukizwa na bakteria wa TB wana hatari ya maisha ya 5-10% ya kuugua TB. Wale walio na kinga dhaifu, kama vile watu wanaoishi na VVU, utapiamlo au kisukari, au watu wanaotumia tumbaku, wana hatari kubwa ya kuugua. Dalili: Dalili za kawaida za ugonjwa wa TB ni pamoja na: Kikohozi cha muda mrefu, maumivu ya kifua, udhaifu au uchovu, kupungua uzito, homa, jasho la usiku. Mara nyingi, dalili hizi zitakuwa nyepesi kwa miezi mingi, hivyo kusababisha kuchelewa kutafuta huduma na kuongeza hatari ya kueneza maambukizi kwa wengine. Mtoa huduma wa afya akishuku mgonjwa kuwa na ugonjwa wa TB, atampeleka mgonjwa kupima. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa unaoshukiwa wa TB ya mapafu, wagonjwa wataombwa kutoa sampuli ya makohozi kwa ajili ya kupima bakteria wa TB. Kwa ugonjwa wa TB usio wa mapafu, sampuli za maji na tishu zilizoathirika za mwili zinaweza kupimwa. WHO inapendekeza vipimo vya haraka vya uchunguzi wa molekuli kama vipimo vya awali kwa watu wanaoonyesha dalili na dalili za TB. Zana nyingine za uchunguzi zinaweza kujumuisha hadubini ya smear ya sputum na X-rays ya kifua.
translations.hypothesis: Watu ambao wameambukizwa lakini bado hawana dalili za ugonjwa, hawawezi kueneza. Watu ambao wameambukizwa na bakteria za TB, wana hatari ya 5-10% ya kuugua TB katika maisha yao. Watu wenye mifumo ya kinga iliyoharibika, kama vile watu wanaosoma HIV, utoaji, au ugonjwa wa sukari, wana hatari kubwa ya kuugua. Dalili: Dalili za kawaida za ugonjwa wa TB ni pamoja na: Mghofuli unaoendelea, maumivu ya kifua, uchovu au uchangamfu, kupoteza uzito, joto, jasho. Kawaida, dalili hizi zita kuwa hafifu kwa miezi kadhaa, na hivyo kusababisha vuguvugu katika utunzaji na kuongeza hatari ya kueneza ugonjwa kwa wengine. Ikiwa daktari ana shaka kwamba mgonjwa ana ugonjwa wa TB, atatuma mgonjwa kwa uchunguzi. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa wa TB wa kifua, wagonjwa wataombwa kutoa sampuli ya sputum kwa uchunguzi wa bakteria za TB. Kwa ugonjwa wa TB usio wa kifua, sampuli za maji na tishu za mwili zinaweza kuchunguliwa. Shirika la Afya ya Dunia (WHO) inapendekeza uchunguzi wa haraka wa kibaolojia kama uchunguzi wa kwanza kwa watu wanaonyesha dalili na ishara za TB. Zana zingine za utambuzi zinaweza kujumuisha uchunguzi wa sputum na picha za kifua.

translations.source: With TB infection, a person gets infected with TB bacteria that lie inactive in the body. This infection can develop into TB disease if their immune system weakens. People with TB infection do not show any signs or symptoms of TB. To identify TB infection, healthcare providers will screen at-risk patients to rule out active TB, and they may use a skin or blood test to check for TB infection. Treatment: TB disease is curable. It is treated by standard 6 month course of 4 antibiotics. Common drugs include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases the TB bacteria does not respond to the standard drugs. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. for drug-resistant TB is longer and more complex.
translations.reference: Kwa maambukizi ya TB, mtu huambukizwa na bakteria ya TB ambayo haifanyi kazi katika mwili. Maambukizi haya yanaweza kukua na kuwa ugonjwa wa TB ikiwa mfumo wao wa kinga utadhoofika. Watu wenye maambukizi ya TB hawaonyeshi dalili zozote za TB. Ili kutambua maambukizi ya TB, watoa huduma za afya watachunguza wagonjwa walio katika hatari ili kudhibiti TB hai, na wanaweza kutumia kipimo cha ngozi au damu ili kuangalia maambukizi ya TB. Matibabu: Ugonjwa wa TB unatibika. Inatibiwa kwa kozi ya kawaida ya miezi 6 ya antibiotiki 4. Dawa za kawaida ni pamoja na rifampicin na isoniazid. Katika baadhi ya matukio, bakteria ya TB haijibu dawa za kawaida. Katika kesi hii, mgonjwa ana TB sugu ya dawa. kwa TB sugu ya dawa ni ya mda mrefu na ngumu zaidi.
translations.hypothesis: Pamoja na ugonjwa wa TB, mtu hupata viumbe vya TB ambavyo hofu katika mwili. Ugonjwa huu unaweza kuendelea kuwa ugonjwa wa TB ikiwa mfumo wa kinga wa mtu huwezesha. Watu wenye ugonjwa wa TB hawataonyesha dalili au ishara zozote za ugonjwa wa TB. Ili kutambua ugonjwa wa TB, wataalamu wa afya watafanya uchunguzi kwa wagonjwa wana hatari ili kuashiria ugonjwa wa TB, na wanaweza kutumia upimaji wa ngozi au damu ili kuangalia ugonjwa wa TB. Matibabu: Ugonjwa wa TB unaweza kutiba. Hutibwa kwa kozi ya kawaida ya dawa za 6 mwezi. Dawa za kawaida ni rifampicin na isoniazid. Katika baadhi ya matukio, viumbe vya TB hazielewi na dawa za kawaida. Katika kesi hii, mgonjwa ana ugonjwa wa TB ambao hauwezi kutiba. Matibabu ya ugonjwa wa TB ambao hauwezi kutiba ni muda mrefu na ni ngumu.

translations.source: The course of TB drugs is provided to the patient with information, supervision and support by a health worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult. If the treatment is not properly completed, the disease can become drug-resistant and can spread. In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill), TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease. This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time. Recent treatment options have shortened the duration to treatment to only 1 or 3 months, as compared to 6 months in the past.
translations.reference: Muda wa dawa za TB hutolewa kwa mgonjwa kwa taarifa, usimamizi na usaidizi na mfanyakazi wa afya au mtu wa kujitolea aliyefunzwa. Bila msaada kama huo, ufuasi wa matibabu unaweza kuwa mgumu. Ikiwa matibabu haijakamilika vizuri, ugonjwa unaweza kuwa sugu wa dawa na unaweza kuenea. Katika kesi ya maambukizi ya TB (ambapo mgonjwa ameambukizwa na bakteria ya TB lakini sio mgonjwa), matibabu ya kuzuia TB yanaweza kutolewa ili kukomesha kuanza kwa ugonjwa huo. Tiba hii hutumia dawa sawa kwa muda mfupi. Chaguo za matibabu za hivi majuzi zimefupisha muda wa matibabu hadi mwezi 1 au 3 tu, ikilinganishwa na miezi 6 iliyopita.
translations.hypothesis: Dawa za kutibu kifafa cha kuvimbi zinatozwa kwa mgonjwa pamoja na maelezo, usimamizi na msaada na mtaalamu wa afya au mtoa huduma mwenye mafunzo. Bila msaada huu, ni vigumu kuzingatia tiba. Ikiwa tiba haitendekewa vizuri, ugonjwa unaweza kuwa na ulemavu na kuenea. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa wa kuvimbi (ambapo mgonjwa ameuambukizwa na bakteria za kuvimbi lakini haujawa na ugonjwa), tiba ya kuzuia kuvimbi inaweza kutumika ili kuacha kuenea kwa ugonjwa. Tiba hii hutumia dawa zimetu sawa, lakini kwa muda mfupi, ikilinganishwa na miezi 6 katika kipindi cha zamani.

translations.source: Ultraviolet radiation Overview: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers the wavelength range of 100–400 nm, which is a higher frequency and lower wavelength than visible light. UV radiation comes naturally from the sun, but it can also be created by artificial sources used in industry, commerce and recreation. The UV region covers the wavelength range 100-400 nm and is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm)UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, all UVC and approximately 90% of UVB radiation is absorbed by ozone, water vapour, oxygen and carbon dioxide. UVA radiation is less affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, the UV radiation reaching the Earth’s surface is largely composed of UVA with a small UVB component. The amount of UV radiation from the sun that hits the Earth’s surface depends on several factors, including the sun’s height in the sky, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer and ground reflection. Reductions in the ozone layer due to human-created pollution increase the amount of UVA and UVB that reaches the surface. This can impact human health, animals, marine organisms and plant life.
translations.reference: Mionzi ya ultraviolet Muhtasari: Mionzi ya Ultraviolet (UV) hufunika masafa ya urefu wa 100-400 nm, ambayo ni masafa ya juu na urefu wa chini wa mawimbi kuliko mwanga unaoonekana. Mionzi ya UV hutoka kwa jua, lakini pia inaweza kuundwa na vyanzo vya bandia vinavyotumiwa katika sekta, biashara na burudani. Kanda ya UV inashughulikia safu ya urefu wa 100-400 nm na imegawanywa katika bendi tatu: UVA (315-400 nm) UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). Mwangaza wa jua unapopita kwenye angahewa, UVC zote na takriban 90% ya mionzi ya UVB humezwa na ozoni, mvuke wa maji, oksijeni na dioksidi kaboni. Mionzi ya UVA huathiriwi kidogo na angahewa. Kwa hiyo, mionzi ya UV inayofikia uso wa Dunia kwa kiasi kikubwa inajumuisha UVA na sehemu ndogo ya UVB. Kiasi cha mionzi ya UV kutoka kwenye jua ambayo hupiga uso wa Dunia inategemea mambo kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na urefu wa jua angani, latitudo, kifuniko cha mawingu, urefu, unene wa safu ya ozoni na kutafakari kwa ardhi. Kupungua kwa tabaka la ozoni kwa sababu ya uchafuzi unaotengenezwa na binadamu huongeza kiwango cha UVA na UVB kinachofika kwenye uso. Hii inaweza kuathiri afya ya binadamu, wanyama, viumbe vya baharini na maisha ya mimea.
translations.hypothesis: Muhtasari wa sinar za ultraviolet: Sinar za ultraviolet (UV) zinaenea katika safu ya mwangiliano ya 100–400 nm, ambayo ni frekwensi ya juu na mwangiliano wa chini kuliko mwanga unaoonekana. Sinar za UV huundwa asilia kutoka kwa jua, lakini pia zinaweza kuundwa na vyanzo bandia zinazotumika katika viwanda, biashara na burudani. Eneo la UV linaenea katika safu ya mwangiliano ya 100-400 nm na linagawanywa katika mitandio tatu: UVA (315-400 nm), UVB (280-315 nm) na UVC (100-280 nm). Wakati mwanga wa jua unapitia anga, sinar za UVC na takriban 90% za UVB hushamilika na ozoni, vapore ya maji, oksijeni na dioksidi ya kaboni. Sinar za UVA zinaathiriwa kidogo na anga. Kwa hiyo, sinar za UV ambazo zinafikia uso wa ardhi, kwa kiasi kubwa, zinaambatana na UVA na UVA ndogo. Kiasi cha sinar za UV kutoka kwa jua ambazo zinafikia uso wa ardhi, hutegemea kadri mambo mengi, ikiwa ni pamoja na kimo cha jua, eneo, usafauti wa mawingu, kimo, unene wa safu ya ozoni na uwakoba wa ardhi. Kupungua kwa safu ya ozoni kutokana na uchafuzi unaoanzishwa na binadamu, huongeza kiasi cha UVA na UVB ambazo zinafikia uso. Hii inaweza kuathiri afya ya binadamu, wanyama, viumbe vya baharini na maisha ya mimea.

translations.source: In humans, increased UV exposure can cause skin cancers, cataracts and immune system damage. Prevention: The rise in the number of cases of skin cancer over the past decades is strongly related to increased exposure to the sun during outdoor activities and to artificial sources of UV radiation such as sunlamps and tanning beds. Overexposure is also the underlying cause of harmful effects on the eyes and immune system. Adopting a few simple precautions can greatly reduce the risk of these health conditions: Limit time in the midday sun from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m., particularly on days when the UV index is high When UV rays are most intense and you must be outside, try to find shade and wear protective clothing, including a brimmed hat that provides sun protection for your head, face and neck. Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen of at least 15 SPF – ideally higher– and reapply every 2 hours. Avoid sunlamps and tanning parlours, as they are known to damage the skin and eyes. Children’s UV exposure should be carefully limited because they are in a dynamic state of growth and therefore more susceptible to environmental threats than adults. Many vital functions such as the immune system are not fully developed at birth, and unsafe environments may interfere with their normal development. WHO Response: WHO works with Member States and partners to increase public understanding of the effects of overexposure to ultraviolet radiation.
translations.reference: Kwa wanadamu, kuongezeka kwa mfiduo wa UV kunaweza kusababisha saratani ya ngozi, mtoto wa jicho na uharibifu wa mfumo wa kinga. Kinga: Kuongezeka kwa idadi ya visa vya saratani ya ngozi katika miongo kadhaa iliyopita kunahusiana sana na kuongezeka kwa mwanga wa jua wakati wa shughuli za nje na vyanzo bandia vya mionzi ya UV kama vile taa za jua na vitanda vya ngozi. Mfiduo kupita kiasi pia ndio sababu kuu ya athari mbaya kwa macho na mfumo wa kinga. Kuchukua tahadhari chache rahisi kunaweza kupunguza sana hatari ya hali hizi za afya: Weka kikomo wakati wa jua la mchana kutoka 10 asubuhi hadi 4 p.m., hasa siku ambazo index ya UV iko juu. Wakati miale ya UV ni mikali zaidi na lazima uwe nje, jaribu kutafuta kivuli na kuvaa mavazi ya kujikinga, ikiwa ni pamoja na kofia yenye ukingo ambayo hutoa ulinzi wa jua kwa kichwa chako, uso na shingo. Tumia kinga ya jua yenye wigo mpana wa angalau SPF 15 - bora zaidi - na upake tena kila saa 2. Epuka taa za jua na vyumba vya ngozi, kwani vinajulikana kuharibu ngozi na macho. Mionzi ya UV ya watoto inapaswa kupunguzwa kwa uangalifu kwa sababu wako katika hali ya ukuaji na kwa hivyo huathirika zaidi na matishio ya mazingira kuliko watu wazima. Kazi nyingi muhimu kama vile mfumo wa kinga haujatengenezwa kikamilifu wakati wa kuzaliwa, na mazingira yasiyo salama yanaweza kuingilia ukuaji wao wa kawaida. Majibu ya WHO: WHO inafanya kazi na Nchi Wanachama na washirika ili kuongeza uelewa wa umma juu ya athari za mionzi ya urujuanimno kupita kiasi.
translations.hypothesis: Kwa wanadamu, pendeza kwa UV inaweza kusababisha saratani za ngozi, glaukoma, na uharibifu wa mfumo wa kinga. Mipango: Kuongezeka kwa idadi ya kesi za saratani za ngozi katika miaka الأخيرة ni moja kwa moja kuhusiana na pendeza nyingi kwa jua wakati wa shughuli za nje, na pia kwa vyanzo vya UV bandia kama vile taa za jua na vyumba vya kupiga tan. Pendeza nyingi pia ni sababu kuu ya madhara kwa macho na mfumo wa kinga. Kuchukua hatua rahisi chache inaweza kupunguza sana hatari za magonjwa haya: Kupunguza muda wa kuwepo kwenye jua kutoka saa 10 hadi saa 4, hasa katika siku ambapo kiwango cha UV ni juu. Wakati nyuzi za UV ni nguvu zaidi, na lazima uwe nje, jaribu kupata umbo na kuvaa mavuli, pamoja na kofia ambayo inatoa kinga dhidi ya jua kwa kichwa, uso na nyuma. Tumia krimi ya kinga dhidi ya UV yenye kiwango cha angalau 15 SPF – bora zaidi – na urejeshe kila baada ya saa 2. Azaa taa za jua na vyumba vya kupiga tan, kwani zinajulikana kwa kuharibu ngozi na macho. Pendeza kwa UV kwa watoto lazima iwe imepangwa kwa uangalifu, kwani wana katika hali ya ukuaji, na kwa hiyo wana hatari zaidi kuliko watu wazima. Vyumba vingi muhimu, kama mfumo wa kinga, hajasikwa kikamilifu, na mazingira hatari yanaweza kuathiri ukuaji wao wa kawaida. Majibu ya WHO: WHO ina kazi na mataifa wanachama na washiriki ili kuongeza ufahamu wa umma kuhusu madhara ya pendeza nyingi kwa nyuzi za UV.

translations.source: The INTERSUN Programme is a collaboration between WHO, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization, the International Agency on Cancer Research and the International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. It promotes and evaluates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and develops an appropriate response through guidelines, recommendations and information dissemination. The goals of the programme including providing practical and sound advice on the health and environmental impacts of UV exposure, to encourage countries to take action to reduce UV-induced health risks and to provide guidance to national authorities about sun awareness programmes. To this end, the programme collaborates with experts and specialist agencies to implement key research activities, identifies and quantifies health risks from UV radiation, develops reliable predictions of health and environmental consequences of changes in UV exposure with stratospheric ozone depletion, and develops practical ways of monitoring change in UV-induced health effects over time. The programme provides tools and guidelines related to the UV index, sun protection for children, artificial sunbeds, tourism and occupational health.
translations.reference: Mpango wa INTERSUN ni ushirikiano kati ya WHO, Mpango wa Mazingira wa Umoja wa Mataifa, Shirika la Hali ya Hewa Duniani, Shirika la Kimataifa la Utafiti wa Saratani na Tume ya Kimataifa ya Ulinzi wa Mionzi isiyo ya Ion. Inakuza na kutathmini utafiti juu ya athari za kiafya za mionzi ya UV, na kukuza jibu linalofaa kupitia miongozo, mapendekezo na usambazaji wa habari. Malengo ya mpango huo ikiwa ni pamoja na kutoa ushauri wa kiutendaji na mzuri kuhusu athari za kiafya na kimazingira zitokanazo na mionzi ya jua, kuhimiza nchi kuchukua hatua ili kupunguza hatari za kiafya zitokanazo na UV na kutoa mwongozo kwa mamlaka za kitaifa kuhusu mipango ya uhamasishaji wa jua. Ili kufikia mwisho huu, mpango huo unashirikiana na wataalam na mashirika maalum kutekeleza shughuli muhimu za utafiti, kutambua na kuhesabu hatari za afya kutokana na mionzi ya UV, kuendeleza utabiri wa kuaminika wa matokeo ya afya na mazingira ya mabadiliko ya mfiduo wa UV na kupungua kwa ozoni ya stratospheric, na kuendeleza njia za vitendo za ufuatiliaji mabadiliko katika athari za kiafya zinazotokana na UV kwa wakati. Mpango huo hutoa zana na miongozo inayohusiana na fahirisi ya UV, ulinzi wa jua kwa watoto, vitanda vya jua bandia, utalii na afya ya kazini.
translations.hypothesis: Programu ya INTERSUN ni ushirikiano kati ya WHO, Programu ya Kimataifa ya Mazingira, Shirika la Kimataifa la Meteorolojia, Taasisi ya Kimataifa ya Utafiti wa Kansa, na Kamishna ya Kimataifa kuhusu Ulinzi wa Madhara ya Hewa Isiyo na Ion. Programu hii inakuza na kutathmini utafiti kuhusu madhara ya UV, na huunda majibu sahihi kupitia miongozo, mapendekezo, na usambazaji wa taarifa. Lengo la programu ni pamoja na kutoa ushauri wa vitendo na wa msingi kuhusu madhara ya UV kwenye afya na mazingira, ili kuhamasisha nchi kuchukua hatua kupunguza hatari za afya zinazotokana na UV, na kutoa ushauri kwa vyombo vya serikali kuhusu programu za uelewa wa UV. Kwa lengo hili, programu ina ushirikiano na wataalamu na mashirika maalumu ili kutekeleza shughuli muhimu za utafiti, kutambua na kuhesabu hatari za afya kutoka kwa UV, kuunda utatizo wa kuamini wa madhara za afya na mazingira kutokana na mabadiliko katika kiwanda cha UV, na kuunda njia za vitendo za kufuatilia mabadiliko katika madhara za afya zinazotokana na UV kwa muda. Programu hutoa zana na miongozo kuhusiana na kiwanda cha UV, ulinzi wa jua kwa watoto, kiti za jua bandia, utalii, na afya ya kazi.

translations.source: Universal health coverage Overview: Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. It covers the full continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care. To deliver on this promise, countries need to have strong, efficient and equitable health systems that are rooted in the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and cost-efficient way to get there. Every country has a different path to achieving UHC and to decide what to cover based on the needs of their populations and the resources at hand. Investing in PHC ensures that all those needs are identified, prioritized and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a robust and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to confronting the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, responding to and recovering from emergencies. WHO response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a WHO strategic priority, with the goal of 1 billion more people benefitting from universal health coverage by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps in emergencies.
translations.reference: Chanjo ya afya kwa wote Muhtasari: Huduma ya afya kwa wote (UHC) ina maana kwamba watu wote wanapata huduma kamili za ubora wa afya wanazohitaji, wakati na mahali wanapozihitaji, bila matatizo ya kifedha. Inashughulikia mwendelezo kamili wa huduma muhimu za afya, kutoka kwa uhamasishaji wa afya hadi kinga, matibabu, urekebishaji na utunzaji wa uponyaji. Ili kutimiza ahadi hii, nchi zinahitaji kuwa na mifumo thabiti ya afya, yenye ufanisi na yenye usawa ambayo imejikita katika jumuiya zinazohudumia. Huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC) ndiyo njia bora na ya gharama nafuu ya kufika hapo. Kila nchi ina njia tofauti ya kufikia UHC na kuamua nini cha kushughulikia kulingana na mahitaji ya idadi ya watu na rasilimali zilizopo. Kuwekeza katika PHC huhakikisha kwamba mahitaji hayo yote yanatambuliwa, yanapewa kipaumbele na kushughulikiwa kwa njia jumuishi; kwamba kuna nguvu kazi ya afya na utunzaji imara na yenye vifaa; na kwamba sekta zote za jamii zinachangia katika kukabiliana na mambo ya kimazingira na kijamii na kiuchumi yanayoathiri afya na ustawi, ikiwa ni pamoja na kujiandaa kwa ajili ya, kukabiliana na hali ya dharura. Jibu la WHO: Kama msingi wa UHC, WHO inapendekeza kuelekeza upya mifumo ya afya kwa kutumia mbinu ya huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC). Kufikia UHC ni kipaumbele cha kimkakati cha WHO, kukiwa na lengo la watu bilioni 1 zaidi kunufaika na huduma ya afya kwa wote ifikapo 2025. tunazingatia usaidizi wa kiufundi ili kujenga taasisi za kitaifa na utoaji wa huduma ili kujaza nafasi muhimu wakati wa dharura.
translations.hypothesis: Muhtasari wa usafirishaji wa afya: Usafirishaji wa afya (UHC) ni kwamba kila mtu anaweza kupata huduma za afya za kiwango, ambazo wanahitaji, wakati na mahali ambapo wanahitaji, bila ya changamoto za kifedha. Hufunika mfululizo kamili wa huduma muhimu za afya, kuanzia uendelezaji wa afya hadi kuzuia, matibabu, uimarishaji, na huduma za mwisho. Ili kutimiza ahadi hii, nchi zinahitaji kuwa na mifumo imara, yenye ufanisi, na sawa, ambazo zinalenga jamii ambazo huwasaidia. Huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC) ni njia bora na yenye gharama nafuu ya kufikia huko. Kila nchi ina njia tofauti ya kufikia UHC na kuamua ni huduma gani kufunika, kulingana na mahitaji ya wakazi wao na rasilimali zinazopatikana. Uinvesti katika PHC unaahidi kwamba mahitaji yote yanaigulishwa, kupangwa, na kushughulika kwa njia iliyoendana; kwamba kuna wafanyakazi wenye uwezo na vifaa vya afya na huduma; na kwamba idara zote za jamii zinaunga mkono katika kushughulikia mambo ya mazingira na kiuchumi ambayo yanaathiri afya na ustawi, pamoja na kupanga, kujibu, na kupulizwa baada ya dharura. Majibu ya WHO: Kama msingi wa UHC, WHO inapendekeza kubadilisha mifumo ya afya kwa kutumia mbinu ya huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC). Kufikia UHC ni kipaumbele muhimu kwa WHO, na lengo ni kwamba mamilioni zaidi ya watu watapata faida ya usafirishaji wa afya. Katika nchi zenye mifumo dhaifu ya afya, tunazingatia usaidizi wa kiufundi ili kujenga taasisi za kitaifa na utoaji wa huduma ili kujaza pengo muhimu wakati wa dharura.

translations.source: In more robust health system settings, we drive public health impact towards health coverage for all through policy dialogue for the systems of the future and strategic support to improve performance. This work is supported by normative guidance and agreements; data, research and innovation; and leadership in the realms of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, multisectoral action, and finance. 2030 Agenda for SDGs WHO’s work is aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which focuses on achieving universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage is a challenge. Progress in service coverage has stalled while the proportion of the population facing catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases continuously. WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC fact sheet for more information.
translations.reference: Katika mipangilio thabiti zaidi ya mfumo wa afya, tunasukuma athari za afya ya umma kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote kupitia mazungumzo ya sera ya mifumo ya siku zijazo na usaidizi wa kimkakati ili kuboresha utendaji kazi. Kazi hii inaungwa mkono na mwongozo na makubaliano ya kawaida; na uongozi katika nyanja za diplomasia, utetezi, usawa wa kijinsia, usawa wa afya na haki za binadamu, hatua za sekta mbalimbali, na fedha. Ajenda ya 2030 ya SDGs Kazi ya WHO inawiana na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu (SDG) lengo la 3.8, ambalo linalenga kufikia bima ya afya kwa wote, ikiwa ni pamoja na ulinzi wa hatari za kifedha, upatikanaji wa huduma bora za afya na gharama nafuu za dawa muhimu na chanjo za bei nafuu kwa wote. Ulimwenguni, maendeleo kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote ni changamoto. Maendeleo katika utoaji wa huduma yamekwama huku idadi ya watu wanaokabiliwa na matumizi mabaya ya afya nje ya mfukoni ikiongezeka kila mara. WHO hufuatilia maendeleo na kuchapisha ripoti za kimataifa ili kufichua ushahidi wa hivi punde kila baada ya miaka miwili. Tazama ripoti hapa na utembelee karatasi ya ukweli ya UHC kwa habari zaidi.
translations.hypothesis: Katika mazingira yenye mfumo wa afya imara, tunaendeleza athari za afya kwa kuhakikisha afya kwa kila mtu kupitia mazungumzo ya sera kwa mifumo ya siku zijazo na usaidizi wa kimkakati ili kuboresha utendaji. Kazi hii inasaidiwa na mwongozo na makubaliano, data, utafiti na ubunifu; na uongozi katika masuala ya diplomasia, utetezi, usawa wa jinsia, usawa wa afya na haki za binadamu, hatua za kiganjo, na fedha. Agenda ya 2030 kwa Malengo ya Maendeleo (SDG) Kazi ya WHO inaambatana na lengo la 3.8 la Malengo ya Maendeleo (SDG), ambalo linaangazia kufikia afya ya kitaifa, pamoja na ulinzi wa hatari za kifedha, upatikanaji wa huduma bora za afya, na upatikanaji wa dawa na chanjo salama, bora, na za bei nafuu kwa kila mtu. Kanda, maendeleo kuelekea afya ya kitaifa ni changamoto. Maendeleo katika upatikanaji wa huduma imesimama, wakati asilimia ya watu wanaotumia pesa nyingi katika matibabu inaendelea kuongezeka. WHO inafuatilia maendeleo na kuchapisha ripoti za kimataifa ili kuonyesha ushahidi mpya kila baada ya miaka miwili. Angalia ripoti hapa na tembelea karatasi ya taarifa kuhusu UHC kwa maelezo zaidi.

translations.source: Urban health Overview: Urbanization is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century that has a significant impact on health. Over 55% of the world’s population live in urban areas – a proportion that is expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most future urban growth will take place in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development trends in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps a city’s most important asset. However, most of the 4.2 billion people living in cities suffer inadequate housing and transport, poor sanitation and waste management, and air quality that fails WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, so-called urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling and active living further combine to make cities epicentres of a noncommunicable disease epidemic and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Around 40% of urban growth is in slums that lack safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to fork, driving demand for unhealthy, processed foods.
translations.reference: Afya ya mjini Muhtasari: Ukuaji wa miji ni mojawapo ya mitindo inayoongoza duniani ya karne ya 21 ambayo ina athari kubwa kwa afya. Zaidi ya 55% ya watu duniani wanaishi mijini - idadi ambayo inatarajiwa kuongezeka hadi 68% ifikapo 2050. Kwa vile ukuaji mkubwa wa miji katika siku zijazo utafanyika katika nchi zinazoendelea, dunia leo ina fursa ya kipekee ya kuongoza ukuaji wa miji na mwelekeo mwingine mkuu wa maendeleo ya miji kwa njia ambayo inalinda na kukuza afya. Hii ni muhimu, sio kwa sababu afya na ustawi wa raia labda ndio mali muhimu zaidi ya jiji. Hata hivyo, wengi kati ya watu bilioni 4.2 wanaoishi mijini wanakabiliana na ukosefu wa makazi na usafiri, usafi wa mazingira na usimamizi duni wa taka, na ubora wa hewa ambao unakiuka miongozo ya WHO. Aina zingine za uchafuzi wa mazingira, kama vile kelele, uchafuzi wa maji na udongo, kinachojulikana kama visiwa vya joto mijini, na ukosefu wa nafasi ya kutembea, kuendesha baiskeli na kuishi hai huchanganyika zaidi na kufanya miji kuwa kitovu cha janga la magonjwa yasiyoambukiza na vichochezi vya mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa. Hazina ya WHO kwa Afya ya Mjini Athari: Takriban 40% ya ukuaji wa miji uko katika makazi duni ambayo hayana maji salama na vyoo, na 91% ya watu katika maeneo ya mijini wanapumua hewa chafu. Linapokuja suala la lishe bora, ukuaji wa miji huongeza umbali kutoka shamba hadi uma, na kusababisha mahitaji ya vyakula visivyo na afya, vilivyochakatwa.
translations.hypothesis: Muhtasari wa afya katika miji: Mji ni mojawapo ya mwenendo muhimu duniani katika karne ya 21, ambapo una athari kubwa kwa afya. Zaidi ya 55% ya wakazi wa dunia wanaishi katika miji – asilimia ambayo inatarajiwa kuongezeka hadi 68% kufikia mwaka wa 2050. Kwa kuwa ukuaji wa miji unaendelea katika nchi zinazoveza, dunia leo ina fursa ya kipekee kuongoza mji na mwenendo mwingine muhimu wa maendeleo ya miji, kwa njia ambayo inalinda na kuendeleza afya. Hii ni muhimu, hasa kwa sababu afya na ustawi wa raia ni moja ya mali muhimu zaidi ya mji. Hata hivyo, karibu watu 4.2 bilioni wanaishi katika miji, wanaishi katika makazi na usafiri duni, usafi na usimamizi wa taka mbaya, na hewa ambayo haifai kanuni za WHO. Ailifa zingine za mchaka, kama vile sauti, mchaka wa maji na udongo, "mifugo ya joto" za miji, na ukosefu wa nafasi ya kwenda kwa miguu, kuendesha baiskeli na kuishi, zinaunga mkono zaidi ili kufanya miji kuwa kituo cha ugonjwa usioambukiza na sababu za mabadiliko ya ikolojia. Hifadhi ya WHO kuhusu athari za afya katika miji: Kiasi cha 40% cha ukuaji wa miji ni katika maeneo ambayo yana utapishaji na usafi salama, na asilimia 91 ya watu katika miji huchukua hewa iliyo na mchaka. Kwa maswala ya lishe, mji huongeza umbali kutoka shamba hadi chakula, na hivyo kuongeza mahitaji ya vyakula vilio na vilivyopakiwa.

translations.source: Urban dwellers are also highly vulnerable to the effects of climate change because of their dependence on fossil fuels for transport, cooking and heating. Cities account for over two thirds of the world’s energy and emit 60% of greenhouse gases, and those inland may experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas because of their large expanses of concrete and limited open green spaces. All of this puts urban dwellers’ health at risk. Most the top 10 causes of death are closely related to rapid and unplanned urbanization, and poor urban design and planning. Cities face the triple health burden of infectious diseases like HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, dengue and diarrhoea; noncommunicable diseases like heart disease, stroke, asthma, cancer, diabetes and depression; and violence and injuries, including road traffic injuries. WHO response: Despite their challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments and climate action. Strong urban policies must prioritize health, as it is essential for fostering good urban livelihoods, building a productive workforce, creating resilient and vibrant communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO’s approach to urban health focuses on better air quality, water and sanitation, and other environmental determinants; healthy urban planning; healthier and smoke-free environments; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy food systems and diets; environmental management of vector-borne diseases; and urban preparedness for emergencies.
translations.reference: Wakazi wa mijini pia wako katika hatari kubwa ya kuathiriwa na mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa kwa sababu ya utegemezi wao wa nishati ya mafuta kwa usafiri, kupikia na kupasha joto. Miji huchukua zaidi ya theluthi mbili ya nishati ya ulimwengu na hutoa 60% ya gesi chafuzi, na zile za ndani zinaweza kukumbwa na halijoto ya 3–5º C juu kuliko maeneo ya vijijini yanayozunguka kwa sababu ya upana wake mkubwa wa saruji na nafasi finyu za kijani kibichi. Haya yote yanahatarisha afya ya wakazi wa mijini. Sababu 10 kuu za vifo zinahusiana kwa karibu na ukuaji wa miji wa haraka na usio na mpango, na muundo mbaya wa mijini na mipango. Miji inakabiliwa na mzigo wa kiafya mara tatu wa magonjwa ya kuambukiza kama vile VVU/UKIMWI, kifua kikuu, nimonia, dengi na kuhara; magonjwa yasiyo ya kuambukiza kama vile ugonjwa wa moyo, kiharusi, pumu, saratani, kisukari na unyogovu; na vurugu na majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na majeraha ya barabarani. Jibu la WHO: Licha ya changamoto zao, miji inaweza kuunda fursa kwa afya bora, mazingira safi na hatua za hali ya hewa. Sera madhubuti za mijini lazima zitangulize afya, kwani ni muhimu kwa ajili ya kuendeleza maisha bora ya mijini, kujenga nguvu kazi yenye tija, kuunda jamii zenye uthabiti na uchangamfu, kuwezesha uhamaji, kukuza mwingiliano wa kijamii na kulinda idadi ya watu walio hatarini. Mtazamo wa WHO kuhusu afya ya mijini unazingatia ubora bora wa hewa, maji na usafi wa mazingira, na viashirio vingine vya mazingira; mipango ya miji yenye afya; mazingira bora na yasiyo na moshi; uhamaji salama na kiafya; kuzuia vurugu na majeraha; kukuza mifumo ya chakula na lishe bora; usimamizi wa mazingira ya magonjwa yanayoenezwa na vekta.na kujiandaa kwa miji kwa dharura.
translations.hypothesis: Watu wa miji pia wana hatari kubwa kutokana na athari za mabadiliko ya ikolojia, kwa sababu wana wasaidia mafuta kwa usafiri, kupikia na joto. Miji ina zaidi ya nusu ya nishati duniani, na hutoka 60% ya gesi za mitambo, na hivyo, maeneo ya ndani yanaweza kuhisi joto la 3–5º C zaidi kuliko maeneo ya maalumu, kwa sababu ya eneo kubwa la matibabu na nafasi ndogo za kijani. Hii yote huhatarisha afya ya watu wa miji. Wahusika kuu 10 wa vifo mara nyingi wana uhusiano na uanzishaji wa haraka na usahihishaji, pamoja na muundo na mipango mbaya ya miji. Miji zinakabiliwa na mzigo wa afya, kama vile magonjwa yaambukizivu kama vile HIV/AIDS, kuhuma, magonjwa ya kupumua, dengue na magonjwa ya matumbo; magonjwa isiyoweza kuambukizwa kama vile magonjwa ya moyo, mashambulizi, asthma, saratani, ugonjwa wa sukari na magonjwa; na ugaidi na majeraha, pamoja na majeraha ya usafiri. Jibu la WHO: Licha ya changamoto, miji zinaweza kuunda fursa za afya bora, mazingira safi na hatua za ikolojia. Sera za miji zinapaswa kutoa umuhimu wa afya, kwani ni muhimu kwa kuendeleza maisha mazuri ya miji, kujenga wafanyakazi wenye ufanisi, kuunda jamii zenye uendelevu na za kuvutia, kurahisisha usafiri, kukuza ushirikiano wa kijamii na kulinda idadi za hatari. Mbinu ya WHO kuhusu afya ya miji inazingatia mazingira bora, maji na usafi, na mambo mengine ya mazingira; mipango bora za miji; mazingira salama na zisizo na sigara; usafiri salama na afya; kuzuia ugaidi na majeraha; kukuza mifumo na lishe bora; usimamizi wa mazingira wa magonjwa yanayosababishwa na wadudu; na tayari kwa dharura katika miji.

translations.source: Working across sectors and ensuring the coherence of policies across different areas is key to creating supportive and enabling environments for health, which ensures that health and equity considerations are integrated throughout the planning process, investments and policy decisions at the local level. To help Member States address the above priorities, WHO works to strengthen the evidence base to help policy-makers make informed decisions when addressing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports monitoring of key health-related indicators. WHO leads and engages in fostering city-to-city exchanges and helps develop institutional and policy frameworks for good urban governance for health and well-being in cities.
translations.reference: Kufanya kazi katika sekta zote na kuhakikisha uwiano wa sera katika maeneo mbalimbali ni muhimu katika kuunda mazingira ya kusaidia na kuwezesha afya, ambayo yanahakikisha kwamba masuala ya afya na usawa yanaunganishwa katika mchakato wa kupanga, uwekezaji na maamuzi ya sera katika ngazi ya ndani. Ili kusaidia Nchi Wanachama kushughulikia vipaumbele vilivyo hapo juu, WHO inafanya kazi ili kuimarisha msingi wa ushahidi ili kusaidia watunga sera kufanya maamuzi sahihi wanaposhughulikia hatari za kiafya. Inatoa zana na mwongozo juu ya kile kinachofanya kazi, na inasaidia ufuatiliaji wa viashiria muhimu vinavyohusiana na afya. WHO inaongoza na kushiriki katika kuhimiza mabadilishano ya jiji hadi jiji na kusaidia kuandaa mifumo ya kitaasisi na sera ya utawala bora wa miji kwa afya na ustawi katika miji.
translations.hypothesis: Kufanya kazi katika sekta mbalimbali na kuhakikisha uwandiko wa sera katika maeneo tofauti ni muhimu kwa kuunda mazingira mazuri na za usaidizi kwa afya, ambapo inawezekana kuingiza masuala ya afya na usawa katika mchakato wa kupanga, michango, na maamuzi ya sera katika viwango vya ndani. Ili kusaidia serikali za wanachama kushughulikia masuala yaliyotajwa, WHO inaendelea kuimarisha msingi wa ushahidi ili kuwasaidia wataalamu wa sera kufanya maamuzi sahihi wakati wa kushughulikia hatari za afya. Inatoa zana na mwongozo kuhusu kile ambacho kinafanya, na inasaidiawachungia dalili muhimu zinazohusiana na afya. WHO inaongoza na kushiriki katika kuendeleza mahusiano kati ya miji, na pia inasaidia kuendeleza miundo ya taasisi na sera kwa utawala bora wa miji kwa ajili ya afya na ustawi katika miji.

translations.source: Violence against children Overview: Violence against children includes all forms of violence against people under 18 years old. For infants and younger children, violence mainly involves child maltreatment (i.e. physical, sexual and emotional abuse and neglect) at the hands of parents and other authority figures. Boys and girls are at equal risk of physical and emotional abuse and neglect, and girls are at greater risk of sexual abuse. As children reach adolescence, peer violence and intimate partner violence, in addition to child maltreatment, become highly prevalent. Violence against children can be prevented. Preventing and responding to violence against children requires that efforts systematically address risk and protective factors at all four interrelated levels of risk (individual, relationship, community, society). May 2016 World Health Assembly resolution endorsed the first ever WHO Global plan of action on strengthening the role of the health system within a national multisectoral response to address interpersonal violence, in particular against women and girls, and against children. A According to this plan, WHO in collaboration with Member States and other partners, is committed to: Monitoring the global magnitude and characteristics of violence against children and supporting country efforts to document and measure such violence. Maintaining an electronic information system that summarizes the scientific data on the burden, risk factors and consequences of violence against children, and the evidence for its preventability.
translations.reference: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto Muhtasari: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto unajumuisha aina zote za ukatili dhidi ya watu walio chini ya umri wa miaka 18. Kwa watoto wachanga na watoto wadogo, unyanyasaji unahusisha hasa unyanyasaji wa watoto (yaani unyanyasaji wa kimwili, kingono na kihisia na kutelekezwa) mikononi mwa wazazi na watu wengine wenye mamlaka. Wavulana na wasichana wako katika hatari sawa ya unyanyasaji wa kimwili na kihisia na kutelekezwa, na wasichana wako katika hatari kubwa ya unyanyasaji wa kijinsia. Watoto wanapobalehe, unyanyasaji wa rika na ukatili wa marafiki wa karibu, pamoja na unyanyasaji wa watoto, huenea sana. Ukatili dhidi ya watoto unaweza kuzuiwa. Kuzuia na kukabiliana na unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto kunahitaji jitihada za kushughulikia hatari na ulinzi katika viwango vyote vinne vya hatari (mtu binafsi, uhusiano, jumuiya, jamii). Azimio la Mei 2016 la Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni liliidhinisha mpango wa kwanza wa utekelezaji wa WHO wa Kimataifa wa kuimarisha jukumu la mfumo wa afya ndani ya mwitikio wa kitaifa wa sekta mbalimbali kushughulikia unyanyasaji wa watu binafsi, hasa dhidi ya wanawake na wasichana, na dhidi ya watoto. A Kulingana na mpango huu, WHO kwa kushirikiana na Nchi Wanachama na washirika wengine, imejitolea: Kufuatilia ukubwa wa kimataifa na sifa za ukatili dhidi ya watoto na kuunga mkono juhudi za nchi kuweka kumbukumbu na kupima ukatili huo. Kudumisha mfumo wa taarifa za kielektroniki unaotoa muhtasari wa data ya kisayansi kuhusu mzigo, sababu za hatari na matokeo ya unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto, na ushahidi wa kuzuilika kwake.
translations.hypothesis: Matumizi ya nguvu dhidi ya watoto: Maelezo: Matumizi ya nguvu dhidi ya watoto ni pamoja na kila aina ya matumizi ya nguvu dhidi ya watu chini ya umri wa 18. Kwa watoto wadogo na watoto wachanga, matumizi ya nguvu mara nyingi ni matumizi ya nguvu kwa watoto (yani, matumizi ya kimwili, kejeli na kihemko) na watu wazazi na viongozi wengine. Wanaume na wasichana wana hatari sawa ya matumizi ya nguvu na ukosefu wa utunzaji, na wasichana wana hatari kubwa ya kejeli. Wakati watoto wanafikia umri wa adolensensi, matumizi ya nguvu kutoka kwa wanafamilia na matumizi ya nguvu kati ya wapendwa, pamoja na matumizi ya nguvu kwa watoto, kuwa za kawaida sana. Matumizi ya nguvu dhidi ya watoto yanaweza kuzuwa. Kuzuia na kujibu matumizi ya nguvu dhidi ya watoto inahitaji juhudi zinazolenga hatari na fursa katika viwango vinne vinavyohusiana (kwa mtu, uhusiano, jamii, na jamii). Katika Majira ya Mei 2016, Majimbo ya Afya ya Dunia (WHO) yalisema azma ya kwanza ya kimataifa kuhusu kuimarisha jukumu la mfumo wa afya katika majibu ya kitaifa ya kushughulikia matumizi ya nguvu, haswa dhidi ya wanawake na wasichana, na dhidi ya watoto. Kulingana na azma hii, WHO, pamoja na mataifa wanachama na washiriki wengine, imetoa ahadi ya: Kutumia ukubwa na sifa za matumizi ya nguvu dhidi ya watoto, na kusaidia juhudi za nchi za kuchunguza na kupima matumizi hayo. Kudumisha mfumo wa taarifa wa kidijitali ambao unafanya muhtasari wa data za kisayansi kuhusu mzigo, hatari na matokeo ya matumizi ya nguvu dhidi ya watoto, na ushahidi wa uwezo wa kuzuia.

translations.source: Developing and disseminating evidence-based technical guidance documents, norms and standards for preventing and responding to violence against children. Regularly publishing global status reports on country efforts to address violence against children through national policies and action plans, laws, prevention programmes and response services. Supporting countries and partners in implementing evidence-based prevention and response strategies, such as those included in INSPIRE: Seven strategies for ending violence against children. Collaborating with international agencies and organizations to reduce and eliminate violence against children globally, through initiatives such as the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, Together for Girls and the Violence Prevention Alliance. Child maltreatment Child maltreatment is the abuse and neglect that occurs to children under 18 years of age. It includes all types of physical and/or emotional ill-treatment, sexual abuse, neglect, negligence and commercial or other exploitation, which results in actual or potential harm to the child’s health, survival, development or dignity in the context of a relationship of responsibility, trust or power. Exposure to intimate partner violence is also sometimes included as a form of child maltreatment.
translations.reference: Kutengeneza na kusambaza hati za mwongozo wa kiufundi, kanuni na viwango vya kuzuia na kukabiliana na ukatili dhidi ya watoto kulingana na ushahidi. Kuchapisha mara kwa mara ripoti za hali ya kimataifa kuhusu juhudi za nchi kushughulikia unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto kupitia sera za kitaifa na mipango ya utekelezaji, sheria, programu za kuzuia na huduma za kukabiliana. Nchi zinazounga mkono na washirika katika kutekeleza mikakati ya uzuiaji na majibu yenye msingi wa ushahidi, kama ile iliyojumuishwa katika INSPIRE: Mikakati saba ya kukomesha ukatili dhidi ya watoto. Kushirikiana na mashirika na mashirika ya kimataifa ili kupunguza na kukomesha ukatili dhidi ya watoto duniani kote, kupitia mipango kama vile Ushirikiano wa Kimataifa wa Kutokomeza Ukatili dhidi ya Watoto, Ushirikiano wa Kimataifa wa Kutokomeza Ukatili dhidi ya Watoto, kwa pamoja kwa Wasichana na Muungano wa Kuzuia Ghasia. Unyanyasaji wa watoto Unyanyasaji wa watoto ni unyanyasaji na utelekezwaji unaotokea kwa watoto chini ya umri wa miaka 18. Inajumuisha aina zote za unyanyasaji wa kimwili na/au kihisia, unyanyasaji wa kijinsia, kutelekezwa, uzembe na unyonyaji wa kibiashara au mwingine, unaosababisha madhara halisi au yanayoweza kutokea kwa afya, maisha, ukuaji au utu wa mtoto katika muktadha wa uhusiano wa mtoto wajibu, uaminifu au madaraka. Mfiduo wa unyanyasaji wa rafiki wa karibu wakati mwingine hujumuishwa kama unyanyasaji wa watoto.
translations.hypothesis: Kuendeleza na kusambaza miongozo za kiufundi, kanuni na viwango, ambavyo ni msingi wa ushahidi, kwa ajili ya kuzuia na kujibu uhalifu dhidi ya watoto. Kutoa taarifa za mara kwa mara kuhusu hali ya kimataifa kuhusu juhudi za nchi za kutekeleza sera na mipango za kuzuia uhalifu dhidi ya watoto, sheria, programu za kuzuia na huduma za kujibu. Kusaidia nchi na washiriki katika kutekeleza mikakati ya kuzuia na kujibu, kama vile hizo zilizopo katika INSPIRE: Mikakati saba za kuacha uhalifu dhidi ya watoto. Kuwa na ushirikiano na mashirika na mashirika za kimataifa ili kupunguza na kuondoa uhalifu dhidi ya watoto, kupitia mashirika kama vile Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, Together for Girls na Violence Prevention Alliance. Matumizi mabaya ya watoto. Matumizi mabaya ya watoto ni matumizi na kupuuzia ambayo hutokea kwa watoto chini ya umri wa 18. Hii ni pamoja na kila aina ya matumizi ya kimwili na/au kihemko, unyonyaji, kupuuzilia, uongo, na matumizi ya kibiashara au nyingine, ambazo husababisha uharibifu au hatari kwa afya, maisha, au maendeleo ya mtoto katika muktadha wa uhusiano wa jukumu, imani, au nguvu. Pia, mara kwa mara, matumizi ya uhalifu kati ya wapendwa inaweza pia kuwa kama aina ya matumizi mabaya ya watoto.

translations.source: Child maltreatment has enormous immediate and long-term repercussions. Beyond death, physical injury and disability, violence can lead to stress that impairs brain development and damages the nervous and immune systems. This in turn is associated with delayed cognitive development, poor school performance and dropout, mental health problems, suicide attempts, increased health-risk behaviours, revictimization and the perpetration of violence. The good news is that child maltreatment can be prevented through interventions that support parents and caregivers, promote non-violent norms and values, provide education and life skills training, strengthen families’ income and economic security, offer high quality response and support services, create and sustain safe environments for children, and implement and enforce laws against child maltreatment. WHO, in collaboration with a number of partners: provides technical and normative guidance for evidence-based child maltreatment prevention; advocates for increased international support for and investment in evidence-based child maltreatment prevention; provides technical support for evidence-based child maltreatment prevention programmes in several low- and middle-income countries. Youth violence Youth violence is a global public health problem.
translations.reference: Unyanyasaji wa watoto una madhara makubwa sana ya papo hapo na ya muda mrefu. Zaidi ya kifo, jeraha la kimwili na ulemavu, vurugu inaweza kusababisha dhiki ambayo huharibu ukuaji wa ubongo na kuharibu mifumo ya neva na kinga. Hii nayo inahusishwa na kucheleweshwa kwa maendeleo ya utambuzi, utendaji duni wa shule na kuacha shule, matatizo ya afya ya akili, majaribio ya kujiua, kuongezeka kwa tabia zinazohatarisha afya, urekebishaji na maandalizi ya unyanyasaji. Habari njema ni kwamba unyanyasaji wa watoto unaweza kuzuiwa kupitia hatua zinazosaidia wazazi na walezi, kukuza kanuni na maadili yasiyo ya ukatili, kutoa elimu na mafunzo ya stadi za maisha, kuimarisha mapato ya familia na usalama wa kiuchumi, kutoa mwitikio wa hali ya juu na huduma za usaidizi, kuunda na kudumisha mazingira salama kwa watoto, na kutekeleza na kutekeleza sheria dhidi ya unyanyasaji wa watoto. WHO, kwa kushirikiana na washirika kadhaa: hutoa mwongozo wa kiufundi na wa kawaida kwa kuzuia unyanyasaji wa watoto kulingana na ushahidi; inatetea ongezeko la usaidizi wa kimataifa kwa uwekezaji katika kuzuia unyanyasaji wa watoto kulingana na ushahidi; hutoa usaidizi wa kiufundi kwa programu za kuzuia unyanyasaji wa watoto kulingana na ushahidi katika nchi kadhaa za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Vurugu za vijana Vurugu za vijana ni tatizo la afya ya umma duniani.
translations.hypothesis: Uharibifu wa watoto una matokeo kubwa za moja kwa moja na za muda mrefu. Mbali na vifo, majeraha ya mwili na ulemavu, uvamizi unaweza kusababisha mvutano ambao unaathiri maendeleo ya ubongo na kuharibu mifumo ya neva na kinga. Hii kwa upande wake inaambatana na ulemavu wa akili, utendaji mbaya shuleni, na kuondoka, matatizo ya afya, majaribio ya suicide, tabia za hatari, upya wa uvamizi, na kuendeleza uvamizi. Habari nzuri ni kwamba, uvamizi wa watoto unaweza kuzuia kupitia hatua za kusaidia wazazi na walezi, kukuza kanuni na maadili zisizo na uvamizi, kutoa elimu na mafunzo ya ujuzi wa maisha, kuimarisha mapato na usalama wa kiuchumi wa familia, kutoa huduma za ubora wa juu, kuunda na kudumisha mazingira salama kwa watoto, na kutekeleza na kutumia sheria dhidi ya uvamizi wa watoto. WHO, kwa ushirikiano na washiriki kadhaa: inatoa mwongozo wa kiufundi na wa kanuni kwa kupunguza uvamizi wa watoto; inakuza usaidizi wa kimataifa na uwekezaji katika kupunguza uvamizi wa watoto; inatoa usaidhi wa kiufundi kwa programu za kupunguza uvamizi wa watoto katika nchi kadhaa za kiuchumi cha chini na katikati. Uvamizi wa vijana Uvamizi wa vijana ni tatizo la afya ya umma katika ulimwengu.

translations.source: It includes a range of acts from bullying and physical fighting, to more severe sexual and physical assault to homicide. Youth violence dramatically increases health, welfare and criminal justice costs; reduces productivity; and generally undermines the fabric of society. Beyond deaths, injuries and psychological harm, youth violence can lead to increased health risk behaviours such as smoking, substance abuse, unsafe sex, and further violence. These in turn are associated with chronic respiratory diseases, cancer, cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, early pregnancy and sexually transmitted diseases. Effective prevention and response strategies include those that promote parenting and early childhood development; school-based bullying prevention, academic, and life and social skills development programmes; therapeutic approaches with high-risk individuals, and community- and society-level approaches like reducing access to and misuse of alcohol and firearms, hotspots and problem-oriented policing, urban upgrading, and poverty de-concentration. WHO and partners decrease youth violence through initiatives that help to identify, quantify and respond to the problem, these include: developing a package for schools-based violence prevention programmes; drawing attention to the magnitude of youth violence and the need for prevention;
translations.reference: Inajumuisha aina mbalimbali za vitendo kutoka kwa uonevu na mapigano ya kimwili, hadi unyanyasaji mkali zaidi wa kingono na kimwili hadi mauaji. Vurugu za vijana huongeza kwa kiasi kikubwa gharama za afya, ustawi na haki ya jinai; hupunguza tija; na kwa ujumla hudhoofisha muundo wa jamii. Zaidi ya vifo, majeraha na madhara ya kisaikolojia, unyanyasaji wa vijana unaweza kusababisha kuongezeka kwa tabia hatari za kiafya kama vile kuvuta sigara, matumizi mabaya ya dawa za kulevya, ngono isiyo salama na vurugu zaidi. Haya nayo yanahusishwa na magonjwa sugu ya kupumua, saratani, magonjwa ya moyo na mishipa, kisukari, mimba za utotoni na magonjwa ya zinaa. Mikakati madhubuti ya kuzuia na kukabiliana nayo ni pamoja na ile inayokuza uzazi na maendeleo ya utotoni; programu za kuzuia uonevu shuleni, kitaaluma, na maisha na ujuzi wa kijamii; mbinu za kimatibabu na watu walio katika hatari kubwa, na mbinu za ngazi ya jamii na jamii kama vile kupunguza ufikiaji na matumizi mabaya ya pombe na silaha za moto, maeneo yenye matatizo makubwa na polisi wenye matatizo, uboreshaji wa miji na kupunguza umaskini. WHO na washirika hupunguza unyanyasaji wa vijana kupitia mipango inayosaidia kutambua, kuhesabu na kukabiliana na tatizo, hizi ni pamoja na: kuandaa kifurushi cha programu za kuzuia unyanyasaji shuleni; kuzingatia ukubwa wa vurugu za vijana na haja ya kuzuia;
translations.hypothesis: Hii ni pamoja na aina mbalimbali za vitendo, kuanzia udanganyifu na mashindano, hadi mauaji na uvamizi wa kijinsia na kimwili. Vitendo vya vijana huongeza sana gharama za afya, ustawi, na utoaji haki, husababisha kupungua kwa uzalishaji, na kwa ujumla huathiri utamaduni wa jamii. Mbali na vifo, majeraha, na uharibifu kiakili, vitendo vya vijana vinaweza kusababisha tabia hatari za afya, kama vile, kutumia sigara, dawa, maharusi hatari, na vitendo zaidi vya uvamizi. Hii kwa kweli inaambatana na magonjwa ya kupumua, saratani, magonjwa ya moyo, ugonjwa wa sukari, ujauzito mapema, na magonjwa yanayotokana na maharusi. Mikakati bora za kuzuia na kujibu ni pamoja na hizo ambazo hushawishi utoto na maendeleo ya utoto; kupunguza udanganyifu katika shule, programu za kielimu na maendeleo ya ujuzi wa maisha na kijamii; njia za matibabu kwa watu wenye hatari, na mikakati kwa viwango vya jamii na kijamii, kama vile kupunguza ufikiaji na matumizi ya pombe na silaha, eneo za hatari, na utekelezaji wa polisi unaolenga masuala. Shirika la Afya ya Dunia (WHO) na washiriki wanapunguza vitendo vya vijana kupitia mashinani ambazo husaidia kutambua, kuhesabu, na kujibu tatizo hilo, ambazo ni pamoja na: kuunda paketi kwa programu za kupunguza vitendo vya vijana katika shule; kuwezesha umuhimu wa vitendo vya vijana na uhitaji wa kupunguza;

translations.source: building evidence on the scope and types of violence in different settings; developing guidance for Member States and all relevant sectors to prevent youth violence and strengthen responses to it; supporting national efforts to prevent youth violence; and collaborating with international agencies and organizations to prevent youth violence globally.
translations.reference: kujenga ushahidi juu ya upeo na aina za vurugu katika mazingira tofauti; kuendeleza miongozo kwa Nchi Wanachama na sekta zote zinazohusika ili kuzuia vurugu za vijana na kuimarisha majibu yake; kuunga mkono juhudi za kitaifa za kuzuia ghasia za vijana; na kushirikiana na mashirika na mashirika ya kimataifa ili kuzuia unyanyasaji wa vijana duniani kote.
translations.hypothesis: kuandaa ushahidi kuhusu ukubwa na aina za uvamizi katika mazingira tofauti; kuendeleza miongozo kwa mataifa na sekta husika ili kuzuia uvamizi wa vijana na kuimarisha majibu yake; kuunga mkono juhudi za kitaifa za kuzuia uvamizi wa vijana; na kushirikiana na mashirika na mashirika za kimataifa ili kuzuia uvamizi wa vijana kote ulimwenguni.

translations.source: Violence against women Overview: Violence against women – particularly intimate partner violence and sexual violence – is a major public and clinical health problem and a violation of women's human rights. It is rooted in and perpetuates gender inequalities.Globally 1 in 3 women experience physical and/or sexual violence in their lifetime, mostly by an intimate partner. This is a stark reminder of the scale of gender inequality and discrimination against women. The United Nations defines violence against women as "any act of gender-based violence that results in, or is likely to result in, physical, sexual, or mental harm or suffering to women, including threats of such acts, coercion or arbitrary deprivation of liberty, whether occurring in public or in private life. Intimate partner violence refers to behaviour by an intimate partner or ex-partner that causes physical, sexual or psychological harm, including physical aggression, sexual coercion, psychological abuse and controlling behaviours. Sexual violence is "any sexual act, attempt to obtain a sexual act, or other act directed against a person’s sexuality using coercion, by any person regardless of their relationship to the victim, in any setting. It includes rape, defined as the physically forced or otherwise coerced penetration of the vulva or anus with a penis, other body part or object." Impact: Violence against women is a global problem of pandemic proportions. It causes devastating, harm to women’s lives and that of their children.
translations.reference: Ukatili dhidi ya wanawake Muhtasari: Ukatili dhidi ya wanawake - hasa unyanyasaji wa mpenzi wa karibu na unyanyasaji wa kijinsia - ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma na kiafya na ukiukaji wa haki za binadamu za wanawake. Umejikita katika na kuendeleza ukosefu wa usawa wa kijinsia.Ulimwenguni 1 kati ya wanawake 3 hupata unyanyasaji wa kimwili na/au kingono maishani mwao, hasa na mwenzi wa karibu. Hiki ni ukumbusho kamili wa ukubwa wa usawa wa kijinsia na ubaguzi dhidi ya wanawake. Umoja wa Mataifa unafafanua unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake kama "kitendo chochote cha unyanyasaji wa kijinsia ambacho husababisha, au kinaweza kusababisha madhara ya kimwili, kingono, au kiakili au mateso kwa wanawake, ikiwa ni pamoja na vitisho vya vitendo kama hivyo, kulazimishwa au kunyimwa uhuru kiholela, iwe unafanyika hadharani au katika maisha ya kibinafsi. Vurugu za mpenzi wa karibu hurejelea tabia ya mshirika wa karibu au mpenzi wa zamani ambayo husababisha madhara ya kimwili, kingono au kisaikolojia, ikiwa ni pamoja na unyanyasaji wa kimwili, kulazimishwa kingono, unyanyasaji wa kisaikolojia na kudhibiti tabia. Unyanyasaji wa kijinsia ni "tendo lolote la ngono, jaribio la kupata tendo la ngono, au kitendo kingine kinachoelekezwa dhidi ya ujinsia wa mtu kwa kulazimishwa, na mtu yeyote bila kujali uhusiano wake na mwathiriwa, katika mazingira yoyote.
