During 2020-2021, COVID-19 resulted in a staggering 336.8 million years of life lost globally. This equates to an average of 22 years of life lost for every excess death, abruptly and tragically cutting short the lives of millions of people. Since 2000, we saw significant improvements in maternal and child health with deaths falling by one-third and one-half, respectively. The incidence of infectious diseases such as HIV, tuberculosis (TB) and malaria also declined, along with a lowered risk of premature deaths from NCDs and injuries. Together, these contributed to an increase in global life expectancy from 67 years in 2000 to 73 years in 2019.
translations.reference: Hatua za haraka zinahitajika ili kukabiliana na maendeleo yaliyokwama kwenye malengo ya maendeleo endelevu yanayohusiana na afya. WHO inachapisha toleo la 2023 la ripoti yake ya kila mwaka ya Takwimu za afya Duniani yenye takwimu kuhusu athari za janga la UVIKO-19 na takwimu za hivi karibuni kuhusu malengo ya mendeleo endelevu (SDGs) yanayohusiana na afya. Ripoti iliyo na Data hadi 2022 inasisitiza kudorora kwa maendeleo ya afya kwenye viashiria muhimu vya afya katika miaka ya hivi karibuni ikilinganishwa na mitindo iliyoonekana katika 2000-2015. Pia inatutahadharisha kuhusu tishio linaloongezeka la magonjwa yasiyo yakuambukiza (NCDs) na mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, na kutoa wito wa muitikio ulioratibiwa na kuimarishwa. UVIKO-19 imegharimu maisha na maendeleo ya kiafya, ripoti hiyo inaandika takwimu zinazohusishwa kuhusu hali ya janga hili kwenye afya kimataifa, na hivyo kuchangia kupungua kwa maendeleo kuelekea SDGs. Katika kipindi cha 2020-2021, UVIKO-19 ilisababisha vifo vya kushangaza vya miaka milioni 336.8 Duniani kote. Hii ni sawa na wastani wa miaka 22 ya maisha kupotea kwa kila kifo kupita kiasi, ghafla na kwa kusikitisha kukatisha maisha ya mamilioni ya watu. Tangu mwaka 2000, tuliona maboresho makubwa katika afya ya uzazi na watoto huku vifo vikishuka kwa theluthi moja na nusu mtawalia. Matukio ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza kama vile VVU, kifua kikuu (TB) na Malaria pia yalipungua, pamoja na kupungua kwa hatari ya vifo vya mapema kutokana na NCDs na majeruhi. Kwa pamoja, haya yalichangia ongezeko la umri wa kuishi Duniani kutoka miaka 67 mwaka 2000 hadi 73 mwaka 2019.
translations.hypothesis: Hatua muhimu inahitajika ili kushughulikia maendeleo iliyostahili katika malengo ya maendeleo yanayohusisha afya. Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) litatoa toleo la 2023 la ripoti yake ya kila mwaka kuhusu takwimu za afya, na takwimu mpya kuhusu athari ya janga la COVID-19 na takwimu za hivi karibuni kuhusu maendeleo katika malengo ya maendeleo yanayohusisha afya (SDGs). Ripoti hii, ambayo ina data hadi 2022, inaonyesha kuwa maendeleo katika afya yameathirika katika miaka الأخيرة, ikilinganishwa na mwenendo uliotishika wakati wa 2000-2015. Pia, inaonya kuhusu hatari inayoongezeka ya magonjwa isiyoweza kuambukizwa (NCDs) na mabadiliko ya ikolojia, na inatoa wito wa majibu iliyojumuishwa na imara. Gharama ya COVID-19 katika maisha iliyopotea na maendeleo ya afya Ripoti hii ina takwimu za hivi karibuni kuhusu athari ya janga kwenye afya ya dunia, ambazo zinaunga mkono kushurutika katika maendeleo ya SDGs. Katika miaka ya 2020-2021, COVID-19 ilisababisha maisha milioni 336.8 kufungwa dunia. Hii ni sawa na wastani wa miaka 22 za maisha kufungwa kwa kila mtu aliyefariki, na hivyo kusababisha maisha ya milioni ya watu kufungwa kwa njia ya ghafla na ya pemdo. Tangu 2000, tuliona michakato muhimu katika afya ya wanawake na watoto, ambapo idadi ya vifo ilipungua kwa asatu na nusu, kwa saba. Pia, idadi ya magonjwa yenye uenezaji, kama vile HIV, kuvimbi (TB) na malaria, ilipungua, pamoja na hatari iliyopungua ya vifo mapema kutokana na magonjwa isiyoweza kuambukizwa na majeraha. Pamoja, haya ilisaidia kuongezeka kwa uongozi wa maisha duniani kutoka miaka 67 mwaka wa 2000 hadi miaka 73 mwaka wa 2019.

translations.source: However, the pandemic has put many health-related indicators further off-track and contributed to inequalities in access to high-quality health care, routine immunizations and financial protection. As a result, improving trends in malaria and TB have been reversed, and fewer people were treated for neglected tropical diseases (NTDs). “The World Health Statistics is WHO’s annual check-up on the state of the world’s health. The report sends a stark message on the threat of noncommunicable diseases, which take an immense and increasing toll on lives, livelihoods, health systems, communities, economies and societies,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “The report calls for a substantial increase in investments in health and health systems to get back on track towards the Sustainable Development Goals.” NCDs ̶ an ever-increasing health threat for future generationsDespite overall health progress, the share of deaths caused annually by NCDs has grown consistently and is now claiming nearly three quarters of all lives lost each year. If this trend continues, NCDs are projected to account for about 86% of the 90 million annual deaths by mid-century; consequently, 77 million of these will be due to NCDs – a nearly 90% increase in absolute numbers since 2019. Stagnating progress calls for acceleration More recent trends show signs of slowdown in the annual rate of reduction (ARR) for many indicators. For example, the global maternal mortality ratio needs to decline by 11.6% per year between 2021 and 2030 to meet the SDG target. Similarly, the net reduction in TB incidence from 2015 to 2021 was only one-fifth of the way to the 2025 milestone of WHO’s End TB Strategy.
translations.reference: Hata hivyo, janga hili limeweka viashiria vingi vinavyohusiana na afya mbali zaidi na kuachangia ukosefu wa usawa katika upatikanaji wa huduma za afya za hali ya juu, chanjo za kawaida na ulinzi wa kifedha. Matokeo yake, uboreshaji wa mwelekeo wa Malaria na TB umebadilishwa, na watu wachache walitibiwa magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyosahaulika (NTDs). "Takwimu za afya ulimwenguni ni ukaguzi wa kila mwaka wa WHO juu ya hali ya afya ya Ulimwenguni. Rioti hiyo inatoa ujumbe mzito kuhusu tishio la magonjwa yasiyoambukiza, ambayo yanaathiri sana maisha, mifumo ya afya, jumuia, uchumi na jamii", alisema Dkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Ripoti hiyo inataka ongezeko kubwa la uwekezaji katika mifumo ya afya na afya ili kurejea katika muelekeo wa malengo ya maendeleo endelevu" NCDs - Tishio la kiafya linaloongezeka kwa vizazi vijavyo licha ya maendeleo ya afya kwa ujumla, sehemu ya vifo vinavyosababishwa na magonjwa yasiyoambukiza kila mwaka imeongezeka mara kwa mara na sasa inadai karibu robo tatu ya maisha yote hupoteza kila mwaka. Kama hali hii itaendelea, NCDs yanakadiriwa kuchangia takribani 86% ya vifo milioni 90 vya kila mwaka kufikia katikati ya karne; kwa hiyo milioni 77 kati ya hivi vinatokea na NCDs- karibu ongezeko la 90% la idadi kamili tangu 2019. Maendeleo yanayodumaa yanahitaji kuongeza kasi Mitindo ya hivi karibuni zaidi inaonyesha dalili za kushuka kwa kiwango cha mwaka cha kupunguza (ARR) kwa viashirio vingi. Kwa mfano, uwiano wa vifo vya uzazi Duniani unahitaji kupungua kwa 11.6% kwa mwaka kati ya 2021 na 2030 ili kufikia lengo la SDG. Vilevile, kupungua kwa jumla kwa matukio ya TB kutoka 2015 hadi 2021 ilikuwa moja ya tano tu ya hatua ya 2025 ya mkakati wa kukomesha kifua kikuu wa WHO.
translations.hypothesis:
Hata hivyo, janga linaathiri vigezo nyingi zinazohusiana na afya, na kusababisha usawa katika upatikanaji wa huduma bora za afya, chanjo za kawaida, na ulinzi wa kifedha. Matokeo yake, mwenendo wa uimarishaji wa magonjwa ya malaria na TB umebadilishwa, na idadi ndogo ya watu walipata matibabu kwa magonjwa ya kiafya ambayo hayoonekane. "Taarifa ya Hali ya Afya ya Dunia ni tathmini ya kila mwaka ya WHO kuhusu hali ya afya duniani. Taarifa hii inaonyesha hatari ya magonjwa ambayo siambukizana, ambazo zinaathiri maisha, uchumi, mifumo ya afya, jamii, na jamii kwa kiwango kikubwa na kinavyoongezeka," alisema Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Katibu Mkuu wa WHO. "Taarifa hii inatoa ombi la kuongezeka kwa fedha katika afya na mifumo ya afya ili kurudi kwenye malengo ya Maendeleo endelevu." Magonjwa ambayo siambukizana – hatari kubwa kwa vizazi vijavyo
Licha ya maendeleo ya jumla katika afya, asilimia ya vifo ambavyo husababishwa na magonjwa ambayo siambukizana, imekuwa ikiongezeka kila mwaka, na sasa inaathiri karibu nusu ya vifo vyote kila mwaka. Ikiwa mwenendo huu utaendelea, magonjwa ambayo siambukizana yanatarajiwa kuwa ni asilimia 86% ya vifo milioni 90 kila mwaka mwishoni mwa karne; kwa hivyo, milioni 77 kati ya hizi zitakuwa ni kutokana na magonjwa ambayo siambukizana – ongezeko la asilimia 90% tangu 2019. Maendeleo yasiyobadilika yanahitaji kasi Zaidi, mwenendo wa hivi karibuni unaonyesha dalili za kupungua kwa kasi ya kupunguza (ARR) kwa vigezo vingi. Kwa mfano, kiwango cha vifo cha wanawake cha kimataifa kinahitaji kupungua kwa asilimia 11.6 kila mwaka kati ya 2021 na 2030 ili kufikia lengo la SDG. Vilevile, kupungua kwa asilimia ya magonjwa ya TB kutoka 2015 hadi 2021 kulikuwa na asilimia moja tu ya lengo la WHO la "Kukomesha TB".

translations.source: Despite a reduction in exposure to many health risks – such as tobacco use, alcohol consumption, violence, unsafe water and sanitation, and child stunting – progress was inadequate and exposure to some risks such as air pollution remains high. Alarmingly, the prevalence of obesity is rising with no immediate sign of reversal. Furthermore, expanded access to essential health services has slowed compared to pre-2015 gains, coupled with no significant progress in reducing financial hardship due to health-care costs. This drastically limits our ability to achieve Universal Health Coverage by 2030. “The COVID-19 pandemic is an important reminder that progress is neither linear nor guaranteed,” warns Dr Samira Asma, WHO Assistant Director-General for Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact. “To stay on track towards the 2030 SDG agenda, we must act decisively and collectively to deliver a measurable impact in all countries.” This year’s report includes for the first time a dedicated section on climate change and health, and we anticipate that this will be of more relevance in the report going forward. For this issue and all other areas timely, reliable and disaggregated data are critical to track progress and improve national and global health policies. Editor’s note: The World Health Statistics report is the World Health Organization’s (WHO) annual compilation of the most recent available data on health and health-related indicators. For inquiries, contact [email protected]
translations.reference: Licha ya kupungua kwa uwezekano wa hatari nyingi za kiafya- kama vile matumizi ya Tumbaku, unywaji pombe, Vurugu, na udumavu wa watoto- maendeleo hayakuwa ya kutosha na kukabiliwa na hatari kama vile uchafuzi wa hewa bado upo juu. Cha kutisha, kuenea kwa fetma kuongezeka bila dalili ya haraka ya kubadilika. Zaidi ya hayo, kupanuka kwa upatikanaji wa huduma muhimu za afya kumepungua ikilinganishwa na mafanikio ya kabla ya 2015, pamoja na hakuna maendeleo makubwa katika kupunguza matatizo ya kifedha na gharama za huduma za afya. Hii inapunguza kwa kiasi kikubwa uwezo wetu wa kufikia huduma ya afya kwa wote ifikapo 2030. Janga la UVIKO-19 ni ukumbusho muhimu kwamba maendeleo si ya mstari wala si ya uhakika", anaonya Dkt Samira Asma, mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa WHO kwa Data, uchaguzi, na utoaji wa athari. "Ili kukaa kwenye mstari kuelekea ajenda ya 2030 ya SDG, lazima tuchukue hatua madhubuti na kwa pamoja ili kuleta athari inayoweza kupimika kwa nchi zote" Ripoti ya mwaka huu inajumuisha kwa mara ya kwanza sehemu maalumu kuhusu mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa na afya, na tunatarajia kwamba hii itakuwa na umuhimu zaidi katika ripoti inayoendelea. Kwa suala hili na maeneo mengine yote kwa wakati, Data ya kuaminika na iliyogawanywa ni muhimu kufuatilia maendeleo na kuboresha sera za afya za kitaifa na kimataifa. Dokezo la mhariri: Ripoti ya takwimu za afya Duniani ni mkusanyo wa kila mwaka wa shirika la afya Duniani (WHO) wa Data zinazopatikana hivi karibuni kuhusu afya na viashirio vinavyohusiana na afya. Kwa maswali, wasiliana na [barua pepe imehifadhiwa]
translations.hypothesis: Licha ya kupungua kwa hatari nyingi za afya – kama vile matumizi ya sigara, matumizi ya pombe, uvunjaji, maji na usafi salama, na ukubwa wa watoto – maendeleo yalikuwa hafifu, na hatari fulani, kama vile mchaka, bado ni kubwa. Kwa hofu, idadi ya uzito inaongezeka, bila dalili za moja kwa moja za kuondoa. Zaidi ya hayo, upatikanaji wa huduma muhimu za afya umelemwa مقارنة مع المكاسب kabla ya 2015, pamoja na hakuna maendeleo muhimu katika kupunguza ugumu wa kifedha kutokana na gharama za huduma za afya. Hii inafungia uwezo wetu wa kufikia Upatikanaji wa Afya kwa mwaka 2030. "Janga la COVID-19 ni tahadhari muhimu kwamba maendeleo si mara kwa mara wala inaaminika," anaripoti Dkt. Samira Asma, Msaidizi Mkuu wa WHO kwa Takwimu, Utafiti na Utendaji. "Ili kufikia lengo la 2030, lazima tuchukue hatua thabiti na pamoja ili kufikia matokeo mazuri katika nchi zote." Ripoti ya mwaka huu ina sehemu maalum kuhusu mabadiliko ya ikolojia na afya, na tunatarajia kwamba hii itakuwa muhimu zaidi katika ripoti zijazo. Kwa masuala haya na yote mengine, data za wakati, za kuaminika na za kugawa ni muhimu ili kufuatilia maendeleo na kuboresha sera za afya za kitaifa na kimataifa. Noti ya mhariri: Ripoti ya Takwimu za Afya ya Dunia ni mkusanyiko wa kila mwaka wa Shirika la Afya ya Dunia (WHO) wa data za hivi karibuni kwenye vigezo za afya. Kwa maswali, wasiliana na [email protected]

translations.source: WHO Director-General announces Global Health Leaders Awards Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot awarded for lifelong commitment to health WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus will present two lifetime achievement awards to recognize outstanding contributions to advancing global health during the high-level opening session of the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly. The Director-General’s Global Leaders Awards, which were established in 2019, will go to Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot. Both recipients are distinguished scientists and public health leaders who were closely involved in the discovery of Ebola virus disease before advancing to leadership positions in global health. The award honours their lifetime achievements in public health. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is being recognized for his innovation, leadership and ground-breaking contributions to the management and treatment of diseases such as Ebola virus disease. He has played a critical role in improving health outcomes in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the African region, and the global community. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum is the Director General of the DRC’s National Institute of Biomedical Research in Kinshasa, full Professor of Microbiology at the University of Kinshasa Medical School and the inaugural president of the Congolese Academy of Science. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum was part of the team that discovered the Zaïre ebolavirus during the first recorded outbreak in 1976, in Equateur Province, Central DRC.
translations.reference: Mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO ataoangoza tuzo za viongozi wa afya Duniani Jean-Jacques-Muyembe-Tamfum na Peter Piot walitunukiwa tuzo ya kujitolea kwa maisha yote kwa afya Mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus atatoa tuzo mbili za mafanikio ya maisha ili kutambua michango bora katika kuendeleza afya Duniani wakati wa ufunguzi wa ngazi ya juu wa mkutano wa Sabini na sita wa afya Duniani. Tuzo za mkurugenzimkuu kwa tuzo za viongozi Duniani, ambazo zilianzishwa mwaka 2019, zitakwenda kwa Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum na Peter Piot. Wapokeaji wote ni wanasayansi mashuhuri na viongozi wa afya ya umma ambao walihusika kwa karibu katika ugunduzi wa virusi vya Ebola kabla ya kusonga mbele hadi nyadhifa za uongozi katika afya ya kimataifa. Tuzo hiyo inaheshimu mafanikio yao ya maisha katika afya ya umma. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum kutoka Jamhuri ya kidemokrasia ya Kongo (DRC) anatambulika kwa uvumbuzi, uongozi na michango yake ya msingi katika usimamizi na matibabu ya magonjwa kma vile ugonjwa wa Ebola. Amekuwa na jukumu muhimu katika kuboresha matokeo ya afya katika Jamhuri ya kidemokrasia ya kongo, kanda ya Afrika, na jumuia ya kimataifa. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum ni mkurugenzi mkuu wa taasisi ya kitaifa ya utafiti wa Tiba kibiolojia ya DRC huko Kinshasa, profesa kamili wa Biolojia katika chuo kikuu cha Kinshasa shule ya afya na Rais wa kuanzishwa kwa chuo kikuu cha Sayansi cha Kongo. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum alikuwa sehemu ya timu iliyogundua virusi vya Ebola vya Zaire wakati wa mlipuko wa kwanza uliorekodiwa mnmo 1976, katika jimbo la Ikweta, katikati mwa DRC.
translations.hypothesis: Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO, Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum na Peter Piot, wamepewa Tuzo za Viongozi wa Afya Duniani. Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO, Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, atoe tuzo mbili za utambuzi kwa kutoa tuzo kwa juhudi kubwa katika kuendeleza afya ya dunia katika kikao cha juu cha Majumuisho ya Siasa ya Afya ya Dunia ya sita. Tuzo za Viongozi za WHO, ambazo zapatikana mwaka 2019, zitagawanywa kwa Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum na Peter Piot. Waathirika wote ni wanasayansi na viongozi wa afya ambao walikuwa wamehusika sana katika utambuzi wa ugonjwa wa virusi vya Ebola kabla ya kuwa viongozi katika afya ya dunia. Tuzo hii inaadhimisha mafanikio yao katika afya ya umma. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum kutoka Jamhuri ya Kidemokrasia ya Kongo (DRC), anagawanywa tuzo kwa ubunifu, uongozi, na michango muhimu katika usimamizi na matibabu ya magonjwa kama vile ugonjwa wa virusi vya Ebola. Ameweza kucheza jukumu muhimu katika kuboresha matokeo ya afya katika Jamhuri ya Kidemokrasia ya Kongo, eneo la Afrika, na jamii ya dunia. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum ni Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Taasisi ya Kitaaluma ya Utafiti ya DRC huko Kinshasa, Profesa Mkuu wa Mikrobiolojia katika Shule ya Tiba ya Chuo Kikuu cha Kinshasa, na Rais wa kwanza wa Taasisi ya Sayansi ya Kongo. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum alikuwa sehemu ya timu ambayo iligundua virusi vya Zaïre ebolavirus wakati wa mlipuko wa kwanza mwaka 1976, katika eneo la Equateur, DRC.

translations.source: He later helped design one of the first effective treatments against the virus and the deployment of experimental Ebola virus disease vaccines. He has worked tirelessly to build the Democratic Republic of the Congo’s scientific capacity, establishing multiple research facilities in the country, and has played key roles with WHO in fighting infectious diseases. Professor Piot from Belgium is being recognized for his extraordinary leadership in the response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic and other significant global health challenges. He has played a vital role in launching major global health initiatives such as UNAIDS, the Global Fund, and others that have improved health outcomes around the world. Under his leadership, UNAIDS became the chief advocate for worldwide action against AIDS, also spearheading UN reform by bringing together 10 different UN organizations. Professor Piot also played a part in the earlier discovery of the Ebola virus, and the containment of the first known outbreak, participating in field epidemiological investigations. He has chaired a number of high-level initiatives, served in numerous high-level advisory roles, including on COVID-19, and has received multiple awards. Professor Piot is the Special Advisor to the EC President, a Professor of Global Health and former Director of the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. “These outstanding individuals embody lifelong dedication, relentless advocacy, a commitment to equity, and selfless service of humanity and people’s health,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “In a time of multiple emergencies, health crises, and conflict, we recognize that leadership in public health and science are more important than ever to advance global health.
translations.reference: Baadae alisaidia kubuni moja wapo ya matibabu ya kwanza ya ufanisi dhidi ya virusi na kupelekwa kwa chanjo ya majaribio ya ugonjwa wa Ebola. Amefanya kazi bila kuchoka kujenga uwezo wa kisayansi wa Jamhuri ya kidemokraia ya Kongo, kuanzisha vituo vingi vya utafiti nchini humo, na amefanya majukumu muhimu na WHO katika kupambana na magonjwa ya kuambukiza. Profesa Piot kutoka Ubelgiji anatambuliwa kwa uongozi wa ajabu katika kukabiliana na janga la VVU/ UKIMWI na changamoto nyingine muhimu za afya Duniani. Amekuwa na jukumu muhimu katika kuzindua mipango mikuu ya afya ya kimataifa kama vile UNAIDS, mfuko wa Dunia, na mengineyo ambayo imeboresha matokeo ya afya Duniani kote. Chini ya uongozi wake, UNAIDS imekuwa mtetezi mkuu wa hatua za kimataifa dhidi ya UKIMWI, pia akiongoza mageuzi kwa kuyaleta pamoja mashirika 10 tofauti ya umoja wa mataifa. Pia profesa Piot alishiriki katika ugunduzi wa awali wa virusi vya Ebola, na kuzuia mlipuko wa kwanza ulipojulikana, alishiriki katika uga wa uchunguzi wa magonjwa. Ameongoza idadi ya mipango ya ngazi ya juu, alihudumu katika majukumu mengi ya ngazi ya juu ya ushauri, ikiwa ni pamoja na UVIKO-19 na amepokea tuzo nyingi. Profesa Piot ni mshauri muhimu wa Rais wa EC, profesa wa afya Ulimwenguni na mkurugenzi wa zamani wa shule ya London ya uafi na tiba ya kitropiki. "Watu hawa mashuhuri wanajumuisha kujitolea kwa maisha yote, utetezi usio na kuchoka, kujitolea kwa usawa, na huduma ya kujitolea kwa Ubinadamu na afya ya watu", alisema Mkurugunzi mkuu wa WHO, Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Katika wakati wa dharura nyingi, migogoro ya afya, na migogoro tunatambua kwamba uongozi katika afya ya umma na sayansi ni muhimu zaidi kuliko hapo awali ili kuendeleza afya Duniani.
translations.hypothesis: Baadaye, alisaidia kubuni mojawapo ya tiba za ufanisi dhidi ya virusi, na pia utumiaji wa chanjo za ugonjwa wa Ebola. Amefanya kazi kwa bidii ili kujenga uwezo wa kisayansi wa Jamhuri ya Kidemokrasia ya Kongo, kwa kuanzisha vituo kadhaa vya utafiti nchini, na amechukua jukumu muhimu na WHO katika kupambana na magonjwa hatari. Profesa Piot kutoka Belgium, anagawanywa heshima kwa uongozi wake wa kipekee katika majibu ya janga la HIV/AIDS na changamoto zingine muhimu za afya duniani. Ameweka jukumu muhimu katika kuanzisha mashinani za afya duniani, kama vile UNAIDS, The Global Fund, na zingine ambazo zimeimarisha matokeo ya afya duniani. Chini ya uongozi wake, UNAIDS ikawa mshauri mkuu katika hatua za kimataifa dhidi ya AIDS, na pia kuongoza ubadilishanaji wa UN kwa kuunganisha vyombo 10 tofauti vya UN. Profesa Piot pia alichukua jukumu katika utambuzi mapema wa virusi vya Ebola, na kudhibiti mlio wa kwanza, kwa kushiriki katika uchunguzi wa kliniki. Amewahi kulelea mashinani kadhaa za kiwango cha juu, na kuwa katika nafasi kadhaa za ushauri, pamoja na COVID-19, na amepokea tuzo kadhaa. Profesa Piot ni Mshauri Maalum wa Rais wa EC, Profesa wa Afya ya Kimataifa, na alikuwa Mkurugenzi wa Shule ya Afya na Sayansi ya London. "Hii ni watu wa kipekee ambao wana kujitolea kwa muda wote, kuwania kwa bidii, kujitolea kwa usawa, na huduma salama kwa binadamu na afya ya watu," alisema Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO, Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Katika wakati wa majanga kadhaa, vishawishi, na migogoro, tunaona kwamba uongozi katika afya na sayansi ni muhimu zaidi kuliko hapo awali ili kuendeleza afya ya kimataifa."

translations.source: Our awardees today personify that passion.” “Their combined stories and successes show how much the world has progressed in terms of collaboration at the global, regional and country levels to strengthen health emergency preparedness, response and resilience. These outstanding scientists will continue to inspire us every day,” he added The ceremony for the awards form part of the high-level opening session of the 76th World Health Assembly being held on Sunday 21 May 2023 in Geneva.
translations.reference: Washindi wetu leo wanawakilisha shauku hiyo" "Simulizi na mafanikio yao ya pamoja yanaonyesha jinsi ulimwengu umepiga hatua katika suala la ushirikiano katika ngazi ya kimataifa, kikanda na nchi ili kuimarisha maandalizi ya dharura ya afya, majibu na ustahimilivu. Wanasayansi hawa mashuhuri wataendelea kukutia moyo", aliongezea sherehe ya tuzo hizo ni sehemu ya kikao cha ngazi ya juu cha ufunguzi wa mkutano wa 76 wa afya Duniani unaofanyika Jumapili tarehe 21 Mei 2023 huko Jeniva.
translations.hypothesis: Washawishi wetu leo, inaonyesha shauku hiyo.” “Hadithi zao na mafanikio, zinaonyesha kiasi ambacho dunia imeboresha katika ushirikiano katika viwango vya kimataifa, za eneo, na za taifa, ili kuimarisha tayari, majibu, na uendelevu katika dharura za afya. Wataalamu hawa wa kipekee, wataendelea kuwaeleza sisi kila siku,” aliongeza. Sherehe za tuzo ni sehemu ya kikao cha juu la Mkutano wa 76 wa Jumuiya ya Afya ya Dunia, ambapo haujafanyika tarehe 21 Mei 2023 katika Geneva.

translations.source: WHO congratulates Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem WHO has validated Benin and Mali as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in WHO’s African Region to achieve this significant milestone. Countries that previously received WHO validation for trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022) and Malawi (September 2022). “WHO congratulates the health authorities of Benin and Mali and their network of global and local partners for these milestones”, said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Following Benin’s and Mali’s success, trachoma remains endemic in 23 countries in WHO’s African Region, bringing us a step closer towards the elimination target for trachoma set in the road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030.” Globally, Benin and Mali join 15 other countries that have been validated by WHO for having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These are Cambodia, China, the Gambia, Ghana, Islamic Republic of Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo and Vanuatu. Trachoma elimination using WHO’s SAFE strategy Both Benin and Mali implemented the WHO-recommended SAFE strategy to eliminate trachoma with the support of WHO and partners. The SAFE strategy consists of surgery to treat late trachoma complications; antibiotics to clear infection; facial cleanliness; and environmental improvement, particularly improving access to water and sanitation, to reduce transmission.
translations.reference: WHO inazipongeza Benini na Mali kwa kutokomeza Trakoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. WHO imeziidhinisha Benini na Mali kama zimetokomeza Trakoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma, na kuzifanya kuwa nchi za tano na sita katika kanda ya Afrika ya WHO kufikia hatua hii muhimu. Nchi ambazo hapo awali zilipokea uthibitisho wa WHO wa kutokomeza Trakoma ni Ghana (Juni 2018), Gambia (Aprili 2021), Togo(Mei 2022) na Malawi (Septemba 2022). "WHO inazipongeza mamlaka za afya za Benini na Mali na mtandao wao wa washirika wa kimataifana wa ndani kwa hatua hizi muhimu", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Kufuatia mafanikio ya Benini na Mali, Trakoma imesalia kuwa ugonjwa katika nchi 23 katika kanda ya Afrika ya WHO, na kutuletea hatua karibu na lengo la kutokomeza Trakoma lililowekwa katika muongozo wa magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyosahaulika 2021-2022". Duniani kote, Benini na Mali zinaungana na nchi nyingine 15 ambazo zimethibitishwa na WHO kwa kutokomeza Trakoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. Nchi hizo ni, Kambodia, China, Gambia, Ghana, Jamhuri ya Kiislamu ya Irani, Jamhuri ya kidemokrasia ya watu wa Lao,Malawi, Meksiko, Moroko, Myanmar, Nepali, Omani, Saudi Arabia, Togo na Vanuatu. Utokomezaji wa Trakoma kwa kutumia mkakati SALAMA wa WHO Benini na Mali zote zilitekeleza makakti SAFE uliopendekezwa na WHO ili kuondoa Trakoma kwa msaada wa WHO na washirika. Mkakati SAFE unajumuisha upasuaji wa kutibu matatizo ya Trakoma ya muda mrefu; viuavidudu ili kuondoa maambukizi; usafi wa uso; na uboreshaji wa usafi wa mazingira, hasa kuboresha upatikanaji wa maji na usafi wa mazingira, ili kupunguza maambukizi.
translations.hypothesis: WHO ina furaha kwa Benin na Mali kwa kuondoa ugonjwa wa trachoma kama tatizo la afya. WHO imethibitisha kwamba Benin na Mali wameondoa ugonjwa wa trachoma kama tatizo la afya, na hivyo kuwa nchi za tano na sita katika eneo la Afrika la WHO kufikia hatua hii muhimu. Nchi ambazo zamani zimepokea uthibitisho kutoka kwa WHO kuhusu kuondoa ugonjwa wa trachoma ni Ghana (Juni 2018), Gambia ( Aprili 2021), Togo (Mei 2022) na Malawi (Septemba 2022). "WHO ina furaha kwa vyombo vya afya vya Benin na Mali, na mtandao wao wa washirika wa kimataifa na wa ndani kwa hatua hizi," alisema Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO. "Baada ya mafanikio ya Benin na Mali, ugonjwa wa trachoma bado ni wa kawaida katika nchi 23 katika eneo la Afrika la WHO, na hutoa hatua moja kwa moja kuelekea lengo la kuondoa ugonjwa wa trachoma ambalo lina katika mpango wa uondoaji wa magonjwa ya kawaida ya Afrika ya 2021–2030." Kanda, Benin na Mali zinaungana na nchi 15 zingine ambazo zimepokea uthibitisho kutoka kwa WHO kuhusu kuondoa ugonjwa wa trachoma kama tatizo la afya. Hizi ni Cambodia, China, Gambia, Ghana, Jamhuri ya Shia ya Iran, Jamhuri ya watu ya Laos, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saud Arabia, Togo na Vanuatu. Uondoaji wa ugonjwa wa trachoma kwa kutumia mkakati wa SAFE wa WHO. Benin na Mali pia wameanzisha mkakati wa SAFE, ambao una uthibitisho kutoka kwa WHO, ili kuondoa ugonjwa wa trachoma, kwa usaidizi wa WHO na washirika. Mkakati wa SAFE una shughuli za tiba za magonjwa ya trachoma, dawa za kupunguza magonjwa, usafi wa uso, na uboreshaji wa mazingira, haswa kupunguza ufikiaji wa maji na usafi, ili kupunguza maambukizi.

translations.source: Through the International Trachoma Initiative, the antibiotic azithromycin is donated by Pfizer to elimination programmes implementing the SAFE strategy. Benin has integrated trachoma elimination interventions with those implemented against other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), under the umbrella of the National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Trachoma is the third NTD to be eliminated in Benin, after dracunculiasis (in 2009) and gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted trachoma impact and surveillance surveys and rolled out interventions to achieve elimination targets, despite security challenges in the northern regions of the country and sociopolitical upheavals in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, which therefore now joins a global group of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD. “These are impressive public health achievements,” said Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. “Benin and Mali demonstrate how strong political will, cross-sector integration, surveillance and community engagement can work in concert to achieve disease elimination.” Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma over the past few years. The number of people requiring antibiotic treatment for trachoma in the WHO African Region fell by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with an estimated 125 million people living in areas requiring interventions against the disease.
translations.reference: Kupitia mpango wa kimataifa wa Trakoma, dawa ya Azithromycin inatolewa na Pfizer kwa programu za kutokomeza zinazotekeleza mkakati SALAMA. Benini imeunganisha afua za kutokomeza Trakoma na zile zinazotekelezwa dhidi ya magonjwa mengine ya kitropiki yaliyosahaulika (NTDs), chini ya mwamvuli wa mpango wa kitaifa wa magonjwa ya kuambukiza. Trakoma ni NTD ya tatu kuondolewa nchini Benini, baada ya Dracunculiasis (mwak 2009) na Trypanosomiasisi ya Binadamu ya Afrika ya Gambia (mwaka 2021). Mali imefanya tafiti za athari za Trakoma na ufuatiliaji na kuzindua afua ili kufikia malengo ya utomezaji, licha ya changamoto za kiusalama katika mikoa ya kaskazini mwa nchi na misukumo ya kijamii na kisiasa katika miaka ya hivi karibuni. Trakoma ni NTD ya kwanza kutokomezwa nchini Mali, ambayo pia sasa inaungana na kundi la kimataifa la nchi 47 ambazo zimetokomeza angalau NTD moja. "Haya ni mafanikio ya afya ya umma", alisema Dk Ibrahima Soce Fall, mkurugenzi wa mpango wa kimataifa wa NTD wa WHO. "Benini na Mali zinaonyesha jinsi dhamira thabiti ya kisiasa, ushirikiano wa sekta mbalimbali, ufuatiliaji na ushirikishaji wa jamii inayoweza kufanya kazi kwa pamoja ili kufikia utokomezaji wa magonjwa". Mafanikio makubwa yamepatikana katika vita dhidi ya Trakoma katika miaka michache iliyopita. Idadi ya watu wanaohitaji matibabu ya viuavijasumu kwa Trakoma katika kanda ya Afrika ya WHO ilipungua kwa milioni 84, kutoka milioni 189 mwaka 2014 hadi milioni 105 kufikia Juni 2022. Maelezo kwa mhariri: Trakoma bado ni tatizo la afya ya umma katika nchi 41 (hadi Juni 2022) huku takribani watu milioni 125 wakiishi katika maeneo yanayohitaji afua dhidi ya ugonjwa huo.
translations.hypothesis: Kupitia Minitaj, azithromycin, ambayo ni dawa ya bakteria, inatozwa na Pfizer kwa programu za kupunguza magonjwa ya trachoma zinazotumia mkakati wa SAFE. Benini imejumuisha programu za kupunguza magonjwa ya trachoma na programu za kupunguza magonjwa mingine ya kiafya ambayo hayatafanywa, chini ya utawala wa Mpango wa Kitaifa wa Magonjwaambukizivu. Trachoma ni mojawapo ya magonjwa ya kiafya ambayo yanaweza kupunguzwa, baada ya dracunculiasis (mnamo 2009) na gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (mnamo 2021). Mali imefanya uchunguzi wa athari na ufuatiliaji wa trachoma, na imeweka sera za kupunguza magonjwa, licha ya changamoto za usalama katika eneo la kaskazini, na mabadiliko ya kisiasa. Trachoma ni mojawapo ya magonjwa ya kiafya ambayo yanaweza kupunguzwa, na hivyo sasa inaungana na kikundi cha mataifa 47 duniani ambapo magonjwa ya kiafya moja au zaidi yana kupunguzwa. "Hii ni mafanikio muhimu katika afya," alisema Dkt. Ibrahima Socé Fall, Mkurugenzi wa Mpango wa Kimataifa wa Magonjwa ya Kiafya wa WHO. "Benini na Mali zinaonyesha jinsi nia imara ya kisiasa, ushirikiano wa sekta, ufuatiliaji, na ushirikishwaji wa jamii zinaweza kufanya kazi pamoja ili kufikia malengo ya kupunguza magonjwa." Maendeleo muhimu yamepatikana katika vita dhidi ya trachoma katika miaka الأخيرة. Idadi ya watu ambao wanahitaji matibabu ya bakteria kwa magonjwa ya trachoma katika eneo la Afrika la WHO imepungua, kutoka milioni 189 mnamo 2014 hadi milioni 105 mnamo Juni 2022. Tafadhali kwa mhariri: Trachoma bado ni tatizo la afya katika 41 mataifa (mnamo Juni 2022), na idadi takribani ya watu milioni 125 wanaishi katika maeneo ambapo kuna mahitaji ya programu za kupunguza magonjwa.

translations.source: Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person. Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.reference: Trakoma hupatikana hasa katika maeneo ya maskini zaidi na ya vijijini zaidi ya Afrika, Amerika ya kati na Kusini, Asia, Pasifiki ya Magharibi na Mashariki ya kati. WHO kanda ya Afrika imeathiriwa kwa kiasi kikubwa na Trakoma huku watu milioni 105 wakiishi katika maeneo hatarishi, ambayo inawakilisha 84% ya wingi wa Trakoma Duniani. Trakoma ni chanzo kikuu cha maradhi ya upofu Duniani kote. Ugonjwa husababishwa kwa kuambukizwa na bakteria Clamydia Trachomatis. Maambukizi huama kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kupitia vidole vilivyochafuliwa, Fomites na nzi ambao wamegusana na uchafu kutoka katika macho au pua ya mtu aliyeambukizwa. Mambo hatarishi ya kimazingira kwa maambukizi ya Trakoma ni pamoja na hali duni ya usafi, kaya zenye msongamano mkubwa wa watu, kutopata maji ya kutosha au matumizi ya vifaa vya usafi wa mazingira. Mnamo 1996, WHO ilizindua muungano wa WHO wa kutokomeza Trakoma Duniani kufikia mwaka 2020 (GET 2020). Pamoja na washirika wengine katika muungano, WHO inaunga mkono utekelezaji wa mkakati SAFE nchini na uimarishaji wa uwezo wa kitaifa kupitia tathmini ya magonjwa, usimamiaji, ufuatiliaji, tathmini ya mradi na uhamasishaji wa rasilimali unaochangia kutokomeza Trakoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. Muongozo, ulioidhinishwa na baraza la afya Duniani mwaka 2020 kupitia uamuzi wake wa 73(33), inaweka 2030 kama tarehe mpya inayolengwa ya kutokomezwa Duniani.
translations.hypothesis: Trachoma kwa kawaida hupatikana katika maeneo maridadi na mijini zaidi katika Afrika, Amerika ya Mashariki na Kusini, Asia, Pasifiki Magharibi na Mashariki. Eneo la Afrika la WHO linaathiriwa sana na trachoma, na watu milioni 105 wanaishi katika maeneo hatarishi, ambayo ni 84% ya mzigo wa ugonjwa wa trachoma duniani. Trachoma ni sababu kuu ya ugonjwa wa kuona duniani. Ugonjwa huu unaasababishwa na ugonjwa wa bakteria, *Chlamydia trachomatis*. Ugonjwa unaenea kutoka mtu hadi mtu kupitia vidole, vitu vinavyoshirikiwa, na nyuki ambazo vimewasiliana na maji kutoka macho au pua ya mtu aliyepata ugonjwa. Mambo ya hatari za mazingira kwa usambazaji wa trachoma ni usafi mbaya, makazi yenye watu wengi, upatikanaji mdogo wa maji au matumizi ya vifaa vya usafi. Mnamo 1996, WHO ilianzisha "WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020)". Pamoja na washiriki wengine katika umo huo, WHO inasaidia utekelezaji wa sera ya SAFE nchi na kuimarisha uwezo wa kitaifa kupitia tathmini ya epidemiolojia,wachungaji, utambuzi, tathmini ya miradi na utengenezaji wa rasilimali, ili kushirikisha katika kuondoa ugonjwa wa trachoma kama tatizo la afya. Mendela, iliyoidhinishwa na Kamati ya Afya ya Dunia mwaka 2020 kupitia uamuzi 73(33), inaweka mwaka 2030 kama tarehe mpya ya lengo kwa uondoaji wa ugonjwa wa trachoma duniani.

translations.source: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.
translations.reference: Watoto milioni 152 walizaliwa njiti katika muongo uliopita Kati ya watoto 10 wanaozaliwa, 1 ni njiti - na kila sekunde 40, mtoto 1 kati ya hao hufariki Viwango vya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati havijabadilika katika muongo mmoja uliopita katika eneo lolote la Dunia. Athari za migogoro, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, UVIKO-19 zinaongeza hatari kwa wanawake na watoto kila mahali. Hatua zinahitajika haraka ili kuboresha uzuiaji wa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, pamoja na utunzaji bora kwa watoto walioathiriwa na familia zao. Inakadiriwa watoto milioni 13.4 walizaliwa Njiti mwaka 2020, na karibu milioni 1 wakifa kutokana na matatizo ya unjiti, kulingana na ripoti mpya iliyotolewa leo na mashirika ya umoja wa mataifa. Hii ni sawa na mtoto 1 kati ya 10 aliyezaliwa mapema (kabla ya wiki 37 za ujauzito) Duniani kote. Alizaliwa hivi karibuni: Muongo wa hatua juu ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, iliyotolewa na shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) na shirika la Umoja wa mataifa la kuhudumia watoto (UNICEF) pamoja na PMNCH - muungano mkubwa zaidi Duniani wa wanawake, watoto, na vijana, inapiga kengele kwenye "Dharura ya kimya kimya" ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, ambayo haijatambuliwa kwa muda mrefu katika kiwango na ukali wake, ambayo inazuia maendeleo katika kuboresha afya na maisha ya watoto. Ripoti hiyo inajumuisha makadirio yaliyosasishwa kutoka WHO na UNICEF, iliyotayarishwa na shule ya London ya usafi na madawa ya kitropiki, kuhusu kuenea kwa watoto wanaozaliwa kabla ya wakati. Kwa ujumla, ripoti inagundua kuwa viwango vya kuzaliwa Njiti havijabadilika katika eneo lolote Duniani katika muongo mmoja uliopita, na watoto milioni 152 walio katika mazingira magumu walizaliwa hivi karibuni kutoka 2010 hadi 2020.
translations.hypothesis: Kumbwa milioni 152 zilizaliwa mapema katika miaka ya hivi majuzi. Kwa kila 10 watoto walioaliwa, 1 ni wa mapema – na kila sekunde 40, 1 wa watoto hao kufa. Kiwango cha kuzaliwa mapema hakijabadilika katika miaka ya hivi majuzi katika kila eneo duniani. Athari za vita, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, na COVID-19 zinaongeza hatari kwa wanawake na watoto kila mahali. Ni muhimu kuchukua hatua haraka ili kuboresha kuzuia kuzaliwa mapema, pamoja na utunzaji bora kwa watoto na familia zao. Kulingana na ripoti mpya iliyotolewa na mashirika na washiriki wa Umoja wa Mataifa leo, takriban watoto milioni 13.4 walizaliwa mapema mwaka wa 2020, na takriban milioni 1 wamefariki kutokana na matatizo ya kuzaliwa mapema. Hii ni sawa na takriban 1 katika kila 10 watoto walioaliwa mapema (kabla ya wiki 37 za ujauzito) duniani. Kuzaliwa mapema: miaka ya hatua katika kuzaliwa mapema, iliyotengenezwa na Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) na Fonde ya Watoto ya Umoja wa Mataifa (UNICEF), pamoja na PMNCH – chama kubwa zaidi duniani kwa wanawake, watoto, na vijana, inaonya kuhusu "dharura isiyo ya kawaida" ya kuzaliwa mapema, ambayo haijulikwa sana katika ukubwa na umuhimu wake, na inathamani maendeleo ya kuboresha afya na maisha ya watoto. Ripoti ina takwimu za hivi majuzi kutoka kwa WHO na UNICEF, ambazo zimetayarishwa na Shule ya Afya na Sayansi ya Kiafya ya London, kuhusu idadi ya kuzaliwa mapema. Katika jumla, inaonyesha kuwa kiwango cha kuzaliwa mapema hakijabadilika katika eneo lolote duniani katika miaka ya hivi majuzi, na takriban watoto milioni 152 wamealiwa mapema kutoka mwaka wa 2010 hadi 2020.

translations.source: Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday. Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays. Survival gaps by region, income, race Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies. Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive. The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally. The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere.
translations.reference: Kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati sasa ndio sababu kuu ya vifo vya watoto, ikichukua zaidi ya 1 kati ya vifo 5 vya watoto vinavyotekea kabla ya miaka 5 ya kuzaliwa. Waathirika wa kuzaliwa Njiti hukabiliwa na matokeo ya afya ya maisha yote, na kuongezeka uwezekano wa ulemavu na uchelewaji wa maendeleo. Mapengo ya kuishi kwa kanda, kipato, rangi Kutokana na ripoti ya kihistoria kuhusu mada mnamo 2012, ripoti hii mpya ya "Muongozo" wa kuzaliwa hivi karibuni inatoa muhtasari wa kina wa kuenea kwa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati na athari zake kwa wanawake, familia, jamii na uchumi. Mara nyingi mno, mahali ambapo watoto huzaliwa huamua kama wataishi. Ripoti hiyo inabainisha kuwa ni mtoto 1 kati ya 10 wanaozaliwa mapema kabla ya wakati (chini ya wiki 28) wanaishi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini, ikilinganishwa na zaidi ya 9 kati ya 10 katika nchi zenye kipato cha juu. Pengo la ukosefu wa usawa kuhusiana na rangi, kabila, mapato, ufikiaji wa huduma bora huamua uwezekano wa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, kifo, na ulemavu, hata katika nchi zenye kipato cha juu. Kusini mwa Asia na Afrika kusini mwa jangwa la Sahara ndizo zinazo viwango vya juu zaidi vya kuzaliwa Njiti, na watoto wachanga katika maeneo haya wanakabiliwa na hatari kubwa zaidi ya vifo. Kwa pamoja, maeneo haya mawili yanachaangia zaidi ya 65% ya watoto wanaozaliwa kabla ya wakati wao kukamilika Duniani. Pia ripoti hiyo inaangazia kwamba athari za migogoro, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, na uharibifu wa mazingira, UVIKO-19, na kupanda kwa gharama za maisha kunaongeza hatari kwa wanawake na watoto kila mahali.
translations.hypothesis: Kujifungua mapema ni sababu kuu ya vifo vya watoto, na inaathiri zaidi ya 1 katika 5 vya vifo vyote vya watoto ambavyo hutokea kabla ya kuzaliwa. Watoto ambao hujifungua mapema wanaweza kukabiliana na matokeo ya kiafya kwa muda mrefu, na kuna uwezekano wa juu wa kuwa na ulemavu na vichelewache katika maendeleo. Faharisi kwa kanda, kipato, kabila Kulingana na ripoti muhimu kuhusu mada hiyo mwaka wa 2012, ripoti hii mpya kuhusu "kujifungua mapema" inatoa muhtasari kamili kuhusu idadi ya kujifungua mapema na athari yake kubwa kwa wanawake, familia, jamii na uchumi. Kawaida, mahali ambapo watoto hujifungua, husababisha ikiwa watakuwa hai. Ripoti inaonyesha kwamba, katika nchi na kipato cha chini, tu 1 katika 10 wa watoto ambao hujifungua mapema (duni ya wiki 28) hujipona, ikilinganishwa na zaidi ya 9 katika 10 katika nchi na kipato cha juu. Utafauti wa kiuchumi, wa kabila, wa kipato, na ufikiaji wa huduma bora, unaathiri uwezekano wa kujifungua mapema, vifo, na ulemavu, hata katika nchi na kipato cha juu. Asia ya Kusini na Afrika ya Mashariki zina idadi kubwa ya kujifungua mapema, na watoto ambao hujifungua mapema katika maeneo haya wana hatari kubwa ya kufariki. Pamoja, maeneo haya mawili yanaathiri zaidi ya 65% ya kujifungua mapema ulimwenguni. Ripoti pia inaonyesha kwamba, athari za migogoro, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, na uharibifu wa mazingira, COVID-19, na gharama za maisha zinazoongezeka, zinaongeza hatari kwa wanawake na watoto kila mahali.

translations.source: For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year. Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report. Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births. This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth. Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism The past decade has also seen a growth of community activism on preterm birth and stillbirth prevention, driven by networks of parents, health professionals, academia, civil society, and others. Throughout the world, groups for affected families of preterm birth have been at the forefront of advocating for access to better care and policy change and supporting other families. Leading up to the International Maternal Newborn Health Conference, in Cape Town, South Africa from 8-11 May, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA and PMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve care for women and newborns and mitigate risks from preterm births: Increased investments: Mobilizing international and domestic resources to optimize maternal and newborn health, ensuring high quality care is provided when and where it is needed. Accelerated implementation: Meeting country targets for progress through implementing established national policies for maternal and newborn care.
translations.reference: Kwa mfano, uchafuzi wa hewa unakadiriwa kuchangia watoto milioni 6 wanaozaliwa Njiti kila mwaka. Karibu mtoto 1 kati ya 10 anayezaliwa kabla ya wakati analiwa katika nchi 10 dhaifu zaidi zilizoathiriwa na majanga ya kibinadamu, kulingana na uchambuzi mpya katika ripoti hiyo. Hatari za afya ya Uzazi, kama vile mimba za utotoni na pre-eclampsia, zinahusishwa kwa karibu na kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati. Hii inasisitiza haya ya kuhakikisha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya ya ngono na uzazi, ikiwa ni pamoja na upangaji uzazi bora, na utunzaji wa hali ya juu wakati wa ujauzito na wakati wa kujifungua. Ajenda ya utekelezaji: uwekezaji zaidi wa nchi na harakati zinazoongozwa na wazazi Pia muuongo uliopita umeshuhudia ukuaji wa harakati wa jamii juu ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati na kuzuia uzazi, unaoendeshwa na mitandao ya wazazi, wataalamu wa afya, wasomi, Asasi za kiraia, na wengine. Kote ulimwenguni, Makundi ya familia zilizoathiriwa za kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati wao wamekuwa mstari wa mbele kutetea upatikanaji wa matunzo bora na mabadiliko ya sera na kusaidia familia nyingine. Kuelekea kongamano la kimataifa la afya ya mama wajawazito, huko Cape Town, Afrika Kusini, kuanzia tarehe 8-11 Mei, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA na PMMNCH wanatoa wito wa kuchukua hatua zifuatazo ili kuboresha huduma kwa wanawake na watoto wachanga na kupunguza hatari zinazotokana na kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati: Ongezeko la uwekezaji: Kuhamasisha rasilimali za kimataifa na za ndani ili kuboresha afya ya uzazi na watoto wachanga, kuhakikisha utunzaji wa hali ya juu unatolewa wakati na mahali unapohitajika. Kuharakisha utekelezaji: Kufikia malengo ya nchi kwa ajili ya maendeleo kupitia sera zilizowekwa za kitaifa za utunzaji wa uzazi na watoto wachanga.
translations.hypothesis: Kwa mfano, mchakafu wa hewa unaelezwa kuwa unaunga mkono kuzaliwa mapema kwa milioni 6 kila mwaka. Kitaro, takriban moja kati ya kumi wa watoto waliozaliwa mapema, huzaulwa katika nchi 10 zinazoathiriwa sana na viongozi, kulingana na uchambuzi mpya katika ripoti. Hatari za afya ya mama, kama vile ujauzito wa watoto na ujauzito wa mapema, zina uhusiano mkubwa na kuzaliwa mapema. Hii inaonyesha umuhimu wa kuhakikisha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya ya kiafamia, pamoja na mpango wa familia, na huduma bora wakati wa ujauzito na wakati wa kuzaliwa. Mpango wa hatua: uwekezaji zaidi wa nchi na utoaji wa nguvu na familia: Miondo mingi pia imeboreshwa katika utoaji wa nguvu kuhusu kuzuia kuzaliwa mapema na kuzaliwa bila maisha, iliyoendeshwa na mitandao ya wazazi, wataalamu wa afya, taasisi za elimu, jamii, na wengine. Kote ulimwenguni, vikundi vya familia zinazohusika na kuzaliwa mapema, vimekuwa katika hali ya kutoa ushauri kuhusu upatikanaji wa huduma bora na mabadiliko ya sera, na pia kusaidia familia zingine. Kabla ya Kongamano la Kimataifa la Afya ya Mama na Mtoto, huko Cape Town, Afrika Kusini, kutoka Mei 8 hadi Mei 11, Shirika la Afya (WHO), UNICEF, UNFPA na PMNCH wanaendelea kwa hatua zifuatazo ili kuboresha huduma kwa wanawake na watoto, na kupunguza hatari za kuzaliwa mapema: Uwekezaji wa ziada: Kutumia rasilimali za kimataifa na za ndani ili kuboresha afya ya mama na watoto, na kuhakikisha kuwa huduma bora zinatozwa wakati na mahali ambapo zinahitajika. Utekelezaji wa haraka: Kutimiza malengo ya nchi kwa maendeleo kupitia utekelezaji wa sera za kitaifa za huduma ya mama na watoto.

translations.source: Integration across sectors: Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors; strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course; and advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems. Locally driven innovation: Investing in locally led innovation and research to support improvements in quality of care and equity in access. Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Co-lead, Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon. While some regions are more affected, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Greater investment in the care of vulnerable newborns can save millions of families from heartbreak. More work is also needed to prevent preterm birth, which will also improve progress in reducing stillbirths and maternal deaths. Together these twin tracks of preterm prevention and care will produce healthier individuals and societies to deliver on economic and social development. Our next generation depends on us all acting now -- the investment may not be small but the returns on this investment will be major for every country"
translations.reference: Ushirikiano katika sekta zote: Kukuza elimu kupitia mzunguko wa maisha; kusaidia uwekezaji mzuri wa kiuchumi; ufadhili wa pamoja katika sekta zote; kuimarisha majibu ya kukabiliana na hali ya hewa katika kipindi chote cha maisha; na kuendeleza uratibu na uthabiti wa mifumo ya dharura. Uvumbuzi unaoendeshwa ndani ya nchi: Kuwekeza katika uvumbuzi na utafiti unaoongozwa na ndani ili kusaidia uboreshaji wa ubora wa utunzaji na usawa katika ufikiaji. Nukuu: Profesa Joy Lawn, Shule ya London ya usafi na Tiba ya kitropiki Kiongozi mwenza, Alizaliwa hivi karibuni: Muongo wa hatua juu ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati (2023) na kuzaliwa hivi karibuni (2012) "Ripoti hii mpya inaonyesha kuwa gharama ya kutochukua hatua katika muongo uliopita ilikuwa watoto milioni 152 walizaliwa mapema sana. Wakati baadhi ya maeneo yameathiriwa zaidi, uzazi wa mapema unatishia maendeleo ya kiafya katika kila nchi. Uwekezaji mkubwa katika utunzaji wa watoto wachanga walio katika mazingira magumu unaweza kuokoa mamilioni ya familia kutokana na huzuni. Pia kazi zaidi inahitajika ili kuzuia kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, ambayo pia itaboresha maendeleo katika kupunguza vifo vya uzazi na vifo vya watoto wachanga. Kwa pamoja kauli mbizi hizi za kuzuia na kutunzaa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati zitazalisha watu binafsi na jamii zenye afya zaidi ili kuleta maendeleo ya kiuchumi na kijamii. Kizazi chetu kijacho kinatutegemea sisi sote kuchukua hatua sasa -- uwekezaji unaweza usiwe mdogo lakini faida ya uwekezaji huu itakuwa kubwa kwa nchi"
translations.hypothesis: Ushirikiano katika sekta mbalimbali: Kushawishi elimu katika hatua zote za maisha; kusaidia matumizi bora za uchumi, kwa ushirikiano katika sekta mbalimbali; kuimarisha majibu ya kukabiliana na mabadiliko ya hali; na kuendeleza ushirikiano na uendelezi wa mifumo ya dharura. Uendelevu wa ndani: Kuwekeza katika uvumbuzi na utafiti unaoongozwa ndani, ili kusaidia kuboresha ubora wa huduma na usawa katika upatikanaji. Maoni: Prof. Joy Lawn, Shule ya Afya na Sayansi ya Afrika, Mshiriki, "Tulazwa mapema: miongo mitano ya hatua (2023) na "Tulazwa mapema (2012) "Taarifa hii mpya inaonyesha kwamba gharama ya kutotenda katika miongo mitano ilikuwa watoto milioni 152 walazwa mapema. Ingawa baadhi yaeneo zinaathiriwa zaidi, kuzaliwa mapema kunathwamini maendeleo ya afya katika kila nchi. Uwekezaji zaidi katika huduma kwa watoto wachanga wanaohitaji unaweza kuokoa familia milioni kutoka kwenye majasho. Pia, ni muhimu kufanya kazi zaidi ili kuzuia kuzaliwa mapema, ambayo pia itasaidia kuboresha maendeleo katika kupunguza kuzaliwa bila maisha na vifo vya wanawake. Pamoja, njia hizi mbili za kuzuia na kutunza watoto walazwa mapema zitatoa watu na jamii afya zaidi, ili kufikia maendeleo ya kiuchumi na kijamii. Taarifa yetu inayokuja inategemea kwamba sisi sote tutachukua hatua sasa – Uwekezaji si mdogo, lakini matumizi ya uwekezaji huu yanaweza kuwa muhimu kwa kila nchi.

translations.source: Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon co-author and national lead of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) “There is now no excuse for silence. We now have in our hands the devices and the knowledge to change outcomes for our most vulnerable newborns. I am proud to see my government now investing and committed to implementation across even rural areas. The next decade can and must be different for those facing preterm birth here and everywhere.” Dr Anshu Banerjee, Director for Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health and Ageing at WHO “Ensuring quality care for these tiniest, most vulnerable babies and their families is absolutely imperative for improving child health and survival. Progress is also needed to help prevent preterm births -- this means every woman must be able to access quality health services before and during pregnancy to identify and manage risks.” Steven Lauwerier, Director of Health (a.i.), UNICEF “After every preterm death is a trail of loss and heartbreak.
translations.reference: Dk Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon mwandishi mwenza na kiongozi wa kitaifa wa Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) "Sasa hakuna kisingizio cha kunyamaza. Sasa tuna mikononi mwetu vifaa na maarifa ya kubadilisha matokeo kwa watoto wetu wachanga walio katika mazingira magumu zaidi. Najivunia kuona serikali yangu sasa inawekeza na kujitolea utekelezaji hata maeneo ya vijijini. Muongo ujao uanaweza na lazima uwe tofauti kwa wale wanaokabiliwa na kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati hapa na kila mahali" Anshu Banerjee, mkurugenzi wa afya ya uzazi, watoto wachanga, watoto na vijana ndani ya WHO "Kuhakikisha huduma bora kwa watoto hawa wadogo zaidi, walio katika mazingira magumu zaidi na familia zao ni muhimu sana kwa kuboresha afya na maisha ya mtoto. Pia maendeleo yanahitajika ili kusaidia kuzuia uzazi kabla ya wakati -- hii inamaana kwamba kila mwanamke lazima aweze kupata huduma bora za afya kabla na wakati wa ujauzito ili kutambua na kudhibiti hatari" Steven Lauwerier, mkurugenzi wa afya (a.i.), UNICEF "Baada ya kila kifo cha mapema ni njia ya kupotez na kuvunjika moyo.
translations.hypothesis: Dato Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Mwandishi wa kitabu "Tulazwa mapema" na mwenyekiti wa taifa wa "Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360)": "Sasa hakuna sababu ya kuendelea kusitika. Sasa tuna vifaa na maarifa ambazo zitatusaidia kubadilisha matokeo kwa watoto wetu dhaifu zaidi. Natumai sana kuona serikali yangu sasa inatumia rasilimali na imejitolea kutumia teknolojia katika maeneo yote, hata kwenye maeneo ya mijini. Decadi ijayo inaweza na lazima kuwa tofauti kwa wale wanaozaliwa mapema hapa na kila mahali." Dk Anshu Banerjee, Mkurugenzi wa Afya ya Mama, Watoto, na Watu Wazima na Uzee katika WHO: "Kuhakikisha huduma bora kwa watoto hawa wadogo na dhaifu, na familia zao, ni muhimu sana kwa kuboresha afya ya watoto na kuwakuza. Pia, ni muhimu kutumia hatua za kuzuia kuzaliwa mapema – hii inamaanisha kuwa kila mama lazima anaweza kupata huduma bora za afya kabla na wakati wa ujauzito ili kutambua na kudhibiti hatari." Steven Lauwerier, Mkurugenzi wa Afya (wa muda), UNICEF: "Baada ya kila kuzaliwa mapema, kuna hasara na huzuni.

translations.source: Despite the many advances the world has made in the past decade, we have made no progress in reducing the number of small babies born too soon or averting the risk of their death. The toll is devasting. It’s time we improve access to care for pregnant mothers and preterm infants and ensure every child gets a healthy start and thrives in life.” Helga Fogstad, Executive Director, PMNCH “Born too soon underlines why we must increase investment and accountability for preterm birth – the world’s largest cause of death of children under the age of five. Progress is flatlining for maternal and newborn health, as well as the prevention of stillbirths, and is now pushed back further through the devastating combination of COVID-19, climate change, expanding conflicts and rising living costs. “By working together in partnership – governments, donors, the private sector, civil society, parents, and health professionals – we can sound the alarm about this “silent emergency”, and bring preterm prevention and care efforts to the forefront of national health and development efforts, building human capital by supporting families, societies and economies everywhere. ”Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: parents of Jalen Foster, and founders of Jalen’s Gift Foundation “Our foundation was founded after the loss of our son, Jalen Foster, who was a preterm stillbirth at 27 weeks due to prenatal onset of a bacteria known as group B streptococcus. Losing our son was devastating, and there was no support in the community and that’s when we decided to provide a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a baby.
translations.reference: Licha ya maendele mengi ambayo ulimwengu umefanya katika muongo mmoja uliopita, hataujafanya maendeleo yoyote katika kupunguza idadi ya watoto wadogo wanaozaliwa mapema au kuepusha hatari ya kifo chao. Uhuru ni mbaya. Ni wakati wa kuboresha upatikanaji wa huduma kwa mama wajawazito na watoto wachanga wanaozaliwa kabla ya wakati na kuhakikisha kila mtoto anapata mwanzo mzuri na kustawi maishani" Helga Fogstad, mkurugenzi mtendaji, PMNCH "Kuzaliwa mapema kunasisitiza kwa nini ni lazima kuongeza uwekezaji na uwajibikaji kwa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, sababu kubwa zaidi ya vifo vya watoto chini ya umri wa miaka mitano, Duniani. Maendeleo yanadorora kwa afya ya uzazi na watoto wachanga, vilevile kuzuia watoto wanaokufa, na sasa yamerejeshwa nyuma zaidi kupitia mchanganyiko mbaya wa UVIKO_19, mabadiliko ya hewa, migogoro inayoongezeka na kupanda kwa gharama za maisha. "Kwa kufanya kazi pamoja kwa ushirikiano- serikali, wafadhili, sekta binafsi, asasi za kiraia, wazazi, na wataalamu wa afya - tunaweza kupinga kengele kuhusu "Dharura hii ya kimya", na kuleta jitihada za kuzuia na matunzo mbele ya afya ya kitaifa na juhudi za maendeleo, kujenga mtaji wa watu kwa kusaidia familia, jamii na uchumi kila mahali". "Gabriela na Jerome Foster, Las Vegaz, Marekani: wazazi wa Janel Foster, na waanzilishi wa Wakafu wa Jalen Gift. "Wakfu wetu ulianzishwa baada ya kufiwa na mtoto wetu wa kiume, Jalen Foster, ambae alijifungua mtoto Njiti akiwa na wiki 27 kutokana na kutokea kwa Bakteria wanaojulikana kama Streptococcus kundi B. Kumpoteza mtoto wetu wa kiume kulituumiza sana, na hakukuwa na usaidizi katika jamii na ndipo tulipoamua kutoa mahali pa faraja na matumaini kwa familia ambazo zimepoteza mtoto.
translations.hypothesis: Licha ya maendeleo mengi ambayo dunia imefanya katika miaka ya hivi, hatujafanya maendeleo yoyote katika kupunguza idadi ya watoto wachanga ambao huja mapema, au katika kuepuka hatari ya kufariki. Hali hii ni mbaya sana. Ni wakati wa kuboresha ufikiaji wa huduma kwa mama wanaokwenda kujifungua na watoto wachanga, na kuhakikisha kwamba kila mtoto anapata mwanzo wa afya na anaendelea kuishi. – Helga Fogstad, Mkurugenzi Mkuu, PMNCH "Watoto wachanga huonyesha kwa sababu lazima tuweke zaidi na ufuatiliaji katika kuzalisha watoto wachanga – sababu kubwa zaidi ya vifo vya watoto chini ya umri wa miaka mitano. Maendeleo katika afya ya mama na watoto wachanga, pamoja na kupunguza vifo vya watoto wachanga, yamepopa, na sasa imepungua zaidi kutokana na mchanganyiko mbaya la COVID-19, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, migogoro inayoongezeka, na gharama za maisha zinazoongezeka. "Kwa kufanya kazi pamoja – vyama vya serikali, washiriki, sekta binafsi, jamii, wazazi, na wataalamu wa afya – tunaweza kuwatazamia "dharura hii zisichojulikana", na kuleta juhudi za kuzuia na kutunza watoto wachanga katika mwanzo wa juhudi za afya na maendeleo za kitaifa, kwa kujenga rasilimali za binadamu kwa kusaidia familia, jamii, na uchumi kila mahali. ”Gabriela na Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, Marekani: wazazi wa Jalen Foster, na waanzilishi wa Jalen's Gift Foundation "Tunaanzisha taasisi yetu baada ya kupoteza mwanao, Jalen Foster, ambaye alikuwa mtoto wachanga ambaye alifariki wakati wa wiki 27, kutokana na ugonjwa wa bakteria anayejulikana kama streptococcus ya aina B. Kupoteza mwanao kulikuwa na msongo mkubwa, na hakukuwa na msaada katika jamii, na ndiyo sababu tuliamua kutoa nafasi ya faraja na matumaini kwa familia ambazo zimepoteza mtoto.

translations.source: We didn’t want other families to feel like we did – alone. So, we started Jalen’s Gift Foundation to help other families going through a loss, to provide multiple free services, such as photograph, grief support and help towards memorial services.” ***Download the Born too soon media resources
translations.reference: Hatukutaka familia nyingine zijisikie kama sisi- peke yao. Kwa hiyo, tulianzisha Wakfu wa Jalen Gift ili kusaidia huduma nyingi bila malipo, kama vile picha, usaidizi wa huzuni na usaidizi kuelekea huduma za ukumbusho" *** Pakua Born too Soon rasilimali za media
translations.hypothesis: Hatutaki familia zingine kujisikia sawa na sisi – kuwa peke. Kwa hiyo, tulianzisha Jalen’s Gift Foundation ili kusaidia familia zingine zinazokumbana na hasara, kutoa huduma nyingi za bure, kama vile picha, msaada wa majasoro, na msaada katika sherehe za kumbukumbu. ***Pakua rasilimali za media za "Born too soon"

translations.source: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict. These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need.
translations.reference: UAE na WHO zinapeleka vifaa muhimu vya matibabu nchini sudani Umoja wa falme za kiarabu (UAE) na shirika la afya Duniani zimewasilisha tani 30 za vifaa vya dharura vya matibabu nchini sudani leo. Ndege iliyobeba vifaa vya matibabu ya majeraha, upasuaji wa dharura, dawa muhimu iliwasili katika uwanja wa ndege wa Port sudan mapema leo asubuhi. Shehena hiyo, yenye thamani ya Dola za kimarekani 444,000, ni ya kwanza ambayo WHO imeweza kuwasilisha kwa ndege hadi Sudani tangu kuzuka kwa mzozo huo. WHO ilisambaza vifaa kwa vituo vya afya kabla ya kuongezeka kwa mgogoro. Viliishiwa baada ya siku chache kutokana idadi ya waliojeruhiwa. Kutumwa kwa ndege hiyo kunakuja kama sehemu ya juhudi za umoja wa falme za kiarabu kuwaunga mkono watu wa Sudani na kuakisi uhusiano wake wa kina na Sudani, hasa kwa kuzingatia hali ya sasa ya nchi hiyo. Pia misaada hiyo ni upanuzi wa maono ya kibinadamu ya UAE na inasisitiza uhusiano wake na nchi nyingie na kulingana na udugu wa kibinadamu na kujitolea kwake kusaidia wengine wakati wa majanga na hali za Dharura. Dk Reem binti Ebrahim Al hashimy, waziri wa nchi na ushirikiano wa kimataifa, alisema kuwa UAE "inaendelea kufanya kazi kwa karibu na shirika la afya Duniani ili kuhakikisha kupelekwa kwa shughuli muhimu za usafirishaji kutuma msaada wa haraka wa matibabu na chakula kusaisaidia kukabiliana na mzozo wa sasa nchini sudani. Sambamba na kutoa wito kwa pande zote za mzozo wa sasa kusitisha mara moja uhasama na kupunguza hali inayosababisha mateso yasiyoelezeka kwa watu wa Sudani, UAE iko imara katika ahadi yake ya kutoa misaada ya usaidizi kwa nchi wakati wa mahitaji.
translations.hypothesis: UAE na WHO hupeleka vifaa muhimu vya matibabu nchini Sudan. United Arab Emirates (UAE) na Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) leo wamepeleka mizigo ya vifaa muhimu vya matibabu nchini Sudan. ndege iliyo na vifaa vya matibabu, tiba ya majeraha, na dawa muhimu ilifika kwenye Uwanja wa ndege wa Port Sudan mapema leo. Mizigo, zenye thamani ya dola milioni 444, ni za kwanza ambazo WHO zameuwezesha kupeleka nchini Sudan tangu kuanza kwa mgogoro. WHO walitolea vifaa kwenye hospitali kabla ya mgogoro kuongezeka. Vifaa hivyo vilitumika baada ya siku chache, kutokana na idadi kubwa ya majeraha. Ujumaji wa ndege ni sehemu ya juhudi za UAE za kuwasaidia raia wa Sudan, na inaonyesha uhusiano wa kina kati ya UAE na Sudan, haswa katika hali iliyo. Pia, misaada hii ni sehemu ya maono wa UAE wa kiutu, na inaonyesha uhusiano wake na nchi zingine, kulingana na umoja wa binadamu na kujitolea kusaidia wengine katika majeraha na hali hatari. Dkt Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Mwinjili wa Serikali kuhusu Ushirikiano wa Kimataifa, alisema kwamba "UAE inaendelea kufanya kazi kwa karibu na Shirika la Afya Duniani ili kuhakikisha utekelezaji wa mafanikio wa shughuli muhimu za usafirishaji, ambazo zitasaidia kushughulikia mgogoro uliopo nchini Sudan. Pamoja na wito wa pando zote za mgogoro kuacha mapigano mara moja na kupunguza hali inayosababisha maumivu makubwa kwa raia wa Sudan, UAE inaendelea na kujitolea kutoa misaada na msaada kwa nchi katika wakati wa uhavaji.

translations.source: The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need. WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed.
translations.reference: Umoja wa falme za kiarabu unalenga hasa kutoa mahitaji ya makundi yaliyo hatarini zaidi yaliyoathiriwa na hali nchini Sudani, hususani wagonjwa, watoto, wazee na wanawake ambao wapo katika hatari zaidi kutokana na mzozo unaoendelea - na safari hizi za ndege moja kwa moja zitatua mapengo makubwa zaidi katika utoaji wa matibabu na chakula. Maadili ya kibinadmu yaliyoshikiliwa kwa kina ya UAE yamemaanisha kwamba imehakikisha kuwa imeendelea kuwasiliana na ulimwengu kujitolea kwake kwa nguvu na kwa kudumu katika kuimarisha amani, usalama na utulivu, sio tu kikanda, lakini pia Duniani kote, wakati wa pamoja na washirika wake na jumuia ya kimataifa, nchi imendelea kusaidia watu wa Sudani wakati wa machafuko" vifaa ni pamoja na kiwewe cha kutosha, vifaa vya upasuaji wa Dharura, na dawa muhimu kufikia mara moja watu 165,000 ambao wanahitaji sana msaada wa kibinadamu. Vituo vya afya kote nchini vinaripoti kuwa vimekosa vifaa vya msingi vya afya na kwamba Bohari ya Taifa ya Dawa haifikii tena kutokana na hali ya usalama. Miongoni mwa msaada mwingine, WHO inapeleka wataalamu wawili wa vifaa vya Dharura wanaosafiri na mizigo ili kuhakikisha kwamba vifaa hivi vinasambazwa mara moja kwa vituo vikuu 13 vya afya ili kusaidia wahudumu wa afya na kupanua huduma kwa wale wanaohitaji. WHO ina tani nyingine 30 za vifaa kwa ajili ya Malaria na magonjwa yasiyoambukiza kama vile Kisukari na Shikinizo la damu, hali ambayo inaweza kuwa mbaya kama haitibiwi. Mifumo hii na takribani 23,000 ya mifuko ya damu inasomwa ndani ya kituo cha vifaa cha kimataifa cha WHO katika jiji la kimataifa la kibinadamu, na WHO kwa sasa inachunguza uwezekano wote wa kuwasilisha vifaa hivi nchini Sudani haraka iwezekanavyo kwa ushirikiano na wizara ya afya ya Sudani. Dk Michael Ryan, mkurugenzi mtendaji, wa mpango wa Dharura ya afya wa WHO, alisema, "kituo cha usafirishaji cha WHO huko UAE kimekuwa kitovu cha muitiko wa haraka wa matukio ya Dharura ulimwenguni. Husaidia zaidi ya nchi 140 katika maeneo yote 6 ya kijografia ya WHO, kitovu hutoa njia ya kuokoa maisha kwa nchi wanachama zinazokabiliwa na Dharura za kiafya zinazotokana na milipuko ya magonjwa, majanga kama vile mafuriko na matetemeko ya ardhi, na athari za migogoro kwenye mifumo ya afya. Msaada unaotolewa na UAE huwezesha WHO kuwa mstari wa mbele katika majibu haya ya Dharura ya afya- kutoa msaada wa matibabu wa kibingwa kwa wale wanaohitaji popote na wakati wowote inapohitajika.
translations.hypothesis: VAE ina umakini maalum katika kuwapa huduma kundi lililohitaji zaidi, ambacho limeathirika na hali katika Sudan, haswa wagonjwa, watoto, watu wazee, na wanawake ambao wana hatari kubwa kutokana na mgogoro unaoendelea – na safari hizi za msaada zitatoa suluhisho moja kwa moja kwa mapungufu muhimu katika utoaji wa huduma za matibabu na chakula. Miongozo ya kimbinadamu ya VAE, ambazo ni muhimu, ime maana kwamba imehakikisha kuwa inaendelea kuwasiliana na ulimwengu kuhusu kujitolea lake kali na usio na kifani katika kuimarisha amani, usalama na utulivu, sio tu katika eneo, bali pia ulimwenguni, huku ikiwa pamoja na washirika na jamii ya kimataifa, nchi inaendelea kusaidia raia wa Sudan katika wakati wa dharura. Rasilimali za afya ni pamoja na vifaa vya kutosha za matibabu dharura, vifaa muhimu vya upasuaji, na dawa muhimu ili kufikia watu 165,000 ambao wana haja kubwa ya msaada wa kimbinadamu. Kituo cha afya katika nchi nzima kinaripoti kuwa wamepoteza vifaa vya msingi za afya, na maduka ya kitaifa ya matibabu si nafuu kutokana na hali ya usalama. Miongoni mwa msaada, WHO ina watendaji wawili wa usafirishaji dharura wanaosafiri na mizigo ili kuhakikisha kuwa vifaa hivi vinapewa mara moja katika vituo muhimu 13 za afya ili kuunga mkono wafanyakazi wa afya na kutoa huduma kwa wale wanaohitaji. WHO ina mizigo mengine ya tani 30 kwa magonjwa ya malaria na magonjwa isiyoweza kuambukizwa kama vile ugonjwa wa sukari na shinikizo, ambayo yanaweza kuwa hatari ikiwa hayatumika. Mizigo hizi na vyombo takribani 23,000 vya damu zinaandaa katika kituo cha kimataifa cha usafirishaji cha WHO, na WHO kwa sasa inaangalia uwezekano zote za kupeleka mizigo hizi kwenda Sudan kwa haraka iwezekanavyo, kwa ushirikiano na Wizara ya Afya ya Sudan. Daktari Michael Ryan, Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Programu ya Matibabu ya dharura ya WHO, alisema, "Kituo cha usafirishaji cha kimataifa cha WHO katika VAE kimekuwa kitovu muhimu katika majibu ya haraka ya mizigo katika matukio ya ulimwenguni. Kusaidia zaidi ya nchi 140 katika eneo la 6 la WHO, kituo hicho kinatoa msaada muhimu kwa mataifa ambayo yana matibabu ya dharura kutokana na magonjwa, majanga kama vile mafuriko na ardhi, na athari za mgogoro kwenye mifumo ya afya. Msaada kutoka VAE unawezesha WHO kuwa katika nafasi ya kwanza katika majibu haya ya matibabu, kwa kutoa msaada wa matibabu maalumu kwa wale wanaohitaji, popote na wakati wowote.

translations.source: As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways.
