Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies. It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.reference: Kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuimarisha usanifu wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya maandalizi ya Dharura ya kiafya, muitikio, na ustahimilivu UAE, WHO, na washirika wanafanya kazi bega kwa bega kupeana vifaa ili kuwafikia waliohatarini zaidi Duniani katika wakati wao wa uhitaji mkubwa" Kwa upande wake, Dk Ahmed Al- Mandhari, mkurugenzi wa WHO kanda ya mashariki ya Mediterania, alisema, "shirika la afya Duniani linaendelea kuratibu na mamlaka ya afya ya Sudani kuwasilisha vifaa muhimu vya afya kupitia njia zote zinazopatikana. Kwa sababu ya mzozo, hospitali, maduka ya dawa na vituo vya huduma ya afya ya msingi vimekosa dawa muhimu, Dawa za kuokoa maisha. Kuwasili kwa tani 30 za ziada za vifaa vya kiwewe na upasuaji wa Dharura vilevile dawa muhimu kutawezesha WHO kusaidia vituo vikuu 13 vya afya na kuanzisha upya huduma za afya kwa wale wanaohitajika. Kituo chetu cha usafirishaji cha kimataifa huko Dubai kina jukumu muhimu katika kuhakikisha usambazaji usiokatizwa wa bidhaa za afya na tunashukuru kwa usaidizi unaotolewa na umoja wa falme za kiarabu kuwezesha safari yetu ya kwanza ya kukodi ya vifaa hivi vinavyohitajika sana. Ni msaada kama huu ambao unaonyesha kwa vitendo dira yetu ya kikanda ya afya kwa wote, na wote" Misaada ya matibabu iliyotolewa na shirika la afya Duniani kupita UAE ilichangia takribani 55% ya jumla ya vifaa vya matibabu vilivyotolewa kwa Sudani kutoka nje ya nchi mwaka 2022, ikionyesha nafasi ya UAE kama kitovu cha usaidizi wa kimataifa wa kibinadamu.
translations.hypothesis: Kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuimarisha mfumo wa kimataifa wa maandalizi, majibu, na uendelevu wa afya, UAE, WHO, na washiriki wanaufanya kazi pamoja ili kuleta vifaa kwa wale ambao ni hatarini zaidi duniani wakati wa mahitaji yao. Kwa upande wake, Dkt. Ahmed Al-Mandhari, Mkurugenzi wa WHO kwa Mediterania Mashariki, alisema, "Shirika la Afya la Dunia linaendelea kusimamia na vyombo vya afya vya Sudan ili kuleta vifaa muhimu za afya kupitia njia zote zinazopatikana. Baada ya mgogoro, hospitali, maduka za dawa, na kituo cha huduma za afya zina vifaa muhimu vya dawa. Tafadhali, vifaa vya ziada vya 30 toni za tiba na upasuaji, pamoja na dawa muhimu, zitasaidia WHO kusaidia vituo 13 muhimu za afya na kuweka upya huduma za afya kwa wale ambao wanahitaji. Kituo chetu cha kimataifa cha logistika huko Dubai kinacheza jukumu muhimu katika kuhakikisha upokeaji wa mara kwa mara wa bidhaa za afya, na tuna shukrani kwa usaidizi ambao umepewa na UAE ili kuwezesha safari yetu ya kwanza ya vifaa hivi muhimu. Msaada kama huu unaonyesha utekelezaji wa maono yetu wa kimataifa wa afya kwa wote, na kwa wote.

translations.source: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others. At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.
translations.reference: Kushirikiana ndani ya nchi ni muhimu kwa maendeleo ya kimataifa kuelekea SDGs zinazohusiana na afya Ripoti mpya ya maendeleo inatolewa kwa pamoja leo na mashirika 13 yaliyotia saini mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa wa maisha ya afya na ustawi kwa wote (SDG3 GAP). Ripoti yenye anuani "Ni nini kilifanya kazi? Nini haikufanya? Nini kinafuata?" inaakisi miaka minne ya kazi ya pamoja ya mashirika makubwa ya kimataifa inayoelezea kile ambacho hakijafanya kazi katika kuimarisha ushirikiano na usaidizi ili kuharakisha maendeleo ya nchi kuelekea malengo ya maendeleo endelevu (SDGs) yanayohusiana na afya. Mwaka huu ni ishara ya nusu ya kuelekea SDGs, lakini Dunia ipo nyuma kufikia malengo ya kimataifa. Tangu kuzinduliwa kwake mwaka 2019, SDG3 GAP iliwezesha kuundwa kwa miundo mipya ya ushirikiano kati ya mashirika yaliyotia saini katika maeneo muhimu kama vile ufadhili endelevu na huduma ya afya ya msingi miongoni mwa mengine. Angalau nchi 67 zimejihusisha kwenye moja au zaidi ya maudhui ya kichochezi ya SDG3 GAP. "Tumipiga hatua muhimu, lakini bado tuna njia ndefu ya kusafiri ili kuboresha jinsi mashirika ya kimataifa yanayofanya kazi pamoja kusaidia nchi. Lazima tusikilize kile ambacho nchi zinatuambia na kuchukua hatua kulingana na muongozo wao", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO na mwenyekiti wa kundi la wakuu wa SDG3 GAP. "Nawashukuru washirika kwa ushirikiano wao na kwa kujitathmini kwa uaminifu uliomo katika ripoti hii. "Ripoti inatoa mapendekezo sita muhimu ili kuhakikisha kuwa nchi zinanufaika kutokana na usaidizi ulioboreshwa zaidi na kutimiza ahadi zilizotolewa katika SDG3 GAP.
translations.hypothesis: Ushirikiano katika eneo ni muhimu kwa maendeleo kimondo katika masuala yanayohusiana na afya. Ripoti mpya inachapishwa pamoja leo na vyombo 13 vya ushirikiano katika "Mpango wa Kimondo wa Afya na Afya Bora kwa Wote" (SDG3 GAP). Ripoti, inayoelewa "Nini lilifanya? Nini halifanya? Nini kitakuwa?" inaonyesha miaka minne ya ushirikiano kati ya vyombo kubwa vya kimondo, na inaeleza kile ambacho kimefanikiwa na kile ambacho halikufanikiwa katika kuimarisha ushirikiano na usaidizi ili kuendesha maendeleo ya nchi katika malengo ya Kimondo yanayohusiana na afya (SDGs). Mwaka huu ni hatua ya katikati kuelekea SDGs, lakini dunia bado inakosa kufikia malengo ya kimondo. Tangu kuzinduliwa mnamo 2019, SDG3 GAP ilitoa fursa ya kuunda miundo mpya ya ushirikiano kati ya vyombo vya ushirikiano katika masuala muhimu kama vile ufadhili endelevu na huduma ya afya ya msingi, miongoni mwa mengine. Angalau nchi 67 zimehusika katika moja au zaidi ya mada za SDG3 GAP. "Tumeendesha maendeleo muhimu, lakini bado tuna njia ndefu ya kusafiri ili kuboresha jinsi vyombo vya kimondo vinafanya kazi pamoja ili kusaidia nchi. Lazima kusikilize kile ambacho nchi zinaeleza na kutenda kwa mwongozo lao," alisema Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Katibu Mkuu wa WHO na mwenyeki wa Kundi la Wajumbe la SDG3 GAP. "Nashukuru washiriki kwa ushirikiano wao na tathmini za kweli zinazomoja katika ripoti hii. "Ripoti ina mapendekezo sita muhimu ili kuhakikisha kwamba nchi zinafaidika na usaidizi bora na kufanikisha ahadi zilizotolewa katika SDG3 GAP.

translations.source: These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach; engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor:
translations.reference: Mapendekezo hayo ni pamoja na Kuendeleza mzunguko wa uboreshaji wa SDG3 GAP kwa afya kwa kutafuta maoni ya nchi wanachama kuhusu jinsi tunavyoshirikiana katika ngazi ya nchi na kujibu mapendekezo yanayohusiana; Kudumisha GAP kama jukwaa la ushirikiano; Kukuza ushirikiano wenye nguvu katika ngazi ya chini kuhusu huduma ya afya ya msingi na kuchunguza mada mpya kama vile mifumo ya afya inayostahimili hali ya hewa. Kutumia kwa pamoja mbinu mpya katika ngazi ya nchi kama vile uwasilishaji kwa mbinu ya athari; Kujihusisha zaidi na mashirika ya kiraia; na Kufanya kazi na nchi wanachama ili kuimarisha vitisho vya ushirikiano kupitia uongozi wa kisiasa, mwelekeo wa utawala na ufadhili ili kusaidia ushirikiano. Matokeo muhimu ya ripoti hiyo yatajikita katika majadiliano ya mkutano ujao wa baraza kuu la umoja wa mataifa la SDG na mkutano wa ngazi ya juu kuhusu huduma ya afya kwa wote mnamo Septemba 2023. Miezi kabla ya matukio haya muhimu inatoa fursa kwa msukumo wa pamoja na nchi wanachama na mashirika ya kimataifa kutambua njia za jinsi ya kutekeleza mapendekezo na kuandaa msingi wa kuimarisha ushirikiano wenye nguvu zaidi na kuharakisha maendeleo kuelekea SDGs zinazohusia na afya katika nusu ya pili ya muda wa SDG. Maelezo kwa mhariri:
translations.hypothesis: Hizi ni pamoja na: kuendeleza mchakato wa uboreshaji wa SDG3 kwa afya kwa kupata maoni kutoka kwa mataifa kuhusu jinsi tunavyoshirikiana katika viwango vya taifa, na kujibu mapendekezo husika; kudumisha GAP kama jukwaa la ushirikiano; kuendeleza ushirikiano imara katika viwango vya taifa katika huduma ya afya ya msingi, na kuchunguza mada mpya kama mifumo ya afya inayostahimili mabadiliko ya hali; kutumia njia mpya pamoja katika viwango vya taifa kama mfumo wa "kuleta matokeo"; kuwasiliana zaidi na jamii; na kufanya kazi na mataifa ili kuimarisha motisha za ushirikiano kupitia uongozi, uongozi, na fedha za kusaidia ushirikiano. Matokeo muhimu ya ripoti itatumika katika majadiliano ya Bunge la Muungano wa Mataifa kuhusu SDG, na Mkutano wa Kulingana wa Mfumo wa Afya wa Kiawakili mnamo Septemba 2023. Aina za kipindi kabla ya hafla hizi zinatoa fursa kwa mataifa na mashirika ya kimataifa ili kutambua njia za kutekeleza mapendekezo na kupanga mazingira ili kutumia ushirikiano imara na kuendeleza maendeleo katika malengo ya afya katika nusu ya pili ya muda wa SDG. Tafadhali kwa mhariri:

translations.source: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO); Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF); Unitaid; United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women);
translations.reference: SDG3 GAP inaeleza jinsi mashirika 13 yaliyotia saini yatatumia njia mpya za kufanya kazi, kuendeleza ushirikiano uliopo kwenye mafanikio, na kuoanisha kwa pamoja msaada wao katika mipango na mikakati ya kitaifa ambayo inamilikiwa na kuongozwa na nchi. Waliotia saini SDG3 GAP ni Gavi, Muungano wa Chanjo; Ufadhili wa kituo cha kimataifa kwa wanawake, watoto na vijana (GFF); Mfuko wa kimataifa wa kupambana na UKIMWI, kifua kikuu na Malaria (mfuko wa kimataifa); Shirika la kazi la kimataifa (ILO); Mpango wa pamoja wa umoja wa mataifa kuhusu VVU/UKIMWI (UNAIDS); Mpango wa maendeleo wa umoja wa mataifa (UNDP); Shirika la umoja wa mataifa la idadi ya watu (UNFPA); Shirika la umoja wa mataifa la kuhudumia watoto (UNICEF); Unitaid; Shirika la umoja wa mataifa la usawa wa jinsia na uwezeshaji wa wanawake (UN Women);
translations.hypothesis: SDG3 GAP inafafanua jinsi mashirika 13 za washiriki watachukua njia mpya za kufanya kazi, kwa kujenga kwenye ushirikiano ambao tayari umejipatia, na kuleta pamoja msaada wao ili kukidhi mipango na sera za kitaifa ambazo ni za kitaifa na zinaongozwa na nchi. Washiriki wa SDG3 GAP ni Gavi, Shirika la Chanjo; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund kwa Vita vya Ugonjwa wa AIDS, Tuberculo na Malaria (Global Fund); Shirika la Kijamii la Kimataifa (ILO); Mpango wa Jumuiya ya Mataifa kuhusu Ugonjwa wa AIDS (UNAIDS); Mpango wa Maendeleo wa Jumuiya ya Mataifa (UNDP); Mfuko wa Jumuiya ya Mataifa kwa Watoto (UNICEF); Unitaid; Kituo cha Jumuiya ya Mataifa kwa Usawa wa Jinsia na Uendelezaji wa Wanawake (UN Women);

translations.source: World Bank Group; World Food Programme (WFP); and World Health Organization (WHO). See full report, What Worked? What’s next?
translations.reference: Kundi la Benki ya Dunia Mpango wa chakula Duniani (WFP); na Shirika la afya Duniani (WHO). Tazama ripoti kamili, Nini kilifanya kazi? Nini kinafuata?
translations.hypothesis: Kundi la Benki ya Dunia; Programu ya Chakula ya Dunia (WFP); na Shirika la Afya cha Dunia (WHO). Angalia ripoti kamili, "Nini lilifanya kazi? Nini kitakuwa baadaye?"

translations.source: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023. Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning.
translations.reference: Matokeo mapya ya uchunguzi yanaonyesha mifano ya afya ikianza kurejeshwa kutokana na janga Baada ya miaka mitatu ya janga la UVIKO-19, mifumo ya afya katika nchi imeanza kuonyesha dalili kuu za kwanza za kuimarika kwa mfumo wa afya kulingana na ripoti ya muda ya WHO juu ya "Duru ya nne ya uchunguzi wa mapigo ya kimataifa juu ya kuendelea kwa huduma muhimu za afya wakati wa janga la UVIKO-19: Novemba 2022 Januari 2023". Ifikapo mapema mwaka 2023, nchi ziliripoti kukabiliwa na usumbufu mdogo katika utoaji wa huduma za afya za kawaida, lakini zilionyesha haja ya kuwekeza katika kurejeshwa na kustahimili nguvu zaidi kwa siku zijazo. Kati ya nchi 139 zilizojibu duru ya nne ya uchunguzi wa mapigo ya moyo wa WHO, nchi ziliripoti kuendelea kukatika kwa karibu robo moja ya huduma kwa wastani. Katika nchi 84 ambapo uchunguzi wa mwenendo unawezekana, asilimia ya huduma zilizokatizwa ilipungua kwa wstani kutoka 56% mwezi Julai - Septemba 2020 hadi 23% mnamo Novemba 2022 - Januari 2023. Usumbufu unaoendelea unaotokana na mahitaji na upande wa ugavi, ikiwa ni pamoja na viwango vya chini vya watu wanaotafuta huduma za afya katika jamii pamoja na upatikanaji mdogo wa wahudumu wa afya na rasilimali nyingine za afya kama vile zahanati huria au akiba inayopatikana ya dawa na bidhaa. Ni habari njema kwamba mifumo ya afya katika nchi nyingi inaanza kurejesha huduma muhimu za afya kwa mamilioni ya watu ambao hawakuzipata wakati wakati wa janga hili", alisema Dk Rudi Eggers, mkurugenzi wa WHO wa huduma za afya jumuishi. "Lakini tunahitaji kuhakikisha kuwa nchi zote zinaendelea kuziba pengo hili ili kurejesha huduma za afya, na kutumia mafunzo tuliyojifunza ili kujenga mifumo ya afya iliyoandaliwa zaidi na thabiti kwa siku zijazo". Ishara mashuhuri za kwanza za kurejeshwa Katika utafiti huu mpya, nchi chache ziliripoti kwa makusudi kupunguza ufikiaji katika majukwaa yote ya utoaji huduma na kazi muhimu za afya ya umma tangu kuripoti 2020-2021, kuonyesha hatua muhimu ya kurejea viwango vya utoaji wa huduma kabla ya janga na utendaji mpana wa mfumo.
translations.hypothesis: Matokeo mapya ya utafiti zinaonyesha kuwa mifumo ya afya zinaanza kupulizwa baada ya janga la COVID-19. Baada ya miaka mitatu ya janga la COVID-19, mifumo ya afya katika nchi zinaanza kuonyesha ishara muhimu za upulizaji, kulingana na ripoti ya makala ya WHO kuhusu "Raundi ya nne ya utafiti wa kimataifa kuhusu ushirikiano wa huduma muhimu za afya wakati wa janga la COVID-19: Novemba 2022 – Januari 2023". Hadi mwanzoni mwa 2023, nchi zilitangaza kuwa zimepata mgongano wa chini katika utoaji wa huduma za afya za kawaida, lakini zilitaja haja ya kuwekeza katika upulizaji na uimara zaidi kwa ajili ya siku zijazo. Miongoni mwa nchi 139 ambazo zilijibu kwa raundi ya nne ya utafiti wa WHO, nchi zilitangaza kuwa kuna mgongano wa wastani katika takriban nusu ya huduma. Katika nchi 84 ambapo inawezekana kufanya uchambuzi wa mwenendo, asilimia ya huduma zinazogongana ilipungua wastani kutoka 56% mnamo Julai-Septemba 2020 hadi 23% mnamo Novemba 2022 – Januari 2023. Mgongano unaoendelea unaunga mkono sababu za utoaji na utoaji, pamoja na viwango vya chini vya utafuti wa huduma za afya katika jamii, pamoja na idadi mdogo ya wafanyakazi wa afya na rasilimali zingine za afya kama vile kliniki za wazi au hisa za dawa na bidhaa. "Ni habari njema kwamba mifumo ya afya katika nchi nyingi zinaanza kurejesha huduma muhimu kwa mamilioni ya watu waliopoteza huduma wakati wa janga," alisema Dkt. Rudi Eggers, Mkurugenzi wa WHO wa Huduma za Afya Zinazounganishwa. "Lakini tunahitaji kuhakikisha kuwa nchi zote zinaendelea kupunguza pengo hili ili kurejesha huduma za afya, na kutumia masomo tuliyosoma ili kujenga mifumo bora na imara za afya kwa siku zijazo". Ishara muhimu za upulizaji katika utafiti huu, idadi ndogo ya nchi zilitangaza kuwa zimepunguza ufikiaji katika kila jukwaa la utoaji huduma na kazi muhimu za afya tangu 2020-2021, ambayo inaonyesha hatua muhimu ya kurudi kwenye viwango vya awali vya utoaji huduma na uendeshaji wa mfumo.

translations.source: By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations. Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery.
translations.reference: Kufikia mwishoni mwa 2022, nchi nyingi ziliripoti dalili za ufufuaji wa huduma, ikiwa ni pamoja katika huduma za afya ya ngono, uzazi, kujifungua, watoto wachanga, watoto na vijana; lishe; chanjo; magonjwa ya kuambukiza (ikijumuisha Malaria, VVU, TB na magonjwa mengine ya zinaa); magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyopuuzwa; magonjwa yasiyoambukiza; udhibiti wa matatizo ya akili, Neva na matumizi ya dutu; utunzaji wa wazee; na huduma za kitamaduni na/au za ziada. Idadi ya nchi zinazoripoti kukatizwa kwa mfumo wao wa kitaifa wa ugavi imepungua kutoka karibu nusu (nchi 29 kati ya 59 zinazojibu) hadi takribani robo (18 kati ya nchi 66 zinazojibu) mwaka jana. Licha ya dalili za kupona, kukatizwa kwa huduma kunaendelea kaika nchi zote katika kanda na viwango vyote vya mapato, na katika mipangilio mingi ya utoaji wa huduma na maeneo ya huduma za ufuatiliaji. Pia nchi zinakabiliana na ongezeko la mlundikano wa huduma - mara nyingi zaidi katika huduma za uchunguzi, utambuzi na matibabu ya magonjwa yasiyoambukiza - ambayo inaweza kusababisha matokeo mabaya kwani watu wanacheleweshwa kupata huduma kwa wakati. Kurejesha utoaji wa huduma muhimu za afya ni muhimu kwa sababu usumbufu- ikiwemo huduma za kukuza afya, kuzuia magonjwa, uchunguzi, matibabu, urekebishaji na kupooza - kunaweza kuwa na athari mbaya zaidi za kiafya kwa idadi ya watu na kiwango cha mtu binafsi kuliko janga lenyewe, hususani miongoni mwa watu walio hatarini. Kuunganisha huduma za UVIKO-19 katika huduma muhimu za afya katika hatua nyingine muhimu kuelekea ufufuaji na mabadiliko ya mfumo, nchi nyingi zimepiga hatua katika kuunganisha huduma za UVIkO-19 katika utoaji wa huduma za afya za kawaida. Takribani 80-90% ya nchi zimeunganisha kikamilifu huduma za chanjo ya UVIKO-19, uchunguzi na udhibiti wa kesi vilevile huduma za hali baada ya UVIKO-19 katika utoaji wa huduma za kawaida. Bado, nchi nyingi (80% yanchi 83 zinazojibu) ziliripoti angalau kizuizi kimoja cha kuongeza ufikiaji wa zana muhimu za UVIKO-19 (m.f uchunguzi wa UVIKO-19, matibabu, chanjo na vifaa vya kinga ya kibinafsi- PPE), na masuala ya wahudumu wa afya na ukosefu wa fedha unaowakilisha vikwazo vya kawaida. Msaada zaidi unahitajika kwa ajili ya kurejeshwa, uthabiti na kujitayarisha Nchi nyingi zimeanza kutumia yale ambayo wamejifunza kipindi cha janga la UVIKO-19, ikijumuisha kupitia kuweka mikakati kadhaa bunifu ya kupunguza usumbufu wa huduma katika utoaji wa huduma za afya.
translations.hypothesis: Kufikia mwisho wa 2022, nchi nyingi ziliiripoti dalili za upulizaji wa huduma, pamoja na katika huduma za afya ya kijinsia, uzazi, mama, mtoto, watoto, na vijana; lishe; chanjo; magonjwaambukizivu (kwa mfano, malaria, HIV, TB, na magonjwa mengine yanayotokana na kijinsia); magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyothamani; magonjwa isiyoweza kuambukizwa; usimamizi wa magonjwa ya akili, neva, na matumizi ya dawa; huduma kwa watu wazee; na huduma za za kisasa na/au za ziada. Idadi ya nchi ambazo ziliiripoti mgongano katika mfumo wao wa utoaji wa bidhaa za kitaifa ilipungua kutoka karibu nusu (29 kati ya nchi 59 ambazo zilijibu) hadi takribani nusu (18 kati ya nchi 66 ambazo zilijibu) katika mwaka wa mwisho. Licha ya dalili za upulizaji, mgongano wa huduma unaendelea katika nchi katika kila eneo na viwango vya kipato, na katika karibu kila eneo la utoaji wa huduma na eneo la huduma. Nchi pia zinajitahidi na zilizikwama katika utoaji wa huduma – hasa katika huduma za uchunguzi, utambuzi, na matibabu ya magonjwa isiyoweza kuambukizwa – ambazo zinaweza kusababisha matokeo hasi, kwani watu wanapopata huduma kwa wakati. Utoaji wa huduma muhimu za afya ni muhimu, kwani mgongano – pamoja na huduma za kukuza afya, kuzuia magonjwa, utambuzi, matibabu, ahuili, na tiba – unaweza kuwa na athari mbaya zaidi kwa afya katika viwango vya jamii na mtu binafsi kuliko janga hilo, hasa katika jamii dhaifu. Uunganisho wa huduma za COVID-19 katika huduma muhimu za afya Hatua muhimu nyingine katika mchakato wa upulizaji na mabadiliko, nchi nyingi zimefanya maendeleo katika kuunganisha huduma za COVID-19 katika utoaji wa huduma za kawaida. Kiasi cha 80-90% ya nchi zimeunganisha kikamilifu huduma za chanjo, utambuzi, na usimamizi wa magonjwa ya COVID-19, pamoja na huduma za baada ya COVID-19, katika utoaji wa huduma za kawaida. Bado, nchi nyingi (80% ya nchi 83 ambazo zilijibu) ziliiripoti angalau tatizo moja katika kuongeza ufikiaji wa vifaa muhimu za COVID-19 (mfano, utambuzi wa COVID-19, tiba, chanjo, na vifaa vya ulinzi wa kibinafsi - PPE), na masuala ya wafanyikazi wa afya na ukosefu wa fedha ni vizuizi vya kawaida. Msaada zaidi unahitajika kwa ahuili, uendelevu, na maandalizi. Nchi nyingi zimeanza kutumia jinsi ambavyo zimejifunza wakati wa janga la COVID-19, pamoja na kupata suluhisho za ubunifu.

translations.source: These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action. While pulse surveys have limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and delivers information rapidly. The term “country” should be understood to include all countries, territories and areas. Trend analyses was completed based on responses from 84 countries, territories, or areas that responded to at least one survey section in all four pulse survey rounds.
translations.reference: Hizi ni pamoja na kupelekea mbinu za matibabu ya simu, uhamasishaji wa huduma za nyumbani au uingiliaji wa kujitegemea, mbinu za kuimarisha upatikanaji wa wahudumu wa afya, uwezo na mbinu za usaidizi, ubunifu katika ununuzi na usambazaji wa dawa na vifaa, mawasiliano zaidi ya kawaida ya jamii,n na ushirikiano na watoa huduma na sekta binafsi. Robo tatu ya nchi ziliripoti mgao wa ziada wa ufadhili kuelekea ufufuaji wa mfumo wa muda mrefu, ustahimilivu na utayari. Nchi zilieleza haja ya msaada wa WHO ili kushughulikia changamoto zilizosalia katika muktadha wa UVIKO-19 na zaidi, mara nyingi zinazohusiana na uimarishaji wa wahudumu wa afya, kujenga uwezo wa ufuatiliaji wa huduma za afya, kubuni huduma za afya ya msingi --mfano inayolengwa ya utunzaji, utawala, sera na mipango na mipango ya fedha na ufadhili. Maelezo kwa mhariri: Katika duru ya nne ya uchunguzi wa kimataifa wa Pulse wa WHO, nchi 222, wilaya na maeneo walialikwa kujibu uchunguzi sanifu wa msingi wa mtandao kati ya Novemba 2022 na Januari 2023. Uchunguzi wa nne ulifuatia tafiti za awali za WHO za 2020 na 2021: Duru ya 1(Mei - Septemba 2020), Duru ya 2(Januari - Machi 2021) na Duru ya 3 (Novemva - Desemba 2021) ambao ulionyesha kiwango ambacho janga hilo lilikuwa likiathiri muelekeo wa huduma za afya na jinsi nchi zinavyochukua hatua. Ilhali tafiti za Pulse zina vikwazo kama vile kuripoti upendeleo na uwakilishi, nguvu ya juhudi hii ni ya kina na inatoa taarifa kwa haraka. Neno "nchi" linapaswa kueleweka kujumuisha nchi, wilaya na maeneo yote. Uchunguzi wa mienendo ulikamilishwa kulingana na majibu kutoka nchi, wilaya au maeneo 84 ambayo yalijibu angalau sehemu moja ya utafiti katika duru zote nne za uchunguzi wa Pulse.
translations.hypothesis: Hii ni pamoja na matumizi ya mbinu za telemedicine, kukuza huduma za nyumbani au mbinu za kujitunza, mbinu za kuimarisha idadi, uwezo na mbinu za usaidizi za wataalamu wa afya, ubunifu katika kupata na kuwasilisha dawa na vifaa, mawasiliano zaidi ya kawaida katika jamii, na ushirikiano na watoa huduma wa sekta binafsi. Katika nusu tatu za nchi, ilikumbiana kuhusu ushirikishwaji wa fedha zaidi kwa uendelezaji wa mfumo, uimara na maandalizi. Nchi zilionyesha haja ya usaidizi wa WHO ili kushughulikia changamoto bado katika muktadha wa COVID-19 na zaidi, ambazo mara nyingi zinahusiana na kuimarisha idadi ya wataalamu wa afya, kujenga uwezo wa utendishaji wa huduma za afya, kuunda mbinu za huduma za afya za msingi, utawala, sera na mipango, na mipango na ushirikishwaji wa fedha. Muhimu kwa waandishi: Katika safu ya nne ya suruhai ya kimataifa ya WHO, nchi 222, eneo na maeneo walitajwa kujibu suruhai iliyoundwa kwa mtandao kati ya Novemba 2022 na Januari 2023. Suruhai ya nne ilifuata suruhai za awali za WHO za 2020 na 2021: Safu ya 1 (Mei-Septemba 2020), Safu ya 2 (Januari-Mach 2021), na Safu ya 3 (Novemba-Desemba 2021) ambazo zilionyesha kiwango ambacho janga lilikuwa linathibitisha, na jinsi nchi zinachukua hatua. Ingawa suruhai za "pulse" zina upande, kama vile upendeleo katika ripoti na uwakilishi, nguvu ya juhudi hii ni kwamba ni kamili na inatoa habari haraka. Neno "nchi" linapaswa kueleweka kama linafaa kwa nchi zote, eneo na maeneo. Uchambuzi wa mwenendo ulifanyika kwa msingi wa majibu kutoka nchi 84, eneo au maeneo ambazo zilijibu sehemu moja au zaidi katika safu zote za suruhai za "pulse".

translations.source: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights. We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health.
translations.reference: Taarifa ya kusherehekea maadhimisho ya miaka 50 ya HRP: Swideni na Uingereza na Ireland kaskazini Ili kukamata ari ya wajumbe wa Kamati ya Sera na Uratibu (PCC) wanaosherehekea miaka 50 ya HRP na mafanikio yake mengi, Sweden na Uingereza na Ireland Kaskazini walisoma maelezo yafuatayo ya shukurani na pongezi katika mkutano wa 36 wa HRP PCC. Kizazi chetu kimeshuhudia maendeleo zaidi katika sayansi na teknolojia kuliko vizazi vyote vilivyotangulia kwa pamoja na kushuhudia mabadiliko kadhaa ya kiakili, kiteknolojia, kiafya na kijamii yakiongozwa na mapinduzi ya uzazi wa mpango na kufuatiwa na maendeleo ya afya ya uzazi,ubunifu wa afya vilevile haki na usawa wa kijinsia. Hata hivyo, changamoto kubwa katika uga wa SRHR zinaendelea, na, bilashaka, baadhi ya maendeleo leo yanahojiwa tena. Tunawapongeza wafanyakazi wa HRP, washirika na waungaji mkono kwa mafanikio mengi hadi sasa na kutambua hatua kubwa zilizofikiwa katika nusu karne katika kutoa ushahidi unaounga mkono upatikanaji kamili wa usawa wa afya na haki za ngono na uzazi. Tungependa kutaja makundi makuu matatu ya mafanikio: Kwanza, utafiti wa ubora wa juu wa HRP umekuwa na athari inayoonekana kwenye sera ya afya ya umma, viwango, na mazoezi Dunianikote kushughulikia mahitaji yanayoendelea ya watu mbalimbali. Pili,HRP, hasa kupitia muungano wa HRP, imeimarisha taasisi za utafii ndani na nje ya nchi, na imewezesha watafiti wengi kupata ujuzi wa utafiti ambao unahakikisha kwamba utafiti wa ubora wa juu wa SRHR unaweza kuendelea, na kwa hakika kuongezeka, katika siku zijazo. Tatu, katika Dunia iliyokumbwa na matatizo, migogoro na kuongezeka upinzani dhidi ya usawa wa kijinsia, HRP imewezesha uzingatiaji wa haki za kimataifa za wanawake na wasichana na watu walio katika mazingira magumu. HRP inasalia kuwa chombo kikuu ndani ya mfumo wa umoja wa mataifa kwa ajili utafiti katika uzazi wa Binadamu na inaendelea kuwa kiongozi wa kipekee katika afya ya ngono na uzazi Duniani.
translations.hypothesis: Taarifa ya sherehe kwa 50th ya HRP: Usweden na Uingereza ya Jumuiya ya Uingereza na Uingereza ya Kaskazini. Ili kukumbusha roho ya wanachama wa Kamati ya Sera na Ushirikishwano (PCC) katika sherehe za 50th ya HRP na mafanikio yake mengi, Usweden na Uingereza ya Jumuiya ya Uingereza na Uingereza ya Kaskazini, wameandika taarifa hii ya shukrani na wito wa sanaa katika mkutano wa 36 wa PCC wa HRP. Kizazi chetu kinaona maendeleo zaidi katika sayansi na teknolojia kuliko vizazi vyote, na tumewataja mabadiliko kadhaa za akili, za kiufundi, za matibabu na kijamii, ambazo zimezinduliwa na mapinduzi ya usamba na zilizofuata na matumizi bora za afya, ubunifu wa afya, na haki na usawa wa kijinsia. Hata hivyo, changamoto kubwa katika eneo la afya ya kijinsia na uzazi zinaendelea, na, kwa kweli, baadhi ya matumizi zina wasiwasi tena. Tunatoa shukrani kwa wafanyakazi, washiriki na wadhamini wa HRP kwa mafanikio mengi hadi sasa, na tunagundua hatua kubwa ambazo zimepatikana katika kipindi cha nusu karne, katika kutoa ushahidi wa uhalisi na usawa katika upatikanaji wa afya na haki za kijinsia na uzazi. Tungependa kuanzisha aina tatu kuu za mafanikio: Kwanza, utafiti wa hali ya juu wa HRP una athari halisi kwenye sera, viwango na utumiaji wa afya ya umma duniani kote, na kushughulikia mahitaji yanayoendelea ya jamii mbalimbali. Pili, HRP, haswa kupitia Jumuiya ya HRP, imefanya kuwa nguvu taasisi za utafiti katika eneo, na imewawezesha watafiti wengi kupata ujuzi wa utafiti ambao unaweza kuhakikisha kwamba utafiti wa hali ya juu wa afya ya kijinsia na uzazi unaendelea, na hata kuongezeka katika siku za usoni. Tatu, katika ulimwengu ulio na majitisho, migogoro na upinzani wa usawa wa kijinsia, HRP imewasaidia wanawake na wasichana na jamii dhaifu kudumisha haki zao. HRP bado ni chombo kuu ndani ya mfumo wa Umoja wa Mataifa kwa utafiti wa uzazi wa binadamu, na inaendelea kuwa na uongozi wa kipekee katika afya ya kijinsia na uzazi duniani.

translations.source: HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.reference: HRP na washirika wake wametuleta karibu na ahadi ya mkutano wa kimataifa wa idadi ya watu na maendeleo wa 1994 na wito wake wa kuweka hali za watu binafsi, hususani wanawake na wasichana, katika mhimili wa maendeleo endelevu. Tunataka kutoa pongezi na shukurani zetu kwa sekretarieti ya HRP, washirika wake, wafadhili wenza na mtandao wa kimataifa wa vituo shirikishi na wataalamu kusaidia kuhakikisha juhudi zake zinachangia kuleta mabadiliko kwa wanawake, wanaume, watoto, vijana na jamii mbalimabali HRP imejitolea kuhudumia. Kwa niaba ya wanachama wa PCC, turuhusu kuthibitisha ushirikiano wetu nanyi ili kuunga mkono mafanikio ya HRP, ambayo mafanikio ya ajenda ya 2030 inategemea.
translations.hypothesis: HRP na washirika zao wamekuza uhusiano wetu na ahadi ya Kongamano la Kimataifa la 1994 kuhusu Ugonjwa na Maendeleo, na wito wa kuwekeza haki za watu, haswa wanawake na wasichana, katika maendeleo endelevu. Tunataka kutoa shukrani na matumaini kwa ofisi ya HRP, washirika, washiriki, na mtandao wa kimataifa wa vituo na wataalamu wanaosaidia kuhakikisha juhudi zao zinaunga mkono maendeleo ya wanawake, wanaume, watoto, vijana, na jamii mbalimbali ambazo HRP inazoeza. Kwa niaba ya wanachama wa PCC, toleni kuwa tunareafisha ushirikiano wetu na wewe ili kuunga mkono mafanikio ya HRP, ambayo ni msingi wa mafanikio ya Agenda ya 2030.

translations.source: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions. The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.reference: WHO inazindua mpango mpya wa kuboresha utayari wa janga Ili kusaidia nchi kujiandaa vyema kwa magonjwa ya mlipuko ya siku sijazo, WHO imezindua mpango mpya leo ambao unatoa muongozo kuhusu upangaji jumuishi wa kukabiliana na vimelea vyovyote vya upumuaji kama vile mafua au virusi vya Korona. Mpango mpya wa kujitayarisha na ustahimilivu kwa mpango wa vitisho vinavyoibuka, au PRET, inajmuisha zana na mbinu za hivi punde zaidi za kujifunza pamoja na kuchukua hatua za pamoja zilizoainishwa wakati wa janga la UVIKO-19 na dharura nyingine za hivi karibuni za afya ya Umma. Kupitia mpango huo, WHO itatumia mbinu ya maambukizi kuziongoza nchi katika upangaji wa janga hilo, ikizingatiwa kwamba nafasi na uwezo mkubwa ni wa wakaida miongoni mwa makundi ya vimelea vya magonjwa. PRET inajibu mwito wa muongozo za kiufundi na usaidizi kwa ajili ya kukuza na kuimarisha utayari jumuishi na muitikio, kama ilivyoainishwa katika maazimio ya baraza la afya ulimwenguni. Janga la UVIKO-19 na dharura nyingine za kiafya zimeonyesha kuwa nchi zinahitaji kuwa tayari kukabiliana na matishio ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza, na mipango mahususi ya kujitayarisha mikononi na uratibu bora na ushirikiano na sekta nyingine kama vile kilimo. "Shughuli za kujitayarisha, kuzuia, na kukabiliana hazipaswi kuwa eneo la sekta ya afya pekee", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Kama vile hali za dharura za kiafya zinavyoathiri katika sekta nyingi, ndivyo pia juhudi zetu za kujitayarisha na kukabiliana nazo zienee katika sekta, taaluma na viini vya magonjwa. Pia ni muhimu kwamba ushirikishwaji wa jamii na usawa ni kitovu cha jamii zetu, hususani kwa wale watu waliotengwa na walio hatarini zaidi". PRET ni mageuzi katika mkabala wa WHO wa kujitayarisha kwa janga kupitia utumiaji wa lenzi ya maambukizi, badala ya kuzingatia magonjwa mahususi.
translations.hypothesis: WHO inaanzisha mpango mpya wa kuboresha tayari dhidi ya magonjwa WHO, ili kusaidia nchi kuwa tayari zaidi kwa magonjwa ya baadaye, imelanzisha mpango mpya leo ambao una kutoa mwongozo kuhusu mipango iliyounganishwa ya kujibu magonjwa yoyote, kama vile ugonjwa wa homa au virusi vya korona. Mpango mpya wa Tayari na Uendelezi dhidi ya Hatari Zinazotokea, au PRET, unaingiza zana na njia za hivi punde za kujifunza pamoja na hatua za pamoja, ambazo zimeanzishwa wakati wa mgogoro wa COVID-19 na majeraha mengine ya afya. Kwenye mpango huu, WHO itatumia mbinu ya usambazaji wa taarifa ili kutoa mwongozo kwa nchi katika kupanga tayari dhidi ya magonjwa, kwani uwezo na uwezo mengi ni sawa katika makundi ya magonjwa. PRET inaagiza hitaji la ushauri na msaada wa kiufundi wa kukuza na kuimarisha tayari na majibu, kama ilivyo katika azimio za Kamati Kuu ya Afya ya Dunia. Mgogoro wa COVID-19 na majeraha mengine ya afya umeonyesha kwamba nchi zinahitaji kuwa tayari kikamilifu kujibu hatari za magonjwa, na kupanga tayari kwa njia iliyofaa, pamoja na ushirikiano bora na sekta zingine, kama vile kilimo. "Shughuli za tayari, kuzuia, na majibu lazima zisite kipeo cha sekta ya afya pekee," alisema Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Katibu Mkuu wa WHO. "Kama vile majeraha yana athari katika sekta nyingi, hivyo pia juhudi zetu za tayari na majibu lazima ziangalie sekta, fani, na magonjwa. Pia ni muhimu kwamba ushirikiano na uwiano wa jamii ni muhimu, hasa kwa jamii ambazo ni dhaifu na hazina ulinzi." PRET ni maendeleo katika mbinu ya WHO katika tayari dhidi ya magonjwa, kupitia matumizi ya mbinu ya usambazaji, badala ya kuzingatia magonjwa maalum.

translations.source: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative. This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action
translations.reference: WHO itaendelea kuandaa na kusambaza muongozo kuhusu magonjwa maalumu inapohitajika. Uzinduzi wa mpango huo unafanyika katika siku ya mwisho ya mikutano na zaidi ya watu 120 wanaowakilisha nchi wanachama, washirika na sekretarieti ya WHO, kufafanua mustakabali ujao wa PRET, ikijumuisha maendeleo ya mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya maandalizi ya janga la vimelea. Sehemu ya kwanza ya mpango wa PRET inaangazia vimelea vya magonjwa ya kupumua, ikiwa ni pamojana mafua, virusi vya Korona, virusi vya kupumua vya syncytial. Kwa kuzingatia janga la UVIKO-19 linaloendelea na tishio linalowezekana la mafua ya ndege, sehemu hii itawezesha nchi kukagua, kupima na kusasisha juhudi zao za kupanga janga la kupumua ili kuhakikisha kuwa zina nafasi na uwezo wa kufanya kazi. Mchakato unaendelea ili kutambua kundi la linalofuata la vimelea vya magonjwa, kama vile Arboviruses, kushughulikiwa chini ya mpango huu. Hii itafuata vipaumbele vilivyoainishwa kupitia mapendekezo kumi ya kuimarisha usanifu wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya maandalizi ya dharura ya afya, majibu, na ustahimilivu (HEPR). Mpango wa PRET unaoleta enzi mpya ya kujitayarisha kwa janga na inawakilisha mageuzi ya shughuli za msingi za WHO ili kusaidia nchi wanachama wote katika kuimarisha utayari wa dharura wa afya, kuzuia, na kukabiliana na nafasi na uwezo. Pia mpango huo unaweza kutumika kutekeleza malengo na masharti ya makubaliano ya janga la ugonjwa ambao kwa sasa unajadiliwa na nchi wanachama wa WHO. Kufuatia mijadala na mazungumzo miongoni mwa washiriki wa mkutano, WHO inawaalika washikadau kwenye wito huu wa kimataifa kuchukua hatua ili kuendeleza utyarishaji wa vimelea vya magonjwa ya kupumua. Wito kwa hatua.
translations.hypothesis: WHO itaendelea kuendeleza na kueneza miongozo kuhusu magonjwa maalum kama inavyohitajika. Mwanzo wa kampeni hii unaendelea siku ya mwisho ya mashauriano na zaidi ya watu 120 wanaowakilishia mataifa, washiriki, na ofisi ya WHO, ili kuweka njia ya maendeleo kwa PRET, pamoja na kuandaa mpango wa kimataifa wa utekelezaji wa maandalizi ya janga la magonjwa ya mfumo wa upumuaji. Moduli ya kwanza ya Kampeni ya PRET inazingatia magonjwa ya mfumo wa upumuaji, pamoja na ugonjwa wa homa, virusi vya korona, na virusi vya respiratory syncytial. Kwa kuzingatia janga la COVID-19 linaloweza, na hatari inayowezekana ya ugonjwa wa homa, moduli hii itasaidia nchi kukagua, kutumia, na kuboresha juhudi zao za kupanga kwa janga la mfumo wa upumuaji, ili kuhakikisha kuwa wana uwezo na uwezo wa kutosha. Inapokuwa, kuna hatua ya kutambua makundi ya magonjwa yanayohitajika, kama vile virusi vya arbovirus, ambayo itatumika katika kampeni hii. Hii itafuata kipaumbele ambapo imebainishwa kupitia ada za kumi za kuimarisha mfumo wa kimataifa wa maandalizi, majibu, na uendelezi wa dharura za afya (HEPR). Kampeni ya PRET inaelekeza enzi mpya kwa maandalizi ya janga, na inaweza kuelekeza mabadiliko katika shughuli kuu za WHO za kusaidia mataifa yote kuimarisha uwezo na uwezo wa maandalizi, kuzuia, na majibu ya dharura za afya. Kampeni hii pia inaweza kutumika kutoa maombi na vipimo vya Mkataba wa Janga, ambapo mataifa ya WHO wanaendelea kuunda. Baada ya maoni na majadiliano kati ya washiriki, WHO inaitwa wadau kuhudhuria wito huu wa kimataifa wa kuchukua hatua ili kuendeleza maandalizi ya janga la magonjwa ya mfumo wa upumuaji.

translations.source: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally. Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority
translations.reference: WHO, nchi wanachama, na washirika wanafanya kazi pamoja kutambua na kutekeleza hatua za kipaumbele ili kuimarisha utayari wa kitaifa, kikanda na kimataifa kwa magonjwa ya milipuko na matishio yanayoibuka ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza. Tunatambua jukumu la jamii na sekta zote, na haja ya kuimarishwa na kuendelea kwa kasi ili kumaliza mzunguko wa hofu na kutekelezwa ambao ni wa kawaida sana katika kujiandaa na kukabiliana na janga. Tunakumbuka mafunzo kutoka kwa majanga na magonjwa ya milipuko ya hapo awali, hususani yale yanayosababishwa na magonjwa ya kupumua. Vimelea hivi vina na vitaendelea kuwa tishio kubwa la kimataifa lenye uwezekano wa kusababisha magonjwa na vifo vingi, kuelemea mifumo ya afya, na kudhoofisha uchumi wa Dunia, na kuzidisha ukosefu wa usawa, ambao upo katika upatikanaji wa zana zote mbili za kuzuia magonjwa ya mlipuko na huduma za afya kwa watu wote. Huu ni wito wa kuchukua hatua ili kuharakisha kujitayarisha kwa magonjwa ya mlipuko na vitisho vinavyoibuka Duniani. Kujiandaa kwa ufanisi kunategemea mipango thabiti na hatua zilizoratibiwa. Ahadi ya pamoja ya hatua zifuatazo itaona maendeleo yaliyofikiwa ifikapo Desemba 2025 kama yatakavyo fafanuliwa katika mfumo wa ufuatiliaji wa (kujiandaa na ustahimilivu kwa vitisho vinavyojitokeza (PRET): Kusasisha mipango ya kujitayarisha ambayo inathibitisha vitendo vya kipaumbele na ambavyo imezingatia mafunzo kutoka kwa matukio ya zamani. Kutambua hatari inayoletwa na vimelea vya magonjwa ya kupumua, kupanga janga la vimelea vya upumuaji kwa kuzingatia mada zilizoainishwa katika Moduli ya PRET #1. Kupanga kipaumbele kwa magonjwa ya vimelea vya upumuaji
translations.hypothesis: WHO, mataifa, na washiriki wanaishi pamoja ili kutambua na kutekeleza hatua muhimu za kuimarisha maandalizi za kitaifa, za eneo, na za kimataifa kwa ajili ya magonjwa ya hatari na hatamoto za magonjwa mapya. Tunagawishi umuhimu wa jamii na vigezo vyote, na uhitaji wa hatua thabiti na endelevu ili kukomesha safu ya hofu na kukosa umakini ambayo ni ya kawaida katika maandalizi na majibu ya magonjwa. Tunaelewa kutoka kwa magonjwa na magonjwa ya awali, hasa yale yaliyosababishwa na viumbe vya njia ya kupumua. Viumbe hivi zinaendelea kuwa hatari kubwa kwa kimataifa, na zina uwezekano wa kusababisha magonjwa na vifo vingi, kuathiri mifumo ya afya, kuathiri uchumi wa kimataifa, na kuongeza tofauti, ambazo ziko katika upatikanaji wa vifaa vya kuzuia magonjwa na huduma za afya kwa watu wote. Hii ni wito wa kuchukua hatua ili kuharakisha maandalizi kwa magonjwa na hatamoto za kimataifa. Maandalizi bora ni pamoja na mipango imara na hatua iliyoendeshwa pamoja. Ahadi za pamoja katika hatua zifuatazo zitasaidia kufikia matokeo hadi Desemba 2025, kama ilivyoelekezwa katika mfumo wa ufuatiliaji wa (Maandalizi na Uendelevu kwa Hatamoto za Kimataifa (PRET)): Andika mipango ya maandalizi ambayo yana uthibitisho wa hatua muhimu na ambayo imechukua kutoka kwa matukio ya awali. Rekebisha hatari iliyo na viumbe vya njia ya kupumua, na kupanga mgongano wa viumbe vya njia ya kupumua kulingana na mada zilizotambuliwa katika Moduli #1 ya PRET: Kupanga mgongano wa viumbe vya njia ya kupumua ni muhimu.

translations.source: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding. At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.reference: Kuongeza muunganisho kati ya washikadau katika upangaji maandalizi ya janga kupitia uratibu na ushirikiano wa kimfumo. Hii inajumuisha kujenga mifumo yenye usawa; kufanya mazoezi ya viungo; Na kushiriki taarifa kuhusu mazoea mazuri, changamoto na fursa. Kujitolea kwa uwekezaji endelevu, ufadhili na ufuatiliaji wa utayari wa janga kwa kuzingatia mapengo yaliyo ainishwa wakati w magonjwa ya mlipuko na milipuko iliyopita. Muitikio wa janga la UVIKO-19 umeonyesha kile kinachoweza kufikiwa kwa kujitolea kisiasa, ushirikishwaji wa jamii na ufadhili. Kiini cha kazi hii ni kuhakikisha usawa kuwa tayari kwa janga lijalo pamoja. Hatua ya jamuii nzima inahitajika ili kufanya maendeleo yaliyoainishwa katika wito huu wa hatua. Utekelezaji unapaswa kuimarisha uimara wa jamii, kudumisha , kuendeleza, na kujenga juu ya mifumo ya kawaida; na kuongeza uwezo mpana zaidi wa kujiandaa na kukabiliana na Dharura. Wito huu wa kuchukua hatua ulitolewa katika maandalizi na ustahimilivu kwa vitisho vinavyoibuka (PRET): mkutano wa kimataifa wa magonjwa ya vimelea vya upumuaji ya baadae uliofanyika tarehe 24-26 Aprili 2023 huko Jeniva, Uswisi.
translations.hypothesis: Kuongeza ushirikiano kati ya washiriki katika mipango ya maandalizi ya janga kupitia ushirikiano na ushirikishwaji wa thabiti. Hii ni pamoja na kujenga mifumo sawa; kufanya mazoezi pamoja; na kushiriki habari kuhusu mbinu nzuri, changamoto, na fursa. Kuwekeza fedha, kutoa rasilimali, na kutumia utayarishaji wa janga kwa umakini, na haswa kwa kutumia fursa zilizotambuliwa katika janga na magonjwa ya zamani. Majibu ya janga la COVID-19 yameonyesha uwezo wa kufanikisha matarajio kwa kujitolea, ushirikiano wa jamii, na ufadhili. Moyo wa kazi hii ni kuhakikisha usawa ili kuwa tayari kwa janga linalofuata pamoja. Ni muhimu kuchukua hatua za jumla za jamii ili kufanikisha maendeleo zilizelezwa katika wito huu. Kwa hiyo, matumizi yanapaswa kuimarisha uwezo wa jamii; kudumisha, kuendeleza, na kutumia mifumo ya kawaida; na kutumia uwezo wa kigeni kwa maandalizi na majibu ya dharura. Wito huu ulitolewa katika Mkutano wa Kimataifa wa Maandalizi na Uendelezi kwa Mauti ya Hatari Zinazotokea (PRET), lililoendelea mnamo 24-26 Aprili 2023 huko Geneva, Uswisi.

translations.source: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year. “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.reference: WHO inapendekeza kuongezeka kwa utekelezaji wa zana zinazopendekezwa za kukabiliana na Malaria Kuadhimisha siku ya Malaria Duniani, shirika la afya Duniani (WHO) linatoa wito wa kuongezwa kwa utekelezaji wa afua mpya na zilizopo ili kuokoa misha kutokana na Malaria. Karibu watoto milioni 1.5 walio katika hatari kubwa ya ugonjwa na kifo kutokana na Malaria nchini Ghana, Kenya na Malawi sasa wamepokea dozi yao ya kwanza ya chanjo ya kwanza ya Malaria, RTS,S/AS 01 (RTS,S), shukrani kwa mpango unaoendelea wa majaribio unaoratibiwa na WHO. Majaribio ya chanjo ya Malaria, ilyozinduliwa mwaka 2019, inaongeza usawa katika ufikiaji wa kuzuia Malaria kwa walio hatarini zaidi na wanaookoa maisha. Kama itatekelezwa kwa mapana, WHO inakadiria kuwa chanjo ya Malaria inaweza kuokoa maisha ya makumi kwa maelfu ya watoto kila mwaka. "Tunazo zana za kukabiliana na ugonjwa wa Malaria, kifurushi cha afua ambacho kinajumuisha udhibiti wa Vekta, Dawa za kuzuia, upimaji na matibabu", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Hawa wameunganishwa na chanjo salama na yenye ufanisi ya Malaria, ambayo, inaweza kuokoa maisha ya makumi ya maelfu ya watoto kila mwaka. Kwa uwekezaji endelevu na juhudi zilizoongezwa kufikia walio hatarini zaidi, kutokomeza Malaria katika nchi nyingi kunawezekana. "Siku ya Malaria Duniani 2023 inaadhimisha chini ya kauli mbiu "wakati wa kutokomeza Malaria: Wekeza, Vumbua, tekeleza". Ndani ya Mada hii, WHO inahimiza utekelezaji bora zaidi wa zana na mikakati iliyopo ya kuzuia, kuchunguza na kutibu Malaria, hasa miongoni mwa watu waliotengwa.
translations.hypothesis: WHO inahimiza uendeshaji zaidi wa zana za kusaidia kupunguza magonjwa ya malaria. Wakati wa Siku ya Dunia ya Malaria, Shirika la Afya Dunia (WHO) inaandika ombi la uendeshaji zaidi wa tiba mpya na za soko ili kuokoa maisha kutoka kwa magonjwa ya malaria. Kimsan, karibu milioni 1.5 wa watoto katika matawi, Kenya na Malawi, sasa wamepokea dozi yao ya kwanza ya chanjo ya malaria, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), shukrani kwa programu ya majaribio inayofanyika na WHO. Programu za majaribio za chanjo ya malaria, ambazo zilianzishwa mwaka 2019, zinaongeza usawa katika kupata chanjo ya malaria, na zinaokoa maisha. Ikiwa itapatikana kwa wingi, WHO inaidha kwamba chanjo za malaria zinaweza kuokoa maisha ya maelfu ya watoto kila mwaka. "Tuna zana ambazo tunaweza kutumia kupunguza magonjwa ya malaria, ambayo ni pamoja na udhibiti wa wadudu, dawa za kuzuia, uchunguzi, na matibabu," alisema Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO. "Hii pamoja na chanjo salama na yenye ufanisi ya malaria, ambayo inaweza kuokoa maisha ya maelfu ya watoto kila mwaka. Kwa uwekezaji endelevu na juhudi za kuongezeka, kuondoa magonjwa ya malaria katika nchi nyingi ni inawezekana." Siku ya Dunia ya Malaria 2023 inaelezwa chini ya jukumu "Ni wakati wa kufikia malaria bila, wekeze, ubuni, na utumie". Katika jukumu hili, WHO inahimiza uendeshaji zaidi wa zana na mikakati za kusaidia, kuzuia, kutambua, na kutibu magonjwa ya malaria, hasa katika jamii zinazostahili.

translations.source: According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations. Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments;
translations.reference: Kulingana na ripoti ya hivi karibuni ya ugonjwa wa Malaria Duniani, iliyochapishwa mnamo Desemba 2022, kulikuwa na visa vipya milioni 247 vya Malaria mwaka 2021. WHO kanda ya Afrika inaendelea kubeba mzigo mzito zaidi wa ugonjwa huo ikichukua wastani wa 95% ya visa vyote vya Malaria (milioni 234) na 96% ya vifo vote (593,000) mnamo 2021. Karibu 80% ya vifo vya Malaria katika kanda ya Afrika vilikuwa miongoni mwa watoto chini ya umri wa miaka mitano. Mikakati na zana mpya Nchi zimepiga hatua katika kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za Malaria kwa watu walio katika hatari zaidi. Licha ya mafanikio fulani, watu wengi walio katika hatari kubwa ya Malaria bado wanakosa huduma zinazoweza kuzuia, kugundua na kutibu ugonjwa huo. Changamoto katika kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za Malaria zimeongezeka, hususani huko Afrika kusini mwa jangwa la Sahara, janga la UVIKO-19, kuunganisha migogoro ya kibinadamu, vikwazo vya fedha, mifumo dhaifu ya ufuatiliaji, na kupungua kwa ufanisi wa zana kuu za kupambana na malaria. Kushughulikia matishio haya na kusaidia nchi katika kujenga programu zinazostahimili ugonjwa wa Malaria, WHO hivi karibuni ilichapisha mikakati na mifumo mipya, ikijumuisha: Mkakati mpya wa kudhibiti ukinzani wa dawa za Malaria barani Afrika; Mpango mpya wa kukomesha kuenea kwa Anopheles stephensi katika mazingira ya mijini;
translations.hypothesis: Kulingana na ripoti ya hivi karibuni kuhusu malaria, iliyotolewa mnamo Disemba 2022, kuna takribani kesi milioni 247 za malaria mwaka wa 2021. Eneo la Afrika la WHO bado lina mzigo mkubwa wa ugonjwa – lina takribani 95% ya kesi zote za malaria (milioni 234) na 96% ya vifo vyote (milioni 593) mwaka wa 2021. Kiasi cha 80% cha vifo vya malaria katika Eneo la Afrika vilitokana na watoto chini ya umri wa miaka mitano. Стратегии na zana mpya Nchi zimefanya maendeleo fulani katika kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za kupambana na malaria kwa idadi kubwa ya watu walio hatarini. Licha ya maendeleo fulani, watu wengi walio hatarini kupata ugonjwa bado hawapati huduma ambazo zinaweza kuzuia, kugundua, na kutibu ugonjwa. Changamoto katika kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za kupambana na malaria zimeongezeka, hasa katika Afrika Kusini, kwa janga la COVID-19, vishindano vya kiutu, ufadhili mdogo, mifumo dhaifu yawachungaji, na kushuka kwa ufanisi wa zana kuu za kupambana na malaria. Ili kushughulikia hatari hizi na kusaidia nchi kujenga programu bora za kupambana na malaria, WHO hivi majuzi ilitolea mbinu na mifumo mpya, pamoja na: mbinu mpya ya kudhibiti uresha wa dawa za kupambana na malaria katika Afrika; kampeni mpya ya kuacha kuenea kwa Anopheles stephensi katika mazingira ya mijini;

translations.source: a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact. Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024.
translations.reference: Mfumo mpya, ulioandaliwa kwa pamoja na WHO na UN-Habitat, ili kuwaongoza viongozi wa majiji katika udhibiti wa Malaria mijini. Zana mpya za kusaisdia nchi kutathmini mifumo yao ya uchunguzi wa Malaria na kutambua maeneo ya uwekezaji Pia WHO imeongeza uwazi, kubadilika na kufikia mapendekezo yake ya Malaria. Miongozo iliyounganishwa na WHO kuhusu Malaria sasa inapatikana kupitia majukwaa mawili ya kidijitaali: MAGICapp na programu ya "Malaria Toolkit". WHO inazihimiza nchi kurekebisha mapendekezo kulingana na mipangilio ya magonjwa ya eneo hilo kwa athari kubwa zaidi. Matarajio kwa afua mpya Kuendelea kwa uwekezaji katika maendeleo ya usambazaji wa chanjo mpya za Malaria na zana za kizazi kijacho itakuwa muhimu katika kufikia malengo ya kimataifa ya 2030 ya Malaria. Chanjo ya pili ya Malaria, chanjo ya R21/matrix-M (R21), kama itaidhinishwa, inaweza kusaidia kuziba pengo kubwa kati ya usambazaji na mahitaji na kupunguza zaidi ugonjwa wa watoto na kifo kutokana na Malaria. Ni kipaumbele kwa WHO kuendeleza na mapitio ya kina na madhubuti ya kitaalamu ya chanjo ya Malaria ya R21 mara Data muhimu za ziada za usalama na ufanisi kutoka kwa majaribio yanayoendelea ya awamu ya 3 ya R21 zitakazopatikana na kutolewa kwa WHO. Utangulizi wa hatua kwa hatua wa chanjo ya Malaria ya RTS,S katika nchi za ziada barani Afrika unatarajiwa kuanza mapema 2024.
translations.hypothesis: mfumo mpya, ulioanzishwa pamoja na WHO na UN-Habitat, ili kuongoza viongozi wa miji katika kudhibiti malaria katika miji. seti mpya ya zana ili kusaidia nchi kutathmini mifumo yao yawachungaji wa malaria na kutambua maeneo ambapo kuna uwezekano wa kuwekeza. WHO pia imepanda uwazi, urahisi na ufikiaji kwa mapendekezo yake kuhusu malaria. Miongozo ya WHO iliyojumuishwa kuhusu malaria sasa inaweza kupatikana kupitia platformi mbili za kidijitali: programu ya MAGICapp na programu "Malaria Toolkit". WHO inashauri nchi kurekebisha mapendekezo kwa hali za magonjwa za eneo ili kufikia matokeo bora. Fursa za matibabu mpya. Uwekezaji endelevu katika uundaji na matumizi ya chanjo mpya za malaria na zana za kizazi kipya itakuwa muhimu katika kufikia malengo ya kimataifa ya malaria mwaka wa 2030. Chanjo ya pili ya malaria, R21/Matrix-M (R21), ikiidhinishwa, inaweza kusaidia kupunguza pengo kubwa kati ya uzalishaji na mahitaji, na pia kupunguza ugonjwa na vifo vya watoto kutokana na malaria. Ni jukumu muhimu kwa WHO kuendelea na tathmini ya kitaalam, thabiti na yenye ufanisi ya chanjo ya R21 ya malaria, mara tu data muhimu zaidi kuhusu usalama na ufanisi kutoka kwa jaribio la R21 la hatua ya 3 zinapatikana na zinatozwa kwa WHO. Inatarajiwa kwamba utangulizi wa chanjo ya RTS,S ya malaria katika nchi zingine zaidi katika Afrika utaanza mapema mwaka wa 2024.

translations.source: In the vector control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. Tools under evaluation include, for example, new types of insecticide-treated nets, targeted baits that attract mosquitoes, spatial repellents, lethal house lures (eaves tubes) and genetic engineering of mosquitoes. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT treatments in the field of antimalarial medicines due to the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. The development of next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," which use a combination of artemisinin and two partner drugs, is underway to reduce the risk of drug resistance. Notes to the editor:For more information on the WHO World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.reference: Katika nafasi ya kudhibiti Vekta, kuna bidhaa 28 mpya katika bomba la R&D. Zana zinazofanyiwa tathmini hujumuisha, kwa mfano, aina mpya za vyandarua vilivyotiwa dawa, chambo lengwa ambacho huvutia mbu, dawa za kuua mbu, viambata vya kuu vya nyumbani (mirija ya michirizi) na uhandisi jeni wa mbu. Watafiti wanatanguliza uendelezaji wa matibabu yasiyo ya ACT katika uwanja wa Dawa za Malaria kutokana na kuibuka na kuenea kwa ukinzani kwa sehemu ya artemisinin. Uundaji wa Dawa za kizazi kijacho, kama vile "ACT tatu", ambazo hutumia mchanganyiko wa Artemisinin na dawa mbili za washirika, unaendelea ili kupunguza hatari ya ukinzani wa Dawa. Maelezo kwa mhariri: Kwa taarifa zaidi kuhusu kampeni ya siku ya Malaria Duniani ya WHO, tembelea: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day-/2023.
translations.hypothesis: Katika eneo la udhibiti wa vector, kuna bidhaa 28 mpya katika hatua ya utafiti na maendeleo. Zana zinazochunguliwa ni pamoja na, kwa mfano, mwavuli mpya wa insecticide, mwavuli maalum ambazo huwavutia nyoka, njia za kupunguza nyoka, mwavuli hatari (pamba), na uboreshaji wa kiwanda wa nyoka. Watafiti wanachagua kuendeleza matibabu ambayo si ACT, katika eneo la dawa za kupambana na malaria, kwa sababu ya kuenea na kuenea kwa upinzani wa sehemu kwa dawa za artemisinin. Maendeleo ya dawa za kizazi kipya, kama vile "triple ACTs," ambazo hutumia mchanganyiko wa artemisinin na dawa mbili, zinaendelea ili kupunguza hatari ya upinzani wa dawa. Muhimu kwa mwandishi: Kwa habari zaidi kuhusu kampeni ya Siku ya Dunia ya Malaria ya WHO, tembelea: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023

translations.source: mRNA Technology Transfer Programme moves to the next phase of its development Inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen, South Africa. Partners gather in Cape Town to discuss the next phase of the mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. Over 200 international participants working with the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme met in Cape Town this week for their first face-to-face meeting. Joined by World Health Organization (WHO) Director General, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and Dr. Joe Phaahla, Minister of Health and Minister of Trade and Industry, Mr. Ebrahim Patel of South Africa together with high-level officials from funding countries, this unique meeting reviewed the progress since WHO and Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) launched it in June 2021. “I am delighted to be here in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for mRNA technology transfer to give low- and middle-income countries equitable access to vaccines and other lifesaving health products,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “I am immensely proud of the achievement of all those involved in this project; in less than two years we have shown that when we work collaboratively, we succeed collectively.” The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners from 15 countries in the Programme, leading experts, industry, civil society representatives, and funders. During the five-day meeting, participants will share progress and discuss critical enablers for the sustainability of the Programme such as intellectual property issues and regulatory aspects, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and key applications relevant to LMICs in other disease areas such as HIV and tuberculosis. The COVID-19 pandemic has underscored that gross inequity exists in access to health products, especially vaccines.
translations.reference: Mpango wa uhawilishaji wa teknolojia ya mRNA unasonga hadi hatua ijayo maendeleo yake Uzinduzi wa kituo cha teknolojia ya mRNA Hub huko Afrige, Afrika kusini. Washirika wanakusanyika Cape Town kujadili awamu inayofuata ya ukuzaji wa chanjo ya mRNA na kubadilishana maarifa. Zaidi ya washirika 200 wa kimaifa wanaofanya kazi na mpango wa uhawilishaji wa mRNA walikutana jijini Cape Town wiki hii kwa mkutano wao wa kwanza wa ana kwa ana. Akijumuika na mkurugenzi mkuu wa shirika la afya Duniani (WHO), Dk.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus na Dk. Joel Phaahla, waziri wa afya na waziri wa biashara na viwanda, Bw. Ebrahim Patel wa Afrika Kusini pamoja na maafisa wa ngazi za juu kutoka nchi zinazofadhili mkutano wa kipekee ulikagua maendeleo tangu WHO na Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) ilipozinduliwa mwezi Juni 2021. Nina furaha kuwa hapa Cape Town na washirika wetu kusaidia mtindo endelevu wa uhamishaji wa Teknolojia ya mRNA ili kuzipa nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati fursa sawa ya kupata chanjo na bidhaaa nyingine za kuokoa maisha", alisema mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO, Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Ninajivunia sana mafanikio ya wale wote waliohusika katika mradi huu; kwa chini ya miaka miwili tumeonyesha kuwa tunapofanya kazi kwa ushirikiano, tunafanikiwa kwa pamoja" Washiriki wa mkutano ni pamoja na washirika wa utengenezaji wa viumbe hai kutoka nchi 15 katika mpango, wataalamu wakuu, viwanda, wawakilishi wa mashirika ya kiraia, na wafadhili. Wakati wa mkutano wa siku tano, washiriki watashiriki maendeleo na kujadili viwezeshaji muhimu vya uendelevu wa mpango kama vile masuala ya mali miliki na vipengele vya udhibiti, vilevile sayansi ya teknolojia ya mRNA na matumizi muhimu yanayohusiana na LMICs katika maeneo mengine ya ugonjwa kama vile VVU na kifua kikuu. Janga la UVIKo-19 limesisitiza kuwa ukosefu wa usawa upo katika upatikanaji wa bidha za afya, hususani chanjo.
translations.hypothesis: Programu ya Uhamishaji wa Teknolojia ya mRNA imetua katika hatua inayofuata ya maendeleo. Uanzishwaji wa kituo cha teknolojia ya mRNA katika Afrigen, Afrika Kusini. Washiriki wanajitokeza katika Cape Town ili kujadili hatua inayofuata ya maendeleo ya chanjo ya mRNA na ushiriki wa maarifa. Zaidi ya 200 washiriki wa kimataifa wanaoshiriki katika Programu ya Uhamishaji wa Teknolojia ya mRNA walikutana katika Cape Town wiki hii kwa ajili ya mkutano wao wa kwanza. Washiriki hao, pamoja na Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO), Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus na Dkt. Joe Phaahla, Waziri wa Afya na Waziri wa Biashara na Viwanda, Bi.
